sources.summary: The paper argues that NGC 1068 shows ongoing negative AGN feedback: its well-studied jet/outflow interacts with the disk, entrains molecular gas, and produces central kiloparsec feedback signatures.

quotes.quote: In this Letter, we present tangible evidence for ongoing negative feedback in the nearby ( 13.97 Mpc; Anand21 ) type-2 Seyfert galaxy NGC 1068 using high-quality ALMA \ (hereafter ) and \ datasets. The outflow and the jet of NGC 1068 are well-studied because of their close distance and apparent brightness e.g., Wilson83,Das06,Barbosa14,Garcia-Burillo14,Garcia-Burillo19 . Based on our finding as well as multi-wavelength studies in the literature, we provide a plausible explanation on what drives and how significant is the negative effect of the AGN feedback in NGC 1068.

Many previous observational studies revealed that there is a tight spatial correlation between the AGN narrow-line region (e.g., D06; Mingozzi19 ), the radio jet e.g., Gallimore96,Capetti97 , and the entrained molecular gas in NGC 1068 e.g., Krips11,Garcia-Burillo14 from a few tens of pc up to 500 pc, all implying that the outflow and the jet in NGC 1068 (1) are driven by the central AGN and (2) interact with the CND and the galactic molecular gas disk (see also Barbosa14 ). Although the exact cause for the hundred-pc-scale ionized gas outflow is still under debate e.g., see May17 , many models agree that the jet from the AGN (and its interaction with the multiphase ISM in the disk) is the origin of all the outflow phenomena in NGC 1068.
Thus, this is clear evidence for the negative AGN feedback in the central 1-kpc of NGC 1068.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 1068 as an obscured AGN with a warped maser disk, radio free-free blobs, compact turbulent gas, and a multi-component dusty torus seen by infrared interferometry.

quotes.quote: Very Long Baseline Array ( VLBA https://science.nrao.edu/facilities/vlba ) water maser Water maser emission is due to a rotational transition in the H _2 O molecule at 22 GHz, and traces high density ( 10^7 < n_H < 10^8 cm ^ 3 ) and warm ( 400 < T < 1000 K) gas. images at sub-parsec resolution revealed a rotating warped disk in NGC 1068 Greenhill1996 . Similar structures were obtained for other AGN Greenhill1997,Kondratko2008 . VLBA images of the continuum radio emission of NGC 1068 further showed two symmetric radio free-free blobs at a radius of 0.3 pc with respect to the non-thermal nuclear emission, which was interpreted as the inner ionized edge of the obscuring torus Gallimore1997a . Further details were provided by ALMA https://www.almaobservatory.org high-resolution submillimeter images, which revealed a compact, parsec-scale, gas component characterized by non-circular motions and enhanced turbulence Garcia-Burillo2016 .

Dust can be also imaged via mid-IR interferometry. This was achieved for the first time in 2004 for NGC 1068 Jaffe2004 , and later refined by . They found that the dusty torus in this source has two components: a compact (0.5 pc thick), elongated hot ( T>800 K) component, coincident with the nuclear water maser; and an extended (3-4 pc), but less elongated colder ( T 300 K) component.

A complex structure for the central hot and warm dust in the torus of NGC 1068 was confirmed by a recent mid-IR spectro-interferometric MATISSE observation at VLTI https://www.eso.org/sci/facilities/paranal/telescopes/vlti.html , in an overall good agreement with the expectations from the original Unification Model GamezRosas2022 .

sources.summary: The review uses NGC1068 as a nearby Seyfert/AGN benchmark for early mid-infrared variability, spectra, ISO PAH mapping, and later ALMA molecular-torus imaging.

quotes.quote: Mid-IR variability on the scale of months was detected in several active galaxies including NGC1068 and 3C273 Rieke1972b . A 8--13 m spectrometer with / 0.02 at the Kitt Peak National Observatory 2m telescope allowed for the first mid-IR spectra of just a handful of bright galaxies including the Seyfert NGC1068 and the starburst galaxy M82 Gillett1975,Kleinmann1976 . These revealed several now familiar features such the 9.7 m silicate absorption, the [ NeII ] 12.8 m line, and a 11.3 m feature of then unknown origin, but later attributed to PAH.

Figure fig:intro a is a 1970s example Kleinmann1976 of the earliest ground-based mid-IR spectra that were available for a handful of bright nearby AGN such as NGC1068. Figure fig:intro b shows that ISO not only greatly expanded mid-IR spectroscopic studies of nearby galaxies and AGN but also provided some spatially resolved results such as this map of the 7.7 m PAH feature At the time, these features were designated UIB or Unidentified Infrared Bands. in NGC1068 LeFloch2001 --a precursor to what we expect to see from JWST MIRI beyond the local Universe.
This view is also supported by recent high resolution ALMA images of molecular gas in the nucleus of NGC1068 Imanishi2018,Garcia-Burillo2019 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068 as a Seyfert starburst neutrino source, noting IceCube’s reported flux and arguing that a hypernebula or AGN-core scenario could explain it.

quotes.quote: Recently, the ten-year point-source searches with IceCube have indicated that the type-II Seyfert starburst galaxy NGC 1068 is the most significant (at 4.2 ) steady source of neutrinos Aartsen+20, IceCubeCollaboration_22 . However, in their respective energy ranges, NGC 1068 and TXS 0506+056 contribute no more than 1 above 10 TeV to the overall HE diffuse background neutrino flux.

Recently, IceCube reported a 1 TeV flux of 1.5 10^ 7 GeV s^ 1 cm^ 2 from NGC 1068 at 14.4 Mpc; IceCubeCollaboration_22 . The peak flux from a single hypernebula accreting at m =10^7 situated at that distance ( 10 chance) would be 2.5 10^ 9 GeV s^ 1 cm^ 2 (see Fig. fig:luminosities ). Therefore, the observed neutrino flux from NGC 1068 could in principle, be explained by a single hypernebula if (1) the JTS is weakly magnetized _ j 10^ 3 , resulting in a larger _ rel 0.1 Sironi&Spitkovsky11 , and (2) in a scenario where a cluster of hypernebulae migrate to the AGN core Secunda+20 . Although, we note that other channels involving AGN coronae could well be operating Murase+20, Inoue+22, Eichmann+22 .

sources.summary: The paper applies its UFO shock-acceleration model to NGC1068, considering an obscured nuclear UFO that could dominate Fermi-LAT gamma rays but contributes little IceCube neutrino flux.

quotes.quote: In what follows we specialize our calculations to the nearby Seyfert galaxy NGC1068 located at a distance D_L = 14 Mpc . Notice that NGC1068 is Compton thick AGN Matt97 . Therefore, a clear detection of an UFO in its nuclear region is extremely challenging, even though Mizumoto2019-NGC1068UFO reported some indications in that sense. Despite one cannot have a compelling evidence of an UFO in NGC1068, in the following we explore the possibility that this galaxy hosts an obscured UFO in its nuclear region.

It is intriguing that NGC1068 is the fourth most relevant object in such catalogs, providing a contribution of about 5-10 . However, our calculations suggests that if the gamma-ray flux detected by Fermi-LAT is associated with the UFO activity in this source, then the maximum energy is bound to be too low to be relevant for UHECRs. Viceversa, if UHECRs are to be produced in the UFO, a different source for the gamma rays has to be found. In this work we investigated the potential of diffusive shock acceleration at the wind termination shocks of ultra-fast outflows (UFOs) in the core of active galaxies. We developed a model of acceleration and transport of particles in the wind bubbles excavated by UFOs and we studied the multimessenger implications in terms of escaping particles and high-energy photons and neutrinos produced through hadronic interactions. We found that protons can be accelerated up to the EeV range and that the far infrared photon field of the torus could play a dominant role in setting the maximum energy in such sources. In addition, the transport condition in the UFO environment could result in a spectral hardening of the escaping flux of the cosmic rays at the highest energies. Such energetic and spectral properties are crucial in light of the recent results obtained by the Pierre Auger Observatory which suggest that the sources of UHECRs could be characterized by hard spectra see e.g. and references therein Auger_combined_fit . UFOs are extremely interesting in terms of multimessenger emission since, in standard conditions, they are expected to shine in gamma rays up to 10 GeV while being opaque beyond 10-10^2 GeV depending on the parametric configuration. While gamma rays are efficiently absorbed, HE neutrinos from pp and p interactions will be copiously produced with a spectrum extending up to 10^2 PeV featuring a spectral slope as hard as the one of their parent protons. Such a property is particularly interesting since UFOs have the potential and possibly the number density in the Universe to simultaneously dominate the CR spectrum at the ankle and the diffuse neutrino flux observed beyond 10^2 TeV. We finally applied our model to the UFO that could be present in the nuclear region of NGC1068 and we found out that, if confirmed, in favourable parametric conditions, a well developed spherical UFO wind bubble could dominate the gamma-ray flux observed by Fermi-LAT while it is unlikely to contribute more than 10 of the total neutrino flux observed by IceCube at TeV.

sources.summary: The review compares NGC 1068 with NGC 4151, noting its Compton-thick Seyfert 2 spectrum, similar black-hole mass, higher likely accretion rate, and weaker-than-expected neutral Fe K line.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:jmm3 shows a comparison of summed HEG spectra from the Seyfert 1 NGC 4151 in its high and low flux states, and the summed HEG spectrum of the Compton-thick Seyfert 2 NGC 1068. The opposing first-order spectra were added, the spectra have been shifted in energy to their respective rest frames, and the flux of NGC 4151 has been adjusted to the distance of NGC 1068. No spectral fits were made. Recent estimates suggest that the mass of the black hole in NGC 1068 is M = 1.5 10^ 7 M_ ; optical reverberation mapping gives a formally equivalent black hole mass in NGC 4151: M = 1.66^ +0.48 _ 0.34 10^ 7 M_ . For these mass estimates, NGC 1068 is likely accreting at an Eddington fraction at or below 0.2 , and NGC 4151 at a rate of = 0.03-0.04 . Given their similar masses, the fact that NGC 1068 is likely surrounded by gas with a higher filling factor and likely accreting at a higher rate than NGC 4151, it should show a stronger neutral Fe K emission line in this comparison.
This can be explained if the gas that is emitting the Fe K line within our line of sight in NGC 4151 is closer to the central engine than the emitting gas within our line of sight is in NGC 1068.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068/M77 as a nearby active galaxy detected by IceCube, modeling its TeV neutrino emission through a possible leptonic muon-pair mechanism.

quotes.quote: As an example, we consider the active galaxy NGC 1068, which IceCube has recently detected as a source of TeV-scale neutrinos. We find that the neutrino emission observed from this source could potentially be generated through muon pair production for reasonable choices of physical parameters.

The IceCube Collaboration has recently reported an excess of 79 events from the direction of the nearby active galaxy, NGC 1068 (also known as M77), corresponding to a 4.2 detection of 1-10 TeV neutrinos (see also, Ref. ). Although NGC 1068 has been detected by the Fermi telescope at 0.1-30 GeV energies , MAGIC has failed to detect gamma rays from source, placing strong limits on its emission in the 0.1-10 TeV band . The lack of TeV-scale gamma rays from this source allows us to rule out the possibility that the observed neutrinos are produced in an optically thin environment, instead favoring scenarios in which cosmic-ray protons are accelerated and produce pions in the dense region immediately surrounding this AGN's supermassive black hole . Observations by NuSTAR and XMM-Newton have detected bright X-ray emission from this source (corresponding to an intrinsic luminosity of L_X 10^ 44 erg/s in the 2-10 keV band, and extending up to energies of _X 10^2 keV ), suggesting that the densities of high-energy radiation in the central region ( i.e. , the corona) could be large enough to efficiently absorb gamma rays through pair production, while still allowing neutrinos to escape.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1068 as IceCube’s strongest active-galaxy neutrino source, spatially consistent with the sky-scan excess and requiring dense-core production near its supermassive black hole.

quotes.quote: After the reanalysis of 10 years of archival data with an improved data selection and enhanced data analysis methods, the active galaxy NGC 1068 emerged as the hottest spot in the neutrino sky map. It is also the most significant source in a search at the positions of 110 preselected high-energy gamma-ray sources.
The first, NGC 1068 (M77), is the most significant source in both searches mentioned above.

Of the 110 astronomical objects tested, NGC 1068 is also the most significant here, with a local P value of 1 × 10^ 7 , or 5.2 ; its flux has best-fitting values of spectral index of 3.2 0.2 and mean number of signal events of 79^ +22 _ 20 . NGC 1068 is contained within the 68 confidence region around the most significant location in the sky scan, offset by 0.11°, consistent with neutrino emission from NGC 1068 (Fig. fig:ngczoom ). After correcting for having tested the 110 sources in the catalog, the global P value for NGC 1068 is 1.1 × 10^ 5 , corresponding to a significance of 4.2 .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1068 as a nearby Seyfert/starburst galaxy whose Fermi spectrum combines starburst hadronic emission above 500 MeV with an additional lower-energy component near the SMBH.

quotes.quote: The recent detection of high-energy neutrinos by IceCube in the direction of the nearby Seyfert/starburst galaxy NGC 1068 implies that radio-quiet active galactic nuclei can accelerate cosmic-ray ions. In this work, we derive a 20 MeV - 1 TeV spectrum of NGC 1068 using 14 years of Fermi LAT observations. We find that the starburst hadronic component is responsible for NGC 1068's emission above 500 MeV.

NGC 1068 is one of the brightest and most studied active galactic nuclei (AGN). It also hosts intense star formation (a starburst). Located at a distance of 10 Mpc curtois2013 , it is classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy because of the absence of broad emission lines in its optical spectrum shields75 .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC1068 as a nearby Seyfert whose gamma-ray and neutrino emission can be explained by jets from stellar-mass black holes embedded in AGN disks.

quotes.quote: We find that emission from internal shocks in the jets can explain gamma rays from nearby radio-quiet Seyfert galaxies including NGC1068, if the Lorentz factor of the jets ( _ j ) is high. On the other hand, for moderate _ j , the emission can significantly contribute to the background gamma-ray and neutrino intensities in the MeV and PeV bands, respectively. Furthermore, for moderate _ j with efficient amplification of the magnetic field and cosmic-ray acceleration, the neutrino emission from NGC1068 and the ultrahigh-energy cosmic rays can be explained. These results suggest that the neutrino flux from NGC1068 as well as the background intensities of MeV gamma rays, neutrinos, and the ultrahigh-energy cosmic rays can be explained by a unified model.

They also identified a nearby Seyfert galaxy, NGC 1068, as a cosmic neutrino source Aartsen2020_Neutrino_NGC1068,IceCube2022_NGC1068 . In addition, they reported a hint of association between neutrino signals and radio-quiet active galactic nuclei (AGN) Abbasi2021_AGN_Neutrino . However, possible mechanisms for CR acceleration and subsequent neutrino production sites in radio quiet AGN remain unclear. This has motivated investigations of non-thermal phenomena in hot accretion flows Kimura2021_llagn,Gutierrez2021 , hot coronae Murase2020,Eichmann22 , accretion shocks Inoue2020 , disk winds InoueS22_Wind , and jets from accreting binaries Sridhar2022 . High-energy gamma rays ( >100 MeV) have also been detected from radio-quiet AGNs Wojaczynski2015 , but the origin of these gamma-rays are also controversial. The majority of these gamma-ray detected AGNs exhibit signatures of starburst activity, which causes gamma-ray production via hadronuclear interactions e.g., Ajello2020starburst . The neutrino-emitting AGN, NGC 1068, is also detected in high-energy gamma rays and shows starburst activity.
We evaluate the possibility that gamma rays and neutrinos from nearby Seyfert galaxies, including NGC1068, are produced in such jets.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068 as IceCube's motivating active galaxy, using its TeV neutrino evidence and gamma-ray opacity to tune a disc-corona neutrino model.

quotes.quote: The IceCube Neutrino Observatory at the South Pole recently reported evidence of TeV neutrino emission from the nearby active galaxy, NGC 1068 . This reinforces the idea that AGN could contribute to the diffuse flux of high-energy neutrinos observed by IceCube since 2013 . Assuming the flux of neutrinos from NGC 1068 to follow a single power-law, the inferred muon neutrino flux at neutrino energy E_ =1 TeV is 5 10^ 14 GeV^ 1 cm^ 2 s^ 1 with spectral index of 3.2 . The corresponding neutrino luminosity in the 1.5 TeV to 15 TeV energy range is much higher than the reported GeV-scale ray luminosity by the Fermi Large Area Telescope (LAT)  Fermi-LAT:2019yla,Ballet:2020hze and the neutrino flux exceeds TeV-scale gamma-ray upper limits reported by MAGIC  .

The absence of TeV gamma-rays from NGC 1068, therefore, suggests that the production site of high-energy neutrinos must be opaque to high energy rays. Such conditions can be found in the matter and radiation-dense region in the closest vicinity of the central SMBH, commonly referred to as AGN corona, a component of the AGN that is known for its brightness in the X-ray band .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1068 as a nearby Seyfert II neutrino source where coronal reconnection near the central black hole can power IceCube TeV neutrinos.

quotes.quote: The recent discovery of astrophysical neutrinos from the Seyfert galaxy NGC 1068 suggests the presence of non-thermal protons within a compact ``coronal'' region close to the central black hole. The acceleration mechanism of these non-thermal protons remains elusive. We show that a large-scale magnetic reconnection layer, of the order of a few gravitational radii, may provide such a mechanism.

Very recently, the IceCube collaboration has reported compelling evidence of a high-energy neutrino flux from NGC 1068 Aartsen_Icecube_2020,IceCube-NGC1068 , a nearby Seyfert II galaxy located at a distance of 10.1 Mpc and Padovani et al., in prep. Tully_2008 . This neutrino flux has been measured with a significance of 4.2 in the energy band 1.5--15 TeV, showing a soft neutrino spectrum d _ /dE_ E_ ^ 3.2 . A comparable TeV gamma-ray flux, which would be expected for an optically thin source, is not observed, with stringent upper limits set by the MAGIC telescope MAGIC-UL-NGC1068 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as a steady neutrino source, combining IceCube and Fermi-LAT observations to constrain ALP production and photon coupling.

quotes.quote: We exploit the Fermi-LAT and IceCube observations of NGC 1068 to set constraints on the ALP parameters. Being a steady source of neutrinos, it offers a better prospect over transient sources. We obtain 95 confidence level (C.L.) upper limits on the photon-ALP coupling constant g_ a 1.37 10^ 11 GeV ^ 1 for ALP masses m_ a 2 10^ 9 eV.

Recently, IceCube Collaboration reported an excess of neutrino events from the direction of NGC 1068 (see also Ref. ), a nearby active type-2 Seyfert galaxy with the jet pointing perpendicular to the line-of-sight. The mean number of signal events was reported to be 79 ^ +22 _ 20 , corresponding to 4.2 level of significance, in the energy range of 1--15 TeV. In the ray band, Fermi Large Area Telescope (LAT) detected emission in the 0.1--300 GeV range but no emission was observed by Major Atmospheric Gamma Imaging Cherenkov Telescopes (MAGIC) and placed strong upper limits in the TeV-scale .

sources.summary: Paper uses NGC 1068 as a FRAMEx comparison, describing elongated VLA radio structure, absent small-scale VLBA jet emission, wind-shocked gas, shock-tracing [Fe 2], and sub-relativistic radio motions.

quotes.quote: For example, NGC 1068 is a FRAMEx target that has an elongated, multi-component radio structure at VLA resolution which has been interpreted as a jet that aligns with an ionizing radiation cone 1988ApJ...328..519P,1997ApJ...476L..67C . But recent VLBA observations by suggest that no small-scale jet is seen, and the observed radio emission is instead formed by AGN winds shocking the host environment. They find that the nuclear radio continuum in NGC 1068 is intricately intertwined with molecular and ionized gases.

As prominent [ Fe 2 ] emission is indicative of shocked emission 2013ApJ...777..156I , the [ Fe 2 ] emission observed in NGC 1068 is inferred as a shock distribution. This distribution is suggested to trace the surface of the molecular gas lanes interacting with the optical NLR, where shock fronts are likely formed from molecular gas lanes rotating into the AGN radiation field. Furthermore, they analyzed two VLBA observations of NGC 1068 with epochs separated by 22 years and found that the measured velocities of the radio structure is traveling at sub-relativistic speeds.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068 as an active-galaxy neutrino point source, using its IceCube events to constrain relic neutrino overdensity along the path to Earth.

quotes.quote: A recent analysis of the high-energy events detected by IceCube has unveiled the origins of some of these astrophysical neutrinos. The most significant among the candidate sources is the active galaxy NGC 1068  , with a global significance of 4.2 . The discovery of these neutrino point sources presents an opportunity for studying scenarios Beyond the Standard Model by exploring deviations in the expected neutrino flux from the source, see e.g. . Crucially, these high-energy neutrinos traverse extensive distances through the C B on their way to Earth.

Overdensities of these relic neutrinos, which lie along the path traversed by the astrophysical neutrinos, can potentially modify the astrophysical neutrino fluxes through the self-interactions of neutrinos, even within the Standard Model (SM). In this Letter, we seek to constrain the overabundance of relic neutrinos by analysing neutrino events from NGC 1068 using publicly available data from IceCube.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as the benchmark non-jetted Seyfert AGN for converting X-ray luminosity functions into a predicted neutrino background.

quotes.quote: IceCube has recently detected an excess of TeV neutrinos with a 4.2 significance from the direction of NGC 1068, the prototype Seyfert II galaxy Abbasi_2022 . This event was somewhat unexpected. Non-jetted AGN We follow and define as jetted AGN with strong relativistic jets, which is not the case for NGC 1068, as discussed in .

Being so close, NGC 1068 can be spatially resolved into a number of components, all possibly relevant to neutrino production Padovani_2024 . These include: 1. a starburst region in the spiral arms of its host galaxy; 2. a kpc jet; 3. a sub-kpc molecular outflow; 4. and the supermassive black hole (SMBH) vicinity. By first using simple order-of-magnitude arguments and then applying specific theoretical models, (and references therein) have come to the conclusion that only the region close to the accretion disc around the SMBH, most likely the X-ray emitting corona, fulfils the conditions to have both the right density of photons needed to provide the targets for protons to sustain neutrino production and to absorb the expected but unobserved rays.

sources.summary: NGC 1068 is discussed as an IceCube Seyfert neutrino source whose hard X-ray and neutrino fluxes are recalculated using NuSTAR and obscuring-column estimates.

quotes.quote: The second neutrino source identified with high confidence was the Seyfert galaxy NGC 1068 ngc1068_2022 . Also, based on this linear scaling, 2020PhRvL.125a1101M listed the brightest neutrino source candidate AGN based on the brightest X-ray objects in the 2--10 keV band, including NGC 1068. AGNs NGC 1068, NGC 3079 z=0.00399 2022ApJS..261....6K and NGC 4151 were indeed suggested to have comparable intrinsic hard X-ray (15--195 keV range) and all-flavor neutrino fluxes Neronov2023 .

In Neronov2023 , the hard X-ray flux of NGC 1068 in the 15--195 keV energy range was calculated by extrapolating its 2--10 keV flux assuming a E^ 2 power-law photon spectrum. They found the observed hard X-ray fluxes as F_ 15-55,obs 2.29^ +0.16 _ 0.30 10^ 11 for NGC 1068 and F_ 15-55,obs 1.70^ +0.08 _ 0.29 10^ 11 for NGC 3079. Near IR--radio studies based on SED modelings find the inclination of the obscuring dust torus of NGC 1068 ranging between 70 and 90 , where 90 means edge-on configuration e.g.

sources.summary: The paper studies methanol in NGC 1068's circumnuclear disk, linking dense, cool CH3OH-traced gas and molecular shocks to AGN feedback and outflowing molecular material.

quotes.quote: The outflowing molecular gas in the circumnuclear disk (CND) of the nearby (D=14 Mpc) AGN-starburst composite galaxy NGC 1068 is considered as a manifestation of ongoing AGN feedback. The large spread of velocities from the outflowing gas is likely driving various kinds of shock chemistry across the CND. We performed a multiline molecular study using CH3OH with the aim of characterizing the gas properties probed by CH3OH in the CND of NGC 1068, and investigating its potential association with molecular shocks.

The gas densities traced by CH3OH point to 10^ 6 cm 3 across all the CND regions. The gas kinetic temperature cannot be well constrained in any of the CND regions though the inferred temperature is likely low ( 100 K). The low gas temperature traced by CH3OH suggests shocks and subsequent fast cooling as the origin of the observed gas-phase CH3OH abundance.

sources.summary: The paper maps the central engine of NGC 1068 in near-infrared lines, linking molecular emission, a suspected torus, rotating molecular material, and radial outflow.

quotes.quote: New near-IR long slit spectroscopic data obtained with ISAAC on VLT/ANTU (ESO/Paranal) complement and extend our previously published near-IR data (Alloin ) to produce and 2 emission line maps and line profile grids of the central 4'' 4'' region surrounding the central engine of 1068. The seeing quality together with the use of an 0.3 '' wide slit and 0.3 '' slit position offsets allow one to perform 2D-spectroscopy at a spatial resolution 0.5 ''. Slit orientations (PA=102 and PA=12 ) were chosen so as to match respectively the equatorial plane and the axis of the suspected molecular/dusty torus in 1068.

An asymmetric distribution of 2 emission around the continuum peak is observed. No 2 emission is detected at the location of the strong 2.2 continuum core (coincident within error-bars with the central engine location), while two conspicuous knots of 2 emission are detected at about 1'' on each side of the central engine along PA=90 , with a projected velocity difference of 140 : this velocity jump has been interpreted in Alloin ( ) as the signature of a rotating disk of molecular material.

A simple way to account for the changes of the 2 line profiles (peak-shift with respect to the systemic velocity, width, asymmetry) over the entire 4'' 4'' region, is to consider that a radial outflow is superimposed over the emission of the rotating molecular disk.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068 as a possible type-2 NLS1 counterpart, estimating its black-hole mass and arguing its inferred accretion rate resembles NLS1 values.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068; a Type 2 NLS1? Here, we briefly discuss the accretion rate of a well-known Seyfert 2 galaxy, NGC 1068. The relatively narrow line and significant FeII multiplets in the polarized emission from NGC 1068 (Miller, Goodrich, Mathews 1991) lead people to suspect it as a type 2 counterpart of NLS1s.

For NGC 1068, the mean [ OIII ] width ( 1150km/s) can not be used for determination, since NLR clouds seem to suffer from the interaction with the jet (Dietrich Wagner 1998). The lowest FWHM ( 200km/s) [ OIII ] clouds seem to be consistent with rotation around the nucleus, and the FWHM width of the line complex from those clouds is about 210km/s (Dietrich Wagner 1998). Consequently, the implied M_ BH ( [ O III ] ) is 4.2 10 ^ 6 .
The filled square in Figure fig:lb_mbh_b indicates the location of NGC 1068, showing that it indeed has a high as NLS1s.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1068's Seyfert 2 torus, using radiative transfer and nuclear maser/mass data to describe obscuration, a rotating maser disk or ring, and a central black hole plus stellar cluster.

quotes.quote: A quantitative treatment for the torus in the prototypical Seyfert 2 nucleus of NGC 1068 together with a radiative transfer calculation for NIR re-emission from the torus is presented. Starting with the interpretation of spectropolarimetry of NGC 1068 by Antonucci Miller , the difference between type 1 and type 2 AGN is attributed to aspect-angle-dependent obscuration.

For the case of NGC 1068 we collected the data in . Some of the observed H _2 O-maser spots around the radio core component S1 trace a rotating disk or ring. The maser velocities appear inconsistent with Keplerian rotation around a central point mass. The rotation profile can be due to a massive disk , but we follow and find a model with a black hole mass of 1.2 10^7 M_ and a strongly concentrated stellar cluster with _ R^ at large radii, a core radius of 0.32 pc and a core density of _ , = 5.25 10^6 M_ pc ^ 3 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as a Seyfert comparison where high local HCN ratios, similar IR-to-FIR colors, and low FIR/HCN indicate AGN-related excitation.

quotes.quote: Recent investigations revealed abnormally high local ratios larger than 0.5 very close to AGNs (e.g. NGC 1068; Usero et al. 2004), suggesting that here the HCN is excited by the AGN emission rather than by starbursts. Assuming similar excitation mechanisms close to a QSO, we can exclude, that the AGN in our observed QSOs is dominating the whole galaxy in a way, that global HCN/CO ratios above 0.5 appear. In Fig. fig:3 we plotted the IR-to-FIR luminosity ratio of the investigated galaxies.

Only the known active galaxy of Seyfert II type, NGC 1068, shows similar flux ratios. This suggests, that the complete IR luminosity (8-1000  m) of quasar hosts comprises a significant contribution of hot dust which is not apparent in non-active galaxies, even in the ULIRG stage. The central AGN is most probably responsible for that contribution. Similarly the global L _ FIR HCN ratio in NGC 1068 seems to be smaller than usual (Fig. fig:2 ), indicating an additional HCN excitation by the AGN.

sources.summary: The paper presents high-resolution mid-infrared spectroscopy of NGC 1068, separating compact torus emission from ionization-cone dust and showing strong subarcsecond spectral complexity.

quotes.quote: We present spatially-resolved, near-diffraction-limited 10 m spectra of the nucleus of the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068, obtained with Michelle, the mid-IR imager and spectrometer on the 8.1 m Gemini North telescope. The spectra cover the nucleus and the central 6.0 \ 0.4 \ of the ionization cones at a spatial resolution of approximately 0.4 \ ( 30 parsecs). The spectra extracted in 0.4 \ steps along the slit reveal striking variations in continuum slope, silicate feature profile and depth, and fine structure line fluxes on subarcsecond scales, illustrating in unprecedented detail the complexity of the circumnuclear regions of this galaxy at mid-IR wavelengths. A comparison of photometry in various apertures reveals two distinct components: a compact (radius < 15 pc), bright source within the central 0.4 \ 0.4 \ and extended, lower brightness emission.

We identify the compact source with the AGN obscuring torus, and the diffuse component with the AGN-heated dust in the ionization cones. While the torus emission dominates the flux observed in the near-IR, the mid-IR flux measured with apertures larger than about 1 \ is dominated instead by the dust emission from the ionization cones; in spite of its higher brightness, the torus contributes less than 30 of the 11.6 \ flux contained in the central 1.2 \ region.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1068's NLR dynamics, finding radiative driving too steep, gravity alone insufficient, and drag needed to decelerate clouds over 100-400 pc.

quotes.quote: We present dynamical models based on a study of high-resolution long-slit spectra of the narrow-line region (NLR) in NGC 1068 obtained with the Space Telescope Imaging Spectrograph (STIS) aboard The Hubble Space Telescope (HST) . The dynamical models consider the radiative force due to the active galactic nucleus (AGN), gravitational forces from the supermassive black hole (SMBH), nuclear stellar cluster, and galactic bulge, and a drag force due to the NLR clouds interacting with a hot ambient medium. The derived velocity profile of the NLR gas is compared to that obtained from our previous kinematic models of the NLR using a simple biconical geometry for the outflowing NLR clouds. The results show that the acceleration profile due to radiative line driving is too steep to fit the data and that gravitational forces along cannot slow the clouds down, but with drag forces included, the clouds can slow down to the systemic velocity over the range 100--400 pc, as observed.

Our analysis applies to NGC 1068 in particular, but has relevance to all Seyferts in general that show signs of gas outflow and subsequent deceleration Ruiz2005 . To test whether gravity is playing any role in stopping and turning back the gas velocity, we will construct a mass profile within 10,000 pc from the nucleus of NGC 1068.

sources.summary: The paper uses UV and X-ray observations to argue that NGC 1068 has anomalous NLR gas composition, making this Seyfert a benchmark for active-galaxy abundance studies.

quotes.quote: Recent ultraviolet and X-ray observations suggest that the Fe/O ratio in the NLR gas in NGC 1068 is abnormally high. The X-ray analysis, which is presented elsewhere, suggests a large Fe/H and the data shown and discussed here, in particular the extremely weak \ line, argue for small O/N and O/C. Models to support this claim are shown and discussed. They include improved reddening estimates, dusty and dust-free calculations, and a range of abundances. The anomelous composition makes NGC 1068 unique among Seyfert galaxies and an unusual laboratory for investigating metal enrichment and depletion.
NGC 1068 is perhaps the ideal benchmark for studying gas composition in active galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper models ASCA soft X-ray lines in NGC 1068, testing Fe/O abundance claims and favoring photoionized gas for the galaxy nucleus emission.

quotes.quote: Previous X-ray and ultraviolet spectroscopy suggested that the Fe/O abundance ratio in NGC 1068 may be abnormally high. We have tested this suggestion by measuring and modeling the ASCA spectrum of NGC 1068. We have measured some 15 X-ray lines, to an accuracy better than a factor 2, and modeled the continuum in two different ways.

NGC 1068 is perhaps the best studied Seyfert 2 galaxy, with X-ray observations by EXOSAT , GINGA , ROSAT , BBXRT and ASCA (for references and general description see Marshall ; 1993). The most striking X-ray feature is the strong (equivalent width, EW = 3 keV) \ complex that is split into three components corresponding to ``neutral'' (6.4--6.5 keV), He-like (6.7 keV) and H-like (6.96 keV) iron lines. The large EW is probably the result of the obscured central continuum and the directly viewed line producing gas (Krolik Kallman; 1988).

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 1068 with Spitzer, finding PAH and infrared emission tied to inner-spiral star formation, H-alpha knots, and spectra dominated by starlight rather than AGN-heated dust.

quotes.quote: We present a study of the nearby Seyfert galaxy NGC 1068 using mid- and far- infrared data acquired with the IRAC, IRS, and MIPS instruments aboard the Spitzer Space Telescope. The images show extensive 8 m and 24 m emission coinciding with star formation in the inner spiral approximately 15 (1 kpc) from the nucleus, and a bright complex of star formation 47 \ (3 kpc) SW of the nucleus. The brightest 8 m PAH emission regions coincide remarkably well with knots observed in an H image. Strong PAH features at 6.2, 7.7, 8.6, and 11.3 m are detected in IRS spectra measured at numerous locations inside, within, and outside the inner spiral. The IRAC colors and IRS spectra of these regions rule out dust heated by the AGN as the primary emission source; the SEDs are dominated by starlight and PAH emission.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 71 with NGC 1068/M 77, describing it as a spiral Seyfert 2 with compact radio jet, oversized galaxy image, visible spiral structure, active nucleus, and globular clusters.

quotes.quote: 3C 71 (NGC 1068, M 77); z=0.003793 A well-known spiral galaxy, and the archetypal Seyfert 2, NGC 1068 is not truly ``radio-loud'' in terms of its radio luminosity. It has an obscured Sy 1 spectrum antonuccimiller85 and a compact (few arcsecond) radio jet aligned NNE-SSW. The NICMOS data were obtained as part of the NICMOS GTO time by thompsoncorbin99 . The galaxy dwarfs the small NIC2 chip, and even the WFPC2 mosaic (PROPOSID's 5479, 5754). We were unable to produce an accurate photometric fit to this object, since we could not estimate the background flux.

sources.summary: The paper uses MIDI observations of the Type 2 AGN NGC1068 as high-spatial-frequency mid-infrared torus data and as a lower limit supporting a common AGN radial brightness distribution.

quotes.quote: In addition, we also use the MIDI data for the Type 2 AGN NGC1068 of . The data provide the highest spatial frequency information in the mid-IR, and the inclination effect at these wavelengths is expected to be relatively small compared with that in the near-IR (see more below).

Therefore, the mid-IR visibilities of the Type 2 AGN NGC1068 provide quite a meaningful lower limit for those of the Type 1 objects at the same spatial frequencies (indicated by the upward arrows in Fig. fig-vis-pl ). If we put together the lower limit from NGC1068 with the visibilities observed for the Type 1 AGNs in the mid-IR (Fig. fig-vis-pl ), the data at different spatial freqencies appear quite coherent, i.e. consistent with the case where these objects share roughly a common radial brightness distribution in face-on inclinations. When compared with various model curves shown in Fig. fig-vis-pl , the mid-IR brightness distribution appears to be consistent with a power-law form, where the index is 2 at 8 m.

sources.summary: NGC 1068 is included as a benchmark obscured Compton-thick AGN with uncertain intrinsic X-ray power and bright subarcsecond mid-infrared core emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068 NGC 1068 is one of the best-studied of obscured AGN; hence our decision to include it on our correlation. In X-rays, it is known to be substantially Compton-thick and reflection-dominated, with > 25 matt00, iwasawa97, koyama89 . Correcting from the observed flux of X-rays scattered from above the obscuring medium into the line-of-sight, or from the flux of the forbidden 5007 \ emission line, intrinsic log L_ 2-10 estimates of 42.8-44 are derived ( iwasawa03, panessa06, levenson06 ). In the mid-IR, several high-resolution studies have been carried out from the ground revealing bright, extended structures on sub-arcsec scales and beyond galliano05_ngc1068, bock00, alloin00 . At the highest spatial sampling, continuum core fluxes of 9--10 Jy have been reported around 12- \ mason06, tomono01 ; these correspond to log L_ 43.8 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1068 as the sample's most obscured Compton-thick AGN and uses its hard X-ray to [O IV] ratio to infer a very high column density.

quotes.quote: The famous Compton--thick NGC 1068 is the most highly--obscured AGN in our sample, based on its hard X-ray to [ O IV ] luminosity ratio. Its [ O IV ] luminosity Assuming a distance of 14.4 Mpc. of 1.2 10^ 8 \ predicts an intrinsic AGN luminosity of ( 3.1 0.9 ) 10^ 11 \ using the Seyfert 1 calibration. The [ O IV ] flux is taken from high resolution ISO spectra sturm , so [ Fe II ] contamination is not an issue; in any case the [ Fe II ] line strength is only 4 that of [ O IV ] . The hard X-ray to [ O IV ] ratio is 490 lower than the Seyfert 1 best fit, implying a column density of N_H = 25.3 to 25.4 \ in the matt99 models. (The column should be still higher if scattered light contributes substantially to the observed X-ray flux.) This inferred column is consistent with the lower limits inferred through a similar comparison to [ O III ] 5007 \ matt97, matt2000 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as a mid-infrared AGN benchmark, noting bright Compton-thick emission, weak PAH features, and a significant AGN contribution.

quotes.quote: Even heavily Compton-thick AGNs that are weak at X-ray energies can be identified using mid-IR spectroscopy (e.g., \ the well-studied NGC 1068 with N_ H > 10^ 25 has bright emission; sturm02 ). By contrast, PAH features tend to be weak or absent in AGN-dominated systems (e.g., NGC 1068; rigopoulou02 ). Comparing this to an AGN-dominated system (i.e., \ NGC 1068) we find EW _ 11.3,PAH (100 percent AGN) 0.008 m.

We note that the predicted AGN--star-formation contributions for four ( 25 percent) of the galaxies (NGC 1068, NGC 4051, NGC 5128 and NGC 5643) differ by more than a factor of two from the mixing model, whilst the other mid-IR identified AGNs lie within a factor of 40 percent. On the basis of Fig. fig_mir_bpt b, the IR emission for five ( 29^ +20 _ 13 percent) of the mid-IR identified AGNs (NGC 1068, NGC 6300, NGC 4051, NGC 5643 and NGC 1448) has a significant contribution from AGN activity ( > 25 percent), only one of which is an optically unidentified AGN (NGC 1448).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068 as an exceptionally Compton-thick Seyfert whose direct AGN emission is hidden even above 10 keV, using Fe K and ionized-scattering estimates to bracket intrinsic X-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: We present our own X-ray analysis of three galaxies below, and we give special consideration to the exceptional case of NGC 1068. NGC 1068 is extremely optically thick, and the direct AGN emission is not detected even above 10 keV Matt97 . In this case, the range of L_X indicates the uncertainty in determining the intrinsic luminosity. We use the results of Lev06 , adopting the estimate of L_X from the Fe K line equivalent width (EW) and luminosity, and considering the fitted reflection model a lower limit, for D=14.4 Mpc. We use the estimate of Iwa97 , who consider the properties of the ionized scattering region, as an upper limit on L_X .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068 as an extreme Seyfert/XDR outlier with strong mid-infrared excess and unusual ionization or chemistry affecting dense-gas line ratios.

quotes.quote: There are a few outliers, the most striking example being the prototype Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068, which has the largest MIR-excess and lies 4.5 from the mean. Interestingly, even when the fractional AGN contribution to the mid-IR light dominates over the starburst contribution, it still appears to be the case that the AGN total IR contribution is mostly less than 25-30 2008MNRAS.385L.130N .

NGC 1068 (and possibly IRAS 05189-2524) may be a special case where unusual ionization or chemistry effects could play a significant role in producing the observed / \ luminosity ratios. Our result is in agreement with findings of 2004A&A...419..897U who conclude that the circumnuclear region of NGC 1068 is effectively a giant XDR. These authors used a combination of single dish and interferometry data of several molecular species to rule out alternative explanations for the observed high HCN/CO ratio 1994ApJ...426L..

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068 as a Seyfert 2 whose observed molecular emission is likely dominated by its nuclear starburst ring, with several detected spectral features.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068, NGC 4258 and NGC 6240 all have Seyfert 2 nuclei; however, the emission we observe in NGC 1068 is likely dominated by the surrounding nuclear starburst ring sch00 .

In NGC 1068 and NGC 4258 only the H41 recombination line was detected and in NGC 4258 this was only one of the three lines detected. In both of these galaxies there is no evidence for any of the other recombination lines at comparable strengths as the H41 line as would be expected, therefore, the identification of this feature at 92.035 GHz as a hydrogen recombinations line is suspect. The only spectral line detected in all 10 galaxies is . \ was detected in nine galaxies, HCN, HNC and CS were detected in seven galaxies and \ was detected in six galaxies. Because our sample of galaxies vary in distance from 2.9 to 101 Mpc, the absolute detection threshold is not the same for these galaxies, however, in all subsequent analysis we utilize line ratios. In only six galaxies, Arp 220, NGC 1068, NGC 253, M82, IC 342, and Maffei 2, five or more spectral features are detected.
The emission observed in NGC 1068 may be dominated by the starburst ring and not gas closely associated with the Seyfert nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as the real Seyfert-2 reference for radiation-pressure cloud simulations, adopting its core-region parameters and comparing to a two-component torus/disc model.

quotes.quote: We treat the central accretion disc as an isotropically radiating point-source with a spectral energy distribution as shown in Fig. 3b of Schartmann_05 , but normalised to correspond to 10 of the Eddington luminosity for the case of the nearby Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068.
Those simulations had been prepared to represent the core region of the nearby Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068.

In this paper, we investigated the radiation pressure interaction of infalling dusty gas clouds with the active nucleus of a Seyfert galaxy, exemplified for the physical parameters of NGC 1068. Dictated by the gas column density, clouds will be accreted or expelled from the central region. Outward accelerated clouds will interact and merge with clouds and filaments further out, until the critical column density is reached. Fig. fig:coldens_3d shows the column density distribution in radial direction for the 3D model of the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068 as discussed in Schartmann_10 . It is shown for all polar angles, each of them averaged over the azimuthal angle. Clearly visible is the two-component structure -- the geometrically thin, but high-column density disc and the extended low-column density torus on tens of parsec scale.

sources.summary: The paper presents new Q-band spectroscopy and torus modeling for NGC 1068, finding excellent fits to its silicate feature and 20 micron spectrum.

quotes.quote: Q band ( 20 m) spectroscopy of NGC 1068 was obtained with Michelle on 20090923 (Program ID GN-2009B-Q-58), using the same chop-nod technique. The 3-pixel (0.54 ) slit and lowQ grating were used, for R 100 , with the slit orientated along the ionisation cones (20 degrees E of N). The galaxy nucleus was observed for a total of 1800 sec on-source, and telluric standard stars were observed before and after NGC 1068.

For NGC 1068 we also show in Figure 5 the fit to the Q band spectrum. It is clear that the interpolated version of the Nenkova et al. (2008a,b) clumpy torus models produce excellent fits to the N band silicate feature, and the 20 m spectrum of NGC 1068.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1068's outer profile as II.o-OLR and describes bar evidence, dust features shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068 (II.o-OLR; (R)SA(rs)b) : This is the closest Seyfert 2 galaxy. Although the RC3 classification is (R)SA(rs)b, it is in fact double-barred. erwin04 summarizes the evidence for both bars, including the inner bar first detected in the NIR by scoville88 and the ``oval disk'' first noted by kormendy79 ; see schinnerer00 for evidence that the latter structure is dynamically barlike. Because the galaxy is so large in angular size, we combined a total of three adjacent SDSS fields. The center is somewhat dusty, so that we used a z image to determine the center, and then translated this information to the image. Although we derive a somewhat higher inclination and a different PA than did pt06 PT06, we get the same classification (this is the only overlap of our sample with PT06).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068 as a type-2 AGN with dominant near-infrared coronal lines, extended emitting regions, heavy absorption, and published line-width measurements compared with this sample.

quotes.quote: Prototype Ty2 AGNs such as NGC 1068 or Circinus, not only show the full spectrum of CLs (i.e., Si, Al, Ca, among others), but in addition, these are the dominant lines in their spectra b93,b29,mul09,mul11 . Additional arguments against the CLR being obscured by the torus can be gathered from Circinus, NGC 1068 and Centaurus A. They all are extreme Compton thick sources even in the hard X-rays.

Moreover, the high spatial resolution studies conducted by b50 and m10 show that Ty2 sources display strong [ Fe vii ] , [ Fe x ] and [ Fe xi ] lines, with emitting regions extending to distances of a few hundred parsecs from the centre in the case of NGC 1068. Similarly, b33 reported for NGC 1068 FWHM values for [ Fe xi ] 0.7892 m and [ Si ix ] 3.9350 m smaller than those of [ Fe ii ] 1.257 m whereas b50 and this work (Table tablex2 ) find the opposite: low- and intermediate- ionization lines present FWHMs smaller or at least comparable to those of the optical and near-IR CLs.

sources.summary: The paper summarizes NGC1068 as a well-studied barred spiral with multiple bars, a Seyfert nucleus, radio jets, red infrared nucleus, and pseudobulge evidence.

quotes.quote: NGC1068 (M77) is one of the most well studied barred spiral galaxies. The main bar was first observed in the near-IR by tex:SM88 while other studies have observed multiple bars (e.g. tex:E04 ). The nucleus hosts a Seyfert1-2 source with double jet observed in the radio ( tex:GB96 ) making the mid- and near- IR nuclei (inner 4   arcsec) appear extremely red ( tex:HS98,tex:BN00 ). tex:DF07 identify the existence of a pseudobulge based on the nuclear structure of the galaxy. H09 found n_ bulge =1.51 , n_ bar =0.7 plus an exponential disk.

sources.summary: The review presents NGC1068 as an extreme nearby Compton-thick AGN whose very high column density blocks transmitted X-rays and strongly diminishes the observed flux.

quotes.quote: NGC1068 represents an extreme case study of a Compton-thick AGN. This is one of the nearest AGN at a redshift of z=0.038 . It has a column density of about 10^ 25 cm^ 2 as derived from BeppoSAX observations (Matt et al. 2004). At such high column densities ( >5 10^ 24 cm^ 2 ), the transmitted component is totally blocked by the obscuring material. The observed X-ray flux, arising from the back-side of the obscuring screen, is diminished by almost two orders of magnitude.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068 as an AGN prototype whose line surveys target central chemistry, aiming to isolate AGN effects from the surrounding starburst ring.

quotes.quote: This project sample consist of 6 galaxies, including two young starbursts (M 83 and NGC 253), two evolved starbursts (M 82 and M 51), the active galactic nucleus (AGN) prototype NGC 1068 and the ULIRG Arp 220. With more than a dozen molecular species detected, these surveys will be able to establish the differences between the chemistry in starburst galaxies (NGC 253 and IC 342) and the AGN dominated NGC 1068. Thus the main motivation of this work is to study the chemical fingerprints of the AGN influence on the surrounding molecular gas. The relatively small ( 18'' ) beam of the 45 m telescope is smaller than the starburst ring in NGC 1068, allowing the observation of the chemical composition towards the AGN without contamination from the molecular material in starburst.
Similarly, Kamenetzky2011 observed the central region of NGC 1068 with Z-spec, where they identified 12 molecular species.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 to test cross-correlation detection of complex emission-line profiles, finding numerous asymmetric or multi-asymmetric [OIII] spectra in its central Seyfert region.

quotes.quote: Applying the proposed procedure of studying the symmetry of the C-C peak function explained in section 2 to the spectra from the central region of NGC 1068, we can point out the following: Only 12 of the emission lines profiles in the central 24 ^ 20 ^ of the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068 (Fig. oiii_profiles ) seems to present symmetric profiles. The percentage of symmetric [ OIII ] 4959,5007 profiles is 24.5 for those spectra with a signal-to-noise larger than 200. 6 of the total number of spectra presents pure red-asymmetries. Red asymmetries are detected in the 26 of spectra with a signal-to-noise larger than 200. 51 of the total number of spectra presents pure blue-asymmetries.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068 as a local AGN whose near-infrared photometry, heavy X-ray absorption, and detected hot dust make it an important outlier in luminosity and color relations.

quotes.quote: In NGC 1068, NGC 1566 and NGC 7582, however, our photometries are significantly (about two magnitudes or a factor 6) brighter than those of prieto2010 . This could indicate problems with the PSF correction in the very high spatial resolution data from prieto2010 .

NGC 1068 has been excluded from all fits involving X-ray measurements since it is heavily absorbed bauer2015 . This affects even the very high energy band used by Swift/BAT as can easily be seen from its large offset from the respective relations.

This is in contrast to NGC 1068, where a similar resolution is reached in infrared interferometric observation and a hot component is clearly detected lopezgonzaga2014 - consistent with the ``bluer'' color of NGC 1068 compared to the Circinus galaxy.

sources.summary: NGC 1068 is discussed as a Seyfert 2 with a circumnuclear disk, surrounding starburst ring, complex gas dynamics, and several shock or dense-gas molecular tracers.

quotes.quote: Among the galaxies in our sample, M 51, NGC 1068, and NGC 7469 host an AGN (the first two are classified as Sy 2, and the latter as Sy 1, Osterbrock93 ). A detailed study devoted to our results in NGC 1068 has been presented by Aladro13 . The CNDs of these galaxies are surrounded by arms of active star forming regions at 1-2 kpc distance from their centres. Other observations of NGC 1068 indicate that some molecules show significant emission from the starburst ring ( <25 for SiO (2-1) and <30 for HCO ^+(1-0) , Usero04 ), while high transitions of the dense gas tracers HCN, HCO ^+ and CS are hardly detected outside the CND Burillo14 . In addition, the gas dynamics in the CND of NGC 1068 are quite complex, and some of the brightest lines, as seen by high-resolution observations, need several (two to four) Gaussian components to better fit the profiles Krips11,Burillo14 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1068 as a distant gamma-ray starburst and prototypical Seyfert whose far-infrared luminosity, active nucleus, star-forming region, and Fermi emission exceed a pure starburst explanation.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068 The most distant gamma-ray detected starburst galaxy, at 13.7 Mpc, NGC 1068 has the lowest detected 100 m flux, yet its luminosity is more than four times greater ( L_ 100 m 8.6 10^ 24 W Hz ^ 1 ) than the other starburst galaxies selected in our sample . Indeed, it is the steep far-IR spectrum and its 100 m luminosity that classifies this object as a starburst . However, its weak active nucleus, surrounded by a region of intense star formation extending 1 kpc , has been widely discussed in the literature as the prototypical Seyfert. A study comparing the gamma-ray emission of NGC 1068 detected by Fermi LAT with those of M 82, NGC 253, and NGC 4945 cited that its gamma-ray luminosity was too high to be explained only by starburst activity . The best-fit photon number power-law index for 100 MeV -- 100 GeV energies is = 2.32 0.10 .

sources.summary: The paper records NGC 1068's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068: We consider the small classical component of the composite bulge, getting R_e=0.5'' Erwin2015a . This explains the large difference with Sani2011 , who derive R_e=8.4'' for the pseudo bulge of the galaxy. The M/L is derived using the distance of KormendyHo2013 and fitting the major-axis kinematic profile ^2+V^2 of Fig. 5 of Emsellem2006 , summing the bulge and disk contributions (see App. C and Fig. fig_kinprofile ). The value M/L_K=0.7 derived by Erwin2015a for the stellar population of the classical bulge is a factor 1.9 larger. We adopt this as the upper error estimate.

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 1068 with Swift spectra but treats it as Compton-thick, making intrinsic emission and spectral variability unreliable in the Swift band.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068 Three flux-binned spectra could be produced from the available Swift data of NGC 1068. The components of our best-fitting model to the spectra required an absorbed power law, a hot gas component for contamination from the host. This is consistent with previous modelling of the source, which used similar models e.g. Bauer2014,Brightman2015 . However, the photon index we obtain is higher than that found with previous data from e.g. NuStar which included higher energies than those detected by Swift , though indices of >2 have been found. NGC 1068 is widely thought to be a Compton-thick AGN, making the intrinsic emission difficult to model Antonucci1985,Ghisellini1994,Brightman2015 , as the degree of absorption could not be constrained with the energy range of Swift , the flux extracted from this spectrum was much lower than the true value.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1068 as a Seyfert 2 AGN with an AGN-driven outflow, extended Seyfert-type excitation, and only a nuclear bar.

quotes.quote: Two of our sample galaxies, NGC 1068 and NGC 5194 (M 51a) are classified as having Seyfert 2 nuclei. In NGC 1068, which has an AGN-driven outflow antonucci1985, evans1991, macchetto1994, riffel2014 , the S II excitation maps show Seyfert type conditions out to large projected distances of 7 kpc .

The rejected galaxy, NGC 1068, is classified as a Seyfert 2 AGN and most of the spaxels in both the nuclear regions and extended disc show Seyfert type excitation conditions (as seen in Figure fig:bpt-sliced ) so we remove this galaxy from further analysis.

NGC 5194 (M51a) and NGC 1068 do not appear to host large-scale stellar bars, but do host nuclear bars. The small size of nuclear bars compared to the total size of the disc in a galaxy limits their ability to radially redistribute gas, so we classify these galaxies as ``Unbarred.'' These nuclear bar structures are confirmed in both galaxies by , where a_ bar /R_ 25 < 0.1 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1068 as a prototypical Seyfert 2 AGN with polarized broad lines, unusually successful near-infrared interferometry, and mid-infrared model decomposition despite difficult coverage.

quotes.quote: Strong support has been given to the idea when Antonucci Miller showed in 1985 that broad lines are seen in the polarized light of the nearby prototypical Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068. For example, NGC 1068 and the Circinus galaxy, the brightest AGNs in the southern sky, offer a flux density of about 10 Jy at 12 m, comparable to the faint end of objects usually studied with this method . Thus, with the currently available baseline lengths, the near-infrared structure is only marginally resolved, except for NGC 1068 which is the only type 2 AGN successfully observed with near-infrared interferometry so far.

A detailed model image and flux decomposition has also been achieved for the only other bright AGN target for MIDI, the prototypical Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068 , but it suffers from a very unfortunate (u,v) coverage due to its declination near 0^ . The usage of the VLTI Sub-Array (VISA), i.e. the smaller Auxiliary Telescopes (ATs), has allowed us to better characterize the extended component in this galaxy and determine its orientation in the polar direction as well.

sources.summary: The paper reports ALMA CO emission from NGC 1068, resolving a compact nuclear structure and high-velocity bipolar outflow that support an AGN torus disk-wind scenario.

quotes.quote: We have detected in ALMA observations CO J = 6 5 emission from the nucleus of the Seyfert galaxy NGC 1068. The low-velocity (up to 70 \ relative to systemic) CO emission resolves into a 12 7 pc structure, roughly aligned with the nuclear radio source. Higher-velocity emission (up to 400 ) is consistent with a bipolar outflow in a direction nearly perpendicular ( 80 ) to the nuclear disk. The position-velocity diagram shows that in addition to the outflow, the velocity field may also contain rotation about the disk axis. These observations provide compelling evidence in support of the disk-wind scenario for the AGN obscuring torus.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1068 as a gamma-ray bright star-forming galaxy with 4.42 significance after background modeling, and includes it among the four brightest systems used to derive the average spectrum.

quotes.quote: Our analysis confirms previous results indicating that the systems NGC 253, NGC 1068, NGC 3034 and NGC 4945 are ray bright SFGs. Utilizing the distribution of P _ bg to translate the likelihood function of each point source the statistical significance of the source (taking into account the possibility of background mismodeling), we can determine that the ray emission observed from each SFG is statistically significant at confidence levels of: 7.00 (NGC 253), 4.42 (NGC 1068), 8.06 (NGC 3034), and 7.10 (NGC 4945). Because the distribution P _ bg takes into account the possibility of background fluctuations, we note that all of these sources are highly significant.

Using the four brightest systems in our analysis (NGC 253, NGC 1068, NGC 3034 and NGC 4945), we calculate the average ray spectrum above 1 GeV to follow a power-law with a spectral index of = -2.32 0.06. These four systems are all individually best-fit with power-law spectra between -2.2 and -2.4.

sources.summary: NGC1068 is included as a molecular-wind AGN with CO high-velocity wings, weak ongoing nuclear star formation, no strong OH absorption, and wind velocities estimated from published spectra.

quotes.quote: Indeed, Davies et al. (2007) found that the on going SFR in the nuclei of Markarian 231 and NGC1068 is probably very small, because of the small observed Br equivalent width within 0.1-0.5 kpc from the active nucleus.

We used additional data from Feruglio et al (2013a,b: NGC6240), Feruglio et al. (2015: Markarian 231), Krips et al. (2011) and Garcia-Burillo et al. (2014; NGC1068), Morganti et al. 2013a,b (IC5066), Alatalo et al. 2011 (NGC1266), Combes et al. (2013; NGC1433), and Sun et al. (2014; SDSSJ135646.10+102609.0). In all these cases the winds are traced from high velocity wings observed in CO(1-0), CO(2-1), CO(3-2).

One source with a CO outflow, NGC1068, does not have strong OH absorption (Stone et al. 2016). It should however be noted that Herschel samples a much bigger region than that on which the CO outflow has been detected.

sources.summary: The survey treats NGC 1068 as a Seyfert galaxy whose water emission traces a circumnuclear disk, star-forming ring, and hot shocked gas from radio-jet interaction.

quotes.quote: NGC1068 NGC 1068 is a nearby and bright Seyfert galaxy seen at an inclination angle of i 40 deg. Molecular gas (CO and HCN) observations towards its center have shown a central circumnuclear disk (CND) with a radius around 100 - 150 pc e.g., krips2011, spinoglio2012 , and a star-forming ring/spiral at a radius of 1.0 - 1.5 kpc tacconi1994, schinnerer2000 . As the brightest prototype Seyfert-2 AGN, NGC 1068 exhibits pronounced radio jets extending out to several kpc from the center gallimore2004, krips2006 .

According to the derived physical parameters, the warm component is very likely associated with the CND, while the cold component should be at least partly related to the star-forming ring/spiral. Therefore, we speculate that this gas is probably associated with the part of molecular gas in the CND which is shock heated to high kinetic temperatures as a consequence of an interaction between the radio jet and the CND krips2011 .

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1068 as an outflowing AGN target, measuring a high broad-component density and localized outflow densities above 1000 cm^-3.

quotes.quote: Three of the targets, namely NGC 1068, NGC 1266 and NGC 2110, are classified as having an ionized outflow based on the presence of broad components in the 5007 profile of the S7 data. The broad components of the profiles in the three targets show fast outflows with FWHM > 500 km/s compared to the other targets which require only narrow components with FWHM < 200 km/s (See Sect. sect3.1 for more details on emission line fitting).

For the broad outflowing component in NGC 1068, NGC 1266 and NGC 2110, we get a density value of 726 90, 1120 295 and 237 78 cm ^ 3 respectively. The spectrum showing these broad components are shown in Fig. fig:sii_spectrum_outflow . Therefore, from the integrated spectrum analysis, the outflowing component has a significantly higher density compared to the non-outflowing components.

In targets with an active outflow inferred from the broad profiles such as NGC 1068 and NGC 2110, the density can reached values > 1000 cm ^ 3 in certain locations supporting a dense outflow scenario as predicted from the integrated spectrum analysis described earlier.

sources.summary: NGC 1068 is a well-known Seyfert 2 AGN selected in optical, infrared, and X-rays, with PAH constraints and diffuse X-rays following spiral arms.

quotes.quote: [ 121 ] NGC 1068 : This well-known AGN meets our selection criteria in all bands: Seyfert 2 in both of the used optical classifications, presence of the [ Ne v ] line in infrared and clear detection of the Fe K line at 6.4 keV in X-rays with a significance of 3.6 . Note that the equivalent width of the 6.2 m PAH feature is not presented in StiArm2013 due to saturation of the spectrograph. HowMaz2007 analyze PAH and warm dust emission in NGC 1068 in detail. Their 6.2 m images are saturated within the inner 500 pc, though they measure an equivalent width of the PAH feature right outside the region of saturation of 0.1 . This is suggestive that the value of of the equivalent width would drop below 0.1 further in.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1068 as an archetypal nearby Seyfert 2 with starburst activity, a bar, a radio jet, bipolar AGN cones, and ionized and molecular outflows.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068 (Seyfert 2 - D_ L 12.5 Mpc - 1" 60 pc) is one of the closest and archetypal Seyfert 2 galaxies. This galaxy also hosts powerful starburst activity (e.g. lester1987,bruhweiler1991 ) and is characterised by a large-scale oval and a nuclear stellar bar aligned NE-SW scoville1988,thronson1989 . NGC 1068 is known to host a radio jet, observed from X-ray to radio wavelengths bland-hawthorn1997 .

Its activity seems confined to bipolar cones centred on the AGN, intersecting the plane of the disc with an inclination of 45^ , such that the disc interstellar medium to the NE and SW sees the nuclear radiation field directly cecil1990,gallimore1994 . This galaxy shows clear evidence of outflowing material in both the ionised (e.g. macchetto1994,axon1997,cecil2000,barbosa2014 ) and molecular gas burillo2014,gallimore2016 , possibly driven by the radio jet ( cecil1990,gallimore1994,axon1997 ), located in the north-east and south-west directions.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as the best CHRESOS example, finding all twelve selected X-ray lines and a power-law DEM whose shape closely matches radiation-pressure-compression predictions.

quotes.quote: Only in one source, NGC 1068, all the 12 lines are detected: the derived DEM distribution is shown in Fig. EMD1 (left panel). The observed DEM evidently appears as a power-law distribution: a linear regression gives a slope of 0.85 (the blue dotted line in the plot). In the same figure, the RPC prediction is over-imposed, the one that best reproduce the data, chosen among those derived for different illuminating SEDs and metallicities (see Fig. EMD_RPC ). The correspondence between the observed DEM and the distribution predicted for a RPC gas is impressive.
Plugging the observed DEM of NGC 1068 ( 10^ 65.1 _ 100 ^ 0.85 cm ^ 3 , Table tab2 ) we get a maximum radius of r_ max = 40 L^2_ X;43 _ 100 ^ 1.15 pc , similar to eqn. ( e: rmax ).

sources.summary: The paper presents a 3 mm molecular line survey of NGC 1068, describing it as a nearby gas-rich AGN galaxy with strong HCN, CN, and HC3N relative to 13CO.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: RA(J2000.0) = 2h42m40s.798 In this value was referred from , where a slightly different value of 2h42m40s.7098 was reported. , Dec(J2000.0) = 00D00'47".938 , and 1150 km s ^ 1 for NGC 1068

quotes.quote: We present observational data of a molecular line survey toward the nearby galaxies NGC 1068, NGC 253, and IC 342 at the wavelengths of 3 mm ( 85--116 GHz) obtained with the Nobeyama 45 m radio telescope. In IC 342 the line survey with high spectral resolution at the 3 mm region was reported for the first time. NGC 1068 is a nearby gas-rich galaxy with X-rays from an active galactic nucleus (AGN), and NGC 253 and IC 342 are nearby gas-rich galaxies with prototypical starbursts. These galaxies are useful to study the impacts of X-rays and ultraviolet radiation on molecular abundances.

As a result we could obtain almost complete data of these galaxies at the 3 mm region: We detected 19--23 molecular species depending on the galaxies including several new detections (e.g., cyclic-C _3 H _2 in IC 342). We found that the intensities of HCN, CN, and HC _3 N relative to ^ 13 CO are significantly strong in NGC 1068 compared to those in NGC 253 and IC 342.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as a test case for CTA constraints on gamma-ray emission models involving AGN-driven molecular winds, weak radio structures, and active-nucleus contributions.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we assess the potentiality of the Cherenkov Telescope Array (CTA) to test CR acceleration models in non-blazar AGN, focusing on the test case of the prototypical Seyfert galaxy NGC 1068 that is a nearby (D=14.4 Mpc) Seyfert 2 galaxy in which AGN-driven molecular wind, and radio structures, similar to collimated outflows but weaker and slower than the jets observed in blazars, have been observed Krips11,Garcia14,Gallimore96,Wynn85,Gallimore06, Sajina11,Honig08 . Moreover, it is the brightest of the star forming galaxies detected by Fermi LAT Ackermann12 . The Fermi collaboration interpreted the gamma-ray detection in terms of CR associated to star-formation processes, adopting the paradigm that Galactic CR are relativistic particles accelerated in SN-driven winds, and that the gamma-ray emission is due to CR interaction with the galactic interstellar medium (ISM). However, some models assuming that the gamma-ray emission is entirely due to starburst activity underestimated the Fermi LAT spectrum Yoast14,Eichmann15 . This suggests that the active nucleus also contributes to the gamma-ray emission.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068 as a prototypical Seyfert with prior starburst and ALMA line-survey context, but reports that its NH3 lines were not detected because of insufficient sensitivity.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068, the prototypical Seyfert galaxy, is also one of the most well-studied objects. Multiple ALMA line surveys have been conducted e.g. 2014AaA...567A.125G,2015PASJ...67....8N,2016ApJ...823L..12G,2016MNRAS.459.3629I .
NGC 1068 underwent two starbursts in the recent past 2012ApJ...755...87S , and Circinus's outflow suggests starburst characteristics 2016ApJ...832..142Z .

The only galaxy with previous NH _3 detections in which we fail to detect the molecule in is NGC 1068. However, the RMS noises of our NGC 1068 spectra are insufficient to detect the lines at the intensities measured by 2011AaA...529A.154A due to the short duration of that observation.

sources.summary: The paper resolves the nucleus of Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068 with GRAVITY, finding a 0.24 pc thin dust-sublimation ring and placing it in AGN unification context.

quotes.quote: We present near-infrared interferometric data on the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068, obtained with the GRAVITY instrument on the European Southern Observatory Very Large Telescope Interferometer. The extensive baseline coverage from 5 to 60 M allowed us to reconstruct a continuum image of the nucleus with an unrivaled 0.2 pc resolution in the K band. We find a thin ring-like structure of emission with a radius =0.24 0.03 pc, inclination i=70 5^ , position angle PA=-50 4^ , and h/<0.14 , which we associate with the dust sublimation region.

NGC 1068 is often regarded as an archetypical Seyfert 2 galaxy e.g. bland-hawthorn1997 . In particular, observations of NGC 1068 played an important role in the early development of unified models of active galactic nuclei (AGN).

sources.summary: The paper models NGC1068 as a nearest Sy2 galaxy whose ALMA torus velocity map reflects global rotation, clumpy structure, orientation effects, and an SMBH mass estimate.

quotes.quote: Recent ALMA observations have resolved the obscuring torus in the nearest Sy2 galaxy, NGC1068, in the millimeter band. These observations have confirmed the presence of a geometrically thick torus with an orbital motion of its matter and the velocity distribution which can reflect the clumpy structure.
The self-gravity of the torus induces the velocity distribution of clouds with a global orbital motion which mimics the ALMA data for NGC1068.

As it was mentioned above, the ALMA observations of the central region of NGC1068 allow to understand the dynamics of the matter in the obscuring torus. The mean velocity distribution of the ALMA data (Fig. fig:vel_map ; left) clearly shows the global rotation of the matter (colored with red and blue) as well as some inhomogeneous spots indicating that the torus has a clumpy structure.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 1068 as an IceCube-hotspot-associated source of interest, but finds it undetected by HAWC with steep 3FHL spectrum and strong upper limits.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068 has become a source of interest due to its coincidence with a hotspot in the IceCube all-sky map 2020PhRvL.124e1103A . While two other starburst galaxies, NGC 253 and M82, have been detected in the VHE range as faint sources, with fluxes 1 of the Crab Nebula 2016CRPhy..17..585O , NGC 1068 remains undetected and an unlikely candidate for HAWC detection. It is a weak 5.3 detection in the 3FHL catalog, with practically no signal above 20 GeV and a rather steep spectral index =3.8 1.0 . The HAWC data has a +1.2 excess at the location of NGC 1068, for an upper limit normalization K_ 2 = 6.46 10^ 13 TeV^ 1 cm^ 2 s^ 1 at 1 TeV. The quasi-differential HAWC limit in the common energy band, N(0.5-2.0 TeV ) 2.32 10^ 12 cm^ 2 s^ 1 , is a factor of eight lower than the respective LAT limit.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as the prototype Seyfert-2 comparison, emphasizing dust-scattered optical polarization, high intrinsic polarization, and geometry perpendicular to the radio morphology.

quotes.quote: The first reports of optical polarization in Seyfert nuclei can be dated back to the 1960s (e.g. Visvanathan Oke 1968; Walker 1968), especially in NGC 1068. Angel et al. (1976) then conclude that the significant optical polarization ( P 5 ) in NGC 1068 is originated from scattering of nuclear light by dust grains. After subtracting the unpolarized starlight from the host galaxy, a very high degree of polarization of 16 in NGC 1068 is revealed for both the continuum and broad emission lines perpendicular to the nuclear symmetry axis determined by the radio morphology. Similar to NGC 1068, high degree of polarization at perpendicular to the radio structure is found to be universal in Seyfert-2s (Antonucci 1983). A schematic cartoon of typical type-2s like NGC 1068 is displayed in Figure 8.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1068 as a nearby type 2 Seyfert, using torus SED parameters and resolved infrared imaging to study its dusty AGN torus.

quotes.quote: Here we use \ simulations to model the nearby ( D = 14.4 Mpc) and bright ( L_ bol = 0.15-1.5 10^ 45 erg s^ 1 ) AGN NGC 1068. Fitting the well-populated SED hereafter LR18 Lopez-Rodriguez+2018 with models generated by \ Nenkova+2008a, Nenkova+2008b provides geometrical parameters of the dust distribution.

The type 2 Seyfert galaxy NGC 1068 has been extensively studied in the IR using single-dish e.g., Mason+2006, RamosAlmeida+2011, Alonso-Herrero+2011, Lira+2013, Ichikawa+2015 and interferometric e.g., Jaffe+2004, Raban+2009, Burtscher+2013, Lopez-Gonzaga+2014 observations. ALMA submillimeter observations have recently resolved the molecular torus in NGC 1068 and constrained its diameter to 12 pc using the dust continuum at 432 \ and CO J=6-5 Garcia-Burillo+2016 , and then found a size 13 4 pc in the HCN J=2-1 transition and 12 5 pc for HCO ^ + J=3-2 Imanishi+2018 . The torus is found to be inclined, and lopsided, at a position angle of its mid-plane about 100--110 \ east of north.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068 as a nearby Seyfert galaxy with hinted neutrino emission, notes its steady ray behavior and possible TeV neutrinos from an AGN outflow, and finds no variability.

quotes.quote: Recent studies have found hints of neutrino emission from blazar TXS 0506+056 as well as the Seyfert galaxy NGC 1068. In this era of multimessenger astronomy, IceCube can provide valuable insight into the sources of cosmic rays and high-energy gamma rays as demonstrated by the follow-up of alert IC170922A and its association with the blazar TXS 0506+056. Detection of astrophysical neutrinos from known gamma-ray emitters can be the "smoking gun" evidence for a hadronic component in their ray spectrum. The two objects of interest, a blazar (TXS 0506+056) and a nearby Seyfert galaxy (NGC 1068), show differing features in their ray spectrum. NGC 1068 has shown no signs of ray variability while TeV neutrinos can be expected from an AGN outflow model .
