Using 7.5 years of track-like events used by the IceCube realtime alert system we test 4 sources for time-variability, namely: NGC 1068, TXS 0506+056, PKS 1424+240, and GB6 J1542+6129. We find all 4 sources to be consistent with the steady hypothesis.

sources.summary: NGC 1068 is described as a nearby, archetypal Seyfert galaxy whose featureless continuum peaks north of the nucleus, with intermediate-age stellar populations and rotating stellar kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068 This galaxy is the brightest and thus most studied Seyfert galaxy, located at a distance of only 14.4 Mpc Crenshaw&Kraemer00 . It is classified as Seyfert 1.9 , with an SAb Hubble classification. Despite being considered the archetypal Seyfert 2 galaxy, its properties are far from standard. We detected a significant contribution of a featureless continuum peaking 1 0 north of the nucleus. Lastly, the peak velocity of this galaxy is well explained by a disk in rotation.

sources.summary: NGC 1068 is described as a radio galaxy and likely TeV-PeV neutrino emitter; the paper's 3FHL analysis recovers its known excess and uses it as a reliability check.

quotes.quote: The two most likely TeV -- PeV neutrino emmiters are TXS 0506+056 and NGC 1068 .
In a time-integrated analysis using 10 years of IceCube data, radio galaxy NGC 1068 is found to be a potential neutrino point source with a significance greater than 2.9 (global significance) .

After examination, we find that the source is NGC 1068. The presence of a possible neutrino excess in the direction of NGC 1068 has already been reported by the IceCube collaboration . The result of NGC 1068 therefore suggest that our analysis is reliable.

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 1068 as having unreliable narrow-band mm spectral-index measurements and instead adopts an index from prior modeling, while noting possible 100 GHz free-free dominance.

quotes.quote: However, NGC 1194 and NGC 1068, observed with a narrower frequency coverage ( < 4 GHz), deviate from the relation. In contrast, as previously mentioned, the measurements for NGC 1194 and NGC 1068 would not be so reliable. The index derived for NGC 1068 is 2.7 1.1 , and this negative slope is inconsistent with a positive slope found from a SED analysis of . Thus, for NGC 1068, we adopt _ mm = 1.3 , inferred from the modeling of , and ^ e _ mm = 0.3 calculated by combining ^ e,stat _ mm predicted from the relation with S^ peak _ , mm / _ mm and the systematic error.
For example, reported that the nuclear 100 GHz emission of NGC 1068 is dominated by free-free emission Gal04 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies three luminous nuclear radio regions in NGC 1068, links them to known Seyfert activity, and classifies the emission as AGN and radio jets.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068 : This well-studied isolated Seyfert 2 galaxy e.g. yuan10 is the nearest LIRG in our sample. At both 15 and 33 GHz, three luminous nuclear regions are identified with Astrodendro . These regions are aligned almost linearly along the N-S direction, with the central region being the brightest at both 15 and 33 GHz. All three regions have been previously identified as radio jets associated with a highly obscured AGN, which is likely located within the southern region e.g. gallimore96, gallimore04 . Therefore we have classified the southern region as ``AGN", and the other two as ``Jet".

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NuSTAR and XMM-Newton observations of the Compton-thick Seyfert 2 NGC 1068, measuring coronal temperature behavior and finding hard-band brightening without soft-band variability.

quotes.quote: We present the timing and spectral analysis of the Compton Thick Seyfert 2 active galactic nuclei NGC 1068 observed using NuSTAR and XMM-Newton . In this work for the first time we calculated the coronal temperature ( kT_ e ) of the source and checked for its variation between the epochs if any. The data analysed in this work comprised of (a) eight epochs of observations with NuSTAR carried out during the period December 2012 to November 2017, and, (b) six epochs of observations with XMM-Newton carried out during July 2000 to February 2015. From timing analysis of the NuSTAR observations, we found the source not to show any variations in the soft band. However, on examination of the flux at energies beyond 20 keV, during August 2014 and August 2017 the source was brighter by about 20 and 30 respectively compared to the mean flux of the three 2012 NuSTAR observations as in agreement with earlier results in literature.

sources.summary: The paper connects NGC 1068’s IceCube neutrino excess to obscured AGN regions near its supermassive black hole, arguing for hidden neutrino production within roughly 30-100 Schwarzschild radii.

quotes.quote: Recently, the IceCube Collaboration reported an excess of 79 events associated with a nearby spiral galaxy known as M77 or NGC 1068  IceCube2022NGC1068 . The reported significance is 4.2 , which was found in a search defined a priori , using a list of the catalog of sources observed at gamma rays and/or other wavelengths, and the result strengthens the previous report of a 2.9 excess  IceCube:2019cia . NGC 1068 is known to be a prototypical Seyfert II galaxy, which is a type of active galactic nucleus (AGN), as well as one of the starburst galaxies. A supermassive black hole (SMBH) at the center and its surroundings are highly obscured by thick gas and dust  e.g., GarciaBurillo+16ALMA,GamezRosas+22 , while X-ray studies have suggested that NGC 1068 is among the brightest AGNs in intrinsic X-rays  Bauer:2014rla,Marinucci:2015fqo,Ricci:2017dhj . NGC 1068 has been considered as a promising neutrino source in light of both AGN  Murase:2019vdl,Kheirandish:2021wkm,Anchordoqui:2021vms,Inoue:2022yak and starburst  Yoast-Hull:2013qfa,Murase:2016gly,Lamastra:2016axo activities.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 1068 as a Type-2 Seyfert with high-energy neutrino detection, compares its IceCube-fit gyrofactor, and says attenuation makes it intrinsically brighter than NGC 4151.

quotes.quote: The importance of these new gamma-ray sources is enhanced by the reported detection of high-energy neutrinos from a Type-2 Seyfert galaxy NGC 1068. We also note that based on a comparison with the IceCube data for NGC 1068 Inoue2020ApJ...891L..33I, IceCube2022Sci...378..538I , a gyrofactor of around _g 1000 is preferred for NGC 1068 to yield a soft neutrino spectrum at TeV.

Despite NGC 4151 being the X-ray brightest Seyfert, IceCube has already detected NGC 1068 at a 4.2 level. As discussed in Inoue2020ApJ...891L..33I , this discrepancy can be attributed to the X-ray attenuation effect and the penetrating power of neutrinos. When we adjust for the X-ray attenuation effect, NGC 1068 emerges as the intrinsically brightest Seyfert, outshining NGC 4151 by a factor of approximately 3.6 .

sources.summary: The review discusses NGC 1068 as a radio-quiet Seyfert with jet-related cosmic-ray activity and IceCube neutrino evidence exceeding its GeV gamma-ray flux.

quotes.quote: A recent ALMA observation toward NGC 1068 (a radio-quiet Seyfert galaxy) unveiled CR production activity, likely at the `head' of the jet, 670 pc away from the supermassive black hole (SMBH) Michiyama2022ApJ...936L...1M . The CR power in the jet is estimated to exceed that expected from activity directly associated recent star formation episodes in this galaxy.

This scenario is supported by the recent IceCube observations, which identified a neutrino hot-spot (with a significance level of 4.2 ) located in the direction of NGC 1068 IceCube2022Sci...378..538I , which is a known ray emitter Lenain2010,3FHL,4FGL .

IceCube observations of NGC 1068 imply that the neutrino flux is greater than the GeV ray flux IceCube2019_NGC1068, IceCube2022Sci...378..538I . This would require a suppression of neutral pion production, which is not easy to achieve.

sources.summary: The paper compares two VLBA epochs for NGC 1068, measuring sub-relativistic extranuclear radio-knot motions and arguing the radio structures are shock byproducts of AGN winds interacting with host gas.

quotes.quote: We present recent Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) 5 GHz radio observations of the nearby, luminous Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068 for comparison to similar VLBA observations made on 1997 April 26. By cross-correlating the positions of emitting regions across both epochs, we find that spatially-resolved extra-nuclear radio knots in this system have sub-relativistic transverse speeds (v 0.1c). We discuss sources of the observed knots and how the radio emission relates to additional phases of gas in the central 150 pcs of this system. We suggest that the most likely explanation for the observed emission is synchrotron radiation formed by shocked host media via interactions between AGN winds and the host environment.

In concert, this intertwined emission across the electromagnetic spectrum suggests a scenario where the observed radio knots in NGC 1068 form within the optical NLR, as traced by the observed [ O 3 ] emission, which represents the intersection between AGN ionization and host galaxy disk material.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 1068 as a nearby type-II Seyfert, measuring its central 7 pc radio spectrum and interpreting it as jet-base, dusty-torus, and compact-corona emission.

quotes.quote: We analyze all the available Atacama Large Millimeter / submillimeter Array archival data of the nearby Type-II Seyfert galaxy NGC 1068, including new 100 GHz data with the angular resolution of 0 05, which was not included in previous continuum spectral analysis. By combining with the literature data based on the Very Large Array, we investigate the broadband radio continuum spectrum of the central 7 pc region of NGC 1068. We found that the flux density is between 10--20 mJy at 5--700 GHz. Due to the inability of the model in previous studies to account for the newly added 100 GHz data point, we proceeded to update the models and make the necessary adjustments to the parameters. One possible interpretation of this broadband radio spectrum is a combination of emission from the jet base, the dusty torus, and the compact X-raying corona with the magnetic field strength of 20 G on scales of 30 Schwarzschild radii from the central black hole.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1068 as an AGN with prior IceCube neutrino evidence and as one of the two highest-significance hard-X-ray AGN point-source candidates.

quotes.quote: There exists evidence of possible neutrino emission from individual AGN, i.e., TXS 0506+056 and NGC 1068 .

From the point source search, the top two sources that result in the highest levels of significance are NGC 1068 and NGC 4151. A previous analysis reported the observation on NGC 1068 and the results of this analysis are compatible with the evidence previously found. Figure fig:ra_dec_scans shows the local probability around the location of the two most significant sources obtained from the point source search.
NGC 1068 has been reported in a previous IceCube study and hence, not considered in the final estimation of the significance evaluation.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068 as a Seyfert II neutrino source, refitting its public IceCube data to measure flux parameters compatible with the published result.

quotes.quote: It has been developed within the IceCube collaboration to perform frequentist statistical data analyses for point-like neutrino source searches, including the one that resulted in a 4.2 evidence for neutrino emission from the Seyfert II galaxy NGC 1068 .
For NGC 1068 specifically, we measure the neutrino flux and compare the result to the one reported in Ref. .

We measured the neutrino flux (E_ )= _ 1TeV (E_ / 1TeV )^ from NGC 1068 with the public data analysis presented in this work and find the best-fit values for the flux parameters to be n_s=60.1 , and =3.2 . The observed mean number of signal events n_s can be converted into a neutrino flux normalization by integrating the effective area at the source location multiplied by the power-law energy flux over the neutrino energy range and the observation time period. We obtain _ 1TeV =3.3 10^ 11 TeV^ 1 cm^ 2 s^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper describes diffuse 266 GHz emission around NGC 1068's nucleus and confirms an X-shaped structure previously interpreted as a bicone-like ionized region.

quotes.quote: 11: NGC 1068 Our 266 GHz image at a resolution of 0 02 shows diffuse emission around the nucleus ( a in the corresponding panel of Figure fig:img4notes , hereafter italic characters are used to indicate such components in the same way) and also three other diffuse components 0 3 away at a position angle (PA) The PA is defined so that 0 \ is the north, and that increases counter-clockwise. of 10 \ ( b ), 0 6 away at a PA of 20 \ ( ), and 0 2 away at a PA of 180 \ ( d ). A high-resolution continuum image obtained at almost the same resolution and frequency (i.e., 0 02 and 257 GHz) was presented by . Their image revealed an X-shaped structure with a PA of 20 \ (blue dotted arrow) around the nucleus, and they interpreted this as being due to edge-brightening of a bicone-like ionized region. The X-shaped structure is clearly seen also in our image.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068 as a Seyfert 2 with a jet-driven molecular outflow, compares CN optical-depth measurements, and finds a high CN bright/faint ratio indicating optically thin emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068, a nearby Seyfert 2 galaxy, has also been shown to have an increasing CN/CO ratio near the AGN and the jet-driven molecular outflow Saito2022b .

The measured (CN bright)/(CN faint) ratios in Mrk 231 are higher than the average values we find for the ULIRGs in our sample, suggesting this galaxy has more optically thin CN emission. used ALMA to observe CN (1-0) in NGC 1068 and found a (CN bright)/(CN faint) ratio of 1.7 and an optical depth of 0.7 . Nakajima2018 estimated an optical depth of 1.42 in NGC 1068. In contrast, we measure a (CN bright)/(CN faint) ratio of 2.44 in NGC 1068, suggesting optically thin emission.
NGC 1068 is a Seyfert 2 AGN with a jet-driven molecular outflow Saito2022a, Saito2022b .

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 1068/M 77 as a Seyfert/star-forming galaxy associated with an IceCube neutrino excess, while noting catalog-selection and soft-spectrum caveats.

quotes.quote: Neutrinos from Seyfert galaxies Another isolated individual neutrino source was associated with the M 77 galaxy, aka NGC 1068. This galaxy combines signs of nuclear activity (Seyfert type 2) and intense star formation. In , the IceCube collaboration rejects the hypothesis of the absence of neutrino association with this galaxy at a statistical significance of 4.2 . Interpretation of this value is not straightforward because of the fact that, by itself, the significance of a neutrino hot spot in the direction close to NGC 1068 in the full-sky scan is 2.0 .

In the analysis of events from NGC 1068, the value of the power-law index of 3.2 0.2 was obtained , that is, the astrophysical origin of the neutrino exces is deduced mainly from the concentration of their arrival directions in a small region of the sky, not from their high energies.

sources.summary: The review presents NGC 1068 as an early spiral nebula whose continuous spectrum, absorption features, bright emission lines, compact nitrogen emission, and broad lines became important for AGN studies.

quotes.quote: In addition, observations of the ``spiral nebula” NGC 1068 showed that in addition to a continuous spectrum with absorption lines, bright emission lines, like those of ordinary gaseous nebulae, are clearly visible. Then these lines did not seem such an important discovery, but later they played a very important role in the study of AGN. A few years later, this structure of the NGC 1068 spectrum was confirmed by Vesto Slipher (1875 -- 1969), and the absorption lines were identified with Fraunhofer lines. Slipher's further observations showed that the nebular nitrogen lines were emitted in a more compact region than the hydrogen lines. But the most important discovery was the unprecedented width of the emission lines.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as the benchmark nearby Seyfert neutrino source, linking IceCube detections, gamma-ray opacity, compact coronal regions, and sub-GeV excess comparisons.

quotes.quote: The existence of hidden neutrino sources has further been supported by the recent IceCube observation of neutrino signals from the nearby Seyfert galaxy, NGC 1068 IceCube:2019cia,IceCube2022NGC1068 . The GeV-TeV ray fluxes seen by LAT Fermi-LAT:2019yla,Ajello:2023hkh and MAGIC upper limits MAGIC:2019fvw are much lower than the TeV neutrino flux measured with IceCube. The region has to be compact for the system to be opaque to GeV rays Murase:2022dog , as expected in disk-corona models of active galactic nuclei (AGNs) Murase:2019vdl,Kheirandish:2021wkm .

Multimessenger data from NGC 1068 suggest that high-energy neutrinos from Seyfert galaxies are likely to originate from compact regions with a size of R 30-100 R_S Murase:2022dog , and X-ray emitting coronae or possible shock regions, located at the vicinity of SMBHs, are the most plausible site of the neutrino production Murase:2019vdl,Inoue:2019yfs,Eichmann:2022lxh,Inoue:2022yak . That said, this signal is very interesting because a similar excess flux over the pionic ray component was reported for NGC 1068 Ajello:2023hkh .

sources.summary: The paper reports no significant AME in NGC 1068, interprets the non-detection in the context of strong AGN environments, and notes Sy2-like infrared colors with little ultra-small dust.

quotes.quote: Combining with the ancillary data, we investigated the radio-IR spectral energy distribution (SED), while searching for the AME signals in five galaxies. The AME in NGC 2903 was well detected at a significant confidence level, whereas that in NGC 2146 and M82 was marginal. NGC 1068 and Arp 299 indicated no significant hints, and we provided the upper limits for the AME.

However, we did not observe AME in strong AGNs such as NGC 1068 and Arp 299, indicating that AME favored non- or rather weak-AGN hosts. This may imply that ultra-small dust grains are easily destroyed by strong AGNs.

NGC 1068, wherein no AME was observed, shared similar ranges with AME-detected targets. This galaxy is a Sy2 type Kawakatu_2004 wherein the very vicinity of the central AGN is obscured, and hence possibly found with similar IR colors as the other AME detections although it has no or a very small fraction of ultra-small dust grains.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1068 as a Seyfert galaxy and confirmed IceCube neutrino source whose emission has 4.2 sigma significance but relatively low Fermi-LAT gamma-ray flux.

quotes.quote: The two most promising sources of TeV-PeV neutrinos discovered by IceCube are TXS 0506+056 and NGC 1068 . TXS 0506+056 is found to be temporally and spatially correlated with a 300 TeV IceCube neutrino event IC-170922A ; furthermore, a neutrino flare from the direction of TXS 0506+056 was detected between 2014 and 2015 . In a time-integrated analysis of searching for neutrino point sources using IceCube data between 2011 and 2020, neutrino emission from the Seyfert galaxy NGC 1068 is found with a global significance of 4.2 .

For instance, the neutrino flare of TXS 0506+056 occurred during a low gamma-ray state , and another confirmed neutrino source NGC1068 exhibits relatively low gamma-ray flux in the Fermi-LAT observations .

By examining the associated sources in 3FHL, we find that the relatively high-confidence correlation is mainly contributed by three already known neutrino sources/source candidates: TXS 0506+056, NGC 1068, and PKS 1424+240.

sources.summary: The paper presents diffraction-limited mid-infrared imaging of NGC 1068, resolving a bright elongated core, patchy extended emission, and structures associated with its torus and radio/NLR geometry.

quotes.quote: We present diffraction-limited IR images at 11.2 and 20.5 m of the central 6'' 6'' region in NGC 1068, collected with the CAMIRAS instrument mounted at the f/36 IR focus of the CFHT/Hawaii 3.6m telescope and at the f/35 IR focus of the ESO/La Silla 3.6m telescope, respectively. After deconvolution, the achieved resolution ( 0.6'' ) reveals a prominent central core emitting about 95 of the total flux at these wavelengths, as well as extended emission, to the South-West (PA = 210 ) and to the North-East (PA = 35 ), broken into patchy components which are particularly conspicuous at 20.5 m and can be isolated as individual clouds. The central core shows an East-West FWHM of 0.6'' (hence unresolved) and a North-South FWHM of 0.9'' corresponding to a resolved full size extension of 100 pc.

Such an elongated shape is in agreement with model predictions of a dusty/molecular torus surrounding the central engine in NGC 1068, observed under an inclination angle of around 65 . Considering that the core at 11.2 and 20.5 m is coincident with the core also seen at 2.2, 3.5 and 4.8 m and that this features the location of the central engine (also the radio source S1), the extended mid-IR emission is found to follow closely the radio jet--like structure in both the North-East and South-West quadrants.

sources.summary: The paper maps H2 emission around NGC 1068's AGN, finding east-west molecular knots, complex line profiles, and a velocity difference implying a large enclosed central mass.

quotes.quote: We present results about the distribution and kinematics of the molecular environment of the AGN in NGC 1068, over a 1.5'' 3.5'' region around the central engine in NGC 1068, derived from H _ 2 line emission detected with ISAAC at VLT/ANTU on ESO/Paranal. The H _ 2 emitting molecular gas is found to be distributed along the East-West direction and with two main peak emission (knots) located at a distance of about 70 pc from the central engine. The eastern H _ 2 knot is more intense than the western one. The line profiles mapped across the entire 1.5'' 3.5'' region, at a spatial resolution of 0.3'' 0.45'' , appear to be quite complex with either a blue or red wing. At first order, we find a velocity difference of 140 km/s between the two knots; if interpreted as quasi-keplerian velocity, this implies a central enclosed mass of 10^8 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1068 as the closest Seyfert 2, with MIR emission dominated by AGN-heated hot dust and FIR emission linked to surrounding star formation.

quotes.quote: The flat FIR over MIR ratio for NGC 1068 is typical of the hot dust continuum from AGNs. NGC 1068 is the closest Seyfert 2 ( L_ IR = 1.7 10^ 11 L_ ). On one hand, its MIR spectrum is completely dominated by a continuum due to the hot dust heated by the central AGN, which produces about 75 of the MIR luminosity (Le Floc'h et al. 2001). On the other hand, the bulk of its FIR luminosity originates from the diffuse region surrounding the nucleus as shown by the SCUBA image at 450 m, which is associated with star formation (Le Floc'h et al. 2001). As a result, the FIR over MIR luminosity ratio is much lower for NGC 1068 than for starbursts (see Fig. FIG:sedir ).

sources.summary: The paper presents Chandra LETGS spectra of NGC 1068, finding photoionized soft X-ray emission in nuclear and secondary regions, lower secondary-region columns, and density structure in the ionization cone.

quotes.quote: Using the combined spectral and spatial resolving power of the Low Energy Transmission Grating (LETGS) on board Chandra , we obtain separate spectra from the bright central source of NGC 1068 (Primary region), and from a fainter bright spot 4 \ to the NE (Secondary region). Both spectra are dominated by discrete line emission from H- and He-like ions of C through S, and from Fe L-shell ions, but also include narrow radiative recombination continua (RRC), indicating that most of the observed soft X-ray emission arises in low-temperature ( kT_ e few eV) photoionized plasma. We confirm the conclusions of Kinkhabwala et al. ( ), based on XMM-Newton Reflection Grating Spectrometer (RGS) observations, that the entire nuclear spectrum can be explained by recombination/radiative cascade following photoionization, and radiative decay following photoexcitation, with no evidence for the presence of hot, collisionally ionized plasma.

In addition, we show that this same model also provides an excellent fit to the spectrum of the Secondary region, albeit with radial column densities roughly a factor of three lower, as would be expected given its distance from the source of the ionizing continuum. The remarkable overlap and kinematical agreement of the optical and X-ray line emission, coupled with the need for a distribution of ionization parameter to explain the X-ray spectra, collectively imply the presence of a distribution of densities (over a few orders of magnitude) at each radius in the ionization cone.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1068 as a Seyfert spiral with an inner near-infrared bar, gas streaming, complex AGN-affected kinematics, and SAURON maps used for bar modeling.

quotes.quote: One of the best evidence for gas streaming towards the centre of a nearby galaxy hosting a central dark mass is given by the well-studied Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068. Indeed, a near-infrared bar is well visible in the central 20 arcseconds of this spiral galaxy, and streaming motions have been uncovered in the gas kinematics (see e.g. Schinnerer et al. 2000 and references therein). Note that an outer oval structure has been advocated by Schinnerer et al. to be a large-scale (weak) primary bar, implying that the inner bar is in fact a secondary bar. Although this is one of the best studies active galactic nucleus, it is surprising to realize how little we know about the gravitational potential in the central few kpc of this galaxy. We have recently obtained integral field spectrography of this inner region using SAURON at the WHT.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1068’s dusty torus, arguing cloud collisions accelerate relativistic electrons that explain S_1 radio emission and predict a detectable GeV bump.

quotes.quote: A geometrically thick dusty torus in NGC 1068 has been unambiguously resolved by an infrared interferometry telescope. This implies clouds composing the dusty torus are undergoing supersonic collisions with each other.

The radio emission from the Component S_1 in NGC 1068 can be explained by the present model. It is predicted that there is a GeV bump in NGC 1068, which is caused by inverse Compton scattering off the infrared photons inside dusty torus. This GeV component can be detected by GLAST .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1068 as a nearby Seyfert II galaxy whose high-resolution data constrain torus parameters; its hottest dust reaches sublimation temperature in the fitted model.

quotes.quote: Our fit of special objects for which high angular resolution observations ( 0.3 ) are available indicates that the hottest dust in NGC 1068 reaches the sublimation temperature while the maximum dust temperature in the low-luminosity AGN Circinus falls short of 1000 K.

NGC 1068 NGC 1068 is a nearby Seyfert II galaxy at a distance of 14.4 Mpc Galliano_03 . Therefore, it has been studied extensively and we were able to find at least some of the needed simulation parameters in literature. The procedure described above led to the parameters summarised in Table tab:param_ngc1068 . The inclination angle of i=85 was chosen in agreement with the tilt of the outflow cone of ionised gas reported by Crenshaw_00 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068 as a nearby luminous Seyfert 2 whose radio continuum is dominated by AGN emission, requiring high-resolution RC-CO analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068 . This is a nearby and luminous Seyfert 2 galaxy, which has been extensively studied from radio wavelengths to X-rays. The nuclear emission is clearly dominated by the radio source related to the AGN. For the study of the point-by-point correlation we do not use the low resolution data because the source is unresolved. We show only the point-by-point correlation and the radial profiles measured in the 6 resolution images. Recently, found that the infrared emission is due to both an AGN component and to the starburst component in the circum-nuclear ring of 3 kpc in size.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC1068 as a Seyfert 2 template with FIR lines, OH emission from dense nuclear clouds, an active nucleus, and a starburst for SPICA detectability estimates.

quotes.quote: The LWS spectrum of the prototype Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC1068, beside the expected ionic fine structure emission lines, shows the 79, 119 and 163 m OH rotational lines in emission, not in absorption as in every other galaxy yet observed. Modeling the OH lines suggests the emitting gas lies in small (0.1pc) and dense clouds ( 10^4 cm^ 3 ) most probably in the nuclear region and is potentially a signature of the torus (Spinoglio et al 2005).

The ISO spectrometers detected in the bright local Seyfert type 2 galaxy, NGC1068, many of the lines plotted in Fig.2 at flux levels of 5-200 10 ^ 16 W m ^ 2 (Alexander et al 2000, Spinoglio et al 2005). This object contains both an active nucleus and a starburst. Considering this galaxy as a template object, we computed the line intensities expected at redshifts ranging from 0.1 to 5.

sources.summary: The paper uses VISIR speckle imaging and MIDI comparison to resolve NGC 1068's Seyfert nucleus, separating compact torus emission from extended ionization-cone dust.

quotes.quote: We present a speckle analysis of the active galactic nucleus (AGN) inside the archetype Seyfert type 2 galaxy NGC 1068. The interferometric processing allows to push the resolution far below the diffraction limit of a 8 m telescope in the N band and to trace two main contributions to the mid-IR flux inside the nucleus. The confrontation with VLT/MIDI (the Mid-InfrareD Interferometer) data points helps to establish the link between dust in the vicinity of the central engine and inside the ionisation cone to get a multi-scale picture of mid-IR sources emitting in the nucleus of NGC 1068.

Both data sets are well reproduced by a model of two components entering in the field of view of MIDI: a compact one ( 20 mas) associated to the dusty layer , and an extended one ( < 400 mas) associated with heated dust in the ionisation cone Poncelet_2006b . This amount of dust of the ionisation cone entering in the field of view of MIDI has then to be taken into account during the modelling of interferometric data.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC1068 as a local Seyfert 2 template with both an active nucleus and starburst, emphasizing its ISO-detected infrared fine-structure and OH emission lines.

quotes.quote: For simplicity, and to cover as many transitions possible, we use a local object which contains both an active nucleus and a starburst, the bright prototype Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC1068. The ISO spectrometers detected in this galaxy many of the lines plotted in Fig. 2 at flux levels of 5-200 10 ^ 16 W m ^ 2 (Alexander et al. ; Spinoglio et al. et al. ).

Notably, the LWS spectrum of the prototype Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068, beside the expected ionic fine structure emission lines, shows the 79, 119 and 163 m OH rotational lines in emission, not in absorption as in every other galaxy yet observed. Modeling the three FIR lines of OH suggests the gas lies in small (0.1pc) and dense clouds ( 10^4 cm^ 3 ) in the nuclear region (potentially a signature of the torus) with a minor contribution from the circumnuclear starburst ring at 3kpc (Spinoglio et al. ).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as a clear AGN chemistry example, describing its circumnuclear disk as an X-ray dominated region with enhanced HCN and SiO emission.

quotes.quote: The 200 pc-radius CND of NGC 1068 is viewed as the first clear example of a giant X-ray dominated region (XDR) leading to enhanced abundances of some molecular species like HCN (Usero \ ). Recently a high-resolution PdBI map has shown that strong SiO emission comes from the CND of NGC 1068 (Garc a-Burillo \ 2008, in prep.). The enhancement of SiO in this case cannot be attributed to the action of ongoing star formation as there is negative evidence of a recent starburst in the CND of NGC 1068 (Davies \ ). Alternatively, the processing of 10 dust grains by X-rays, as a mechanism to enhance silicon chemistry in gas phase, would explain the large SiO abundances of the CND.

sources.summary: The review uses NGC 1068 as a Seyfert 2 example whose face-on host contains an edge-on torus and AGN-scale molecular environment.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068, the archetype Seyfert 2 galaxy and one of the most studied active nuclei, is a revealing example. While the galaxy is oriented roughly face-on, the torus is edge-on to within 5 . The nearly edge-on orientation of the torus and accretion disk are indicated by the geometry and kinematics of both water maser Greenhill97, Gallimore01 and narrow-line emission Crenshaw00 . Some other interesting information about the AGN immediate surroundings in this source comes from molecular line observations.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as observational evidence for AGN torus structure, describing scattered Seyfert X-ray emission and resolved dusty nuclear components with revised temperature estimates.

quotes.quote: For example, the properties of the X-ray emission in the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068 corresponds to the scattered emission expected from warm absorbers in Seyfert 1 galaxies Kinkhabwala02 .

Mid-infrared high spatial resolution studies of the nucleus of NGC 1068 using the Very Large Telescope Interferometer have resolved a dusty structure which is 2.1 pc thick and 3.4 pc in diameter JaffeNATUR . Observations support a multi-temperature model: the temperature of the warm component was established to be 300 K and inside of it a second, compact and hot ( > 800K) component has been found. Further studies of NGC 1068 systematically reduced estimates of the temperatures of different components PonceletPerrin06 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1068 as an archetypal Seyfert 2 whose face-on host contains an edge-on AGN torus, maser disk, and larger molecular structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068 is the archetype Seyfert 2 galaxy and one of the most studied active nuclei. While the galaxy is oriented roughly face-on, its AGN torus is edge-on to within 5 , as indicated by the geometry and kinematics of both water maser Greenhill97, Gallimore01 and narrow-line emission Crenshaw00 . The masers trace the disk to \ 1pc, inside the torus which is traced by IR to \ 2--3 pc Jaffe04 . A nearly edge-on molecular structure is traced to distances much larger than the torus outer edge.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as a nearby Seyfert 2 comparison, describing its narrow-line-region outflow kinematics and why similar unresolved spectra would lack a large velocity offset.

quotes.quote: Using the Hubble Space Telescope Imaging Spectrograph (STIS), obtained spatially resolved spectroscopy of outflowing gas in the NLR of the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068. The outflowing clouds produce a complicated velocity structure in the emission lines, with multiple emission components having velocity offsets (both blueshifts and redshifts) up to several hundred km s ^ 1 . This velocity structure is confined to the inner 100 pc of the emitting region; outside of this, the outflow velocity drops and reaches zero at 300 pc.

To get a rough approximation of what the spectrum of a system like NGC 1068 would look like in DEEP2, we can coadd the neighboring STIS spectra of NGC 1068 from ; this yields a broadened emission line with a blue wing but no large overall offset in the velocity of its peak.

sources.summary: NGC 1068 is presented as a nearby LIRG Seyfert 2 with a circumnuclear starburst ring; the paper observes both its AGN and ring positions.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068 is a nearby luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG) prototype of a Seyfert 2 nucleus Anto85 . The AGN is enclosed by a circumnuclear starburst ring with 14'' ( 1 kpc) radius Myers87,Schinne00 . Thus, we have observed two positions in NGC 1068. One towards the central AGN and an offset position 0'',-14'' towards a peak of emission within the circumnuclear ring, hereafter NGC 1068 ^* , mostly tracing the starburst.

sources.summary: The paper notes that NGC 1068 has a weak, extinction-affected broad-line region and a water-maser SMBH mass estimate, making BLR-based mass estimation unsuitable.

quotes.quote: Only a minority of the galaxies in the sample (NGC 1068, NGC 4051 and NGC 5033) are known to have detected BLRs. However, the BLRs of NGC 1068 and NGC 5033 (both are Seyfert 1.9) are extremely weak and are therefore likely to suffer from optical extinction. Thus, estimates using the BLR are not possible for these two AGNs.

Indeed, in our sample there are currently only two AGNs (NGC 1068 and NGC 4945) with estimations from the mapping of water maser spots ( 1.6 10^7 and 1.1 10^6 , respectively) and one AGN (NGC 5128) with a spatially resolved gas dynamical estimate ( 2.4 10^8 ).

sources.summary: The paper models X-ray polarisation from NGC 1068's active nucleus, focusing on misaligned ionised outflows and the obscuring torus and arguing future polarimetry can constrain that geometry.

quotes.quote: We model the expected X-ray polarisation induced by complex reprocessing in the active nucleus of the Seyfert-2 galaxy NGC 1068. Recent analysis of infrared interferometry observations suggests that the ionised outflows ejected by the central engine are not aligned with the symmetry axis of the obscuring torus. This conclusion was obtained by extrapolating the apparent orientation of the narrow line region to the inner parts of the ionisation cones. We show that future measurements of the soft X-ray polarisation vector unambiguously determine the orientation of the ionisation cones.

sources.summary: The paper reports weaker dispersion for NGC1068, says the linear method should recover the dominant mass component, and finds disk mass similar to or higher than the central black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: For NGC4258 and NGC1068, the dispersion is much weaker with 0.2-0.3 . For these two objects, the linear approach should give the mass of the most massive component quite correctly (i.e. see Tab. tab:synthetic ).

In contrast, results obtained through the linear approach are satisfactory for UGC3786, NGC3393, and NGC1068 where disk mass is similar to, or higher than the mass of the central black hole. For these objects, the massive disk should be prone to instabilities.

sources.summary: NGC 1068 is presented as the archetypal Seyfert 2 revealing a hidden BLR in polarized light, with parsec-scale infrared mapping showing two dust components.

quotes.quote: A strong observational evidence of a unification between type 2 and type 1 Seyfert nuclei has been the discovery of broad optical lines in the polarized spectrum of the archetypal Seyfert 2, NGC 1068, obtained by antonucci85 . This finding revealed the presence of a Broad Line Region (BLR) in this Seyfert 2 nucleus, which is hidden to our line of sight, but whose light is scattered in our direction from material (in the case of NGC 1068, likely free electrons in ionized gas) distributed on scales larger than the absorber.

The possibility to effectively `image' the dusty component of the parsec-scale torus has become possible in 2004, when mid-infrared interferometry allowed jaffe04 to map for the first time the dust at parsec resolution in the Seyfert 2, NGC 1068. Their results, refined by following observations rab09 , are consistent with a two-component dust distribution: an inner (0.5 pc of thickness), rather elongated hot ( T>800 K) component, and a more extended (3-4 pc), less elongated colder ( T 300 K) component.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1068 as an archetypal Seyfert-2 galaxy with tilted ionized winds, focusing on polarization signatures from misaligned outflows and viewing angles.

quotes.quote: In the case of the archetypical Seyfert-2 galaxy NGC 1068, the AGN is observed at a rather high inclination and the ionized winds are therefore well seen from the side. A recent study by suggests that the polar winds of NGC 1068 are inclined with respect to the torus axis.

This research note focuses on the expected polarization signatures of NGC 1068 with misaligned outflows as a function of the polar and azimuthal viewing angle. We base our model on the description given in assuming that the bi-conical outflows are inclined by 18 ^ from the vertical torus axis.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as a case study showing that unconstrained CLUMPY torus fitting gives an implausible face-on geometry, while an [OIII] luminosity prior recovers a better picture.

quotes.quote: For example, the canonical Seyfert II galaxy NGC 1068, which has a well constrained SED, including a large number of IR photometric observations and a well determined MIR spectrum, is best fitted by models presenting a small value of the torus angle, this is, models with face-on orientations. This is not supported by a large body of evidence on the geometry of the central region in this object.
Figure ngc1068 shows the case study for NGC 1068. When restricting L^ bol _ model to the value implied by the observed [ OIII ] fluxes, the correct picture emerges.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1068 as a Compton-thick Seyfert with diffuse disk-like VLBI core emission, jet-like radio substructure, misaligned maser/core axes, and low radio loudness despite jet evidence.

quotes.quote: An inspection of VLBI images reveals that the majority of the detected nuclei consist of a single slightly resolved component, occasionally (in seven sources, including 1.6 GHz data) showing an elongated structure; only in the case of NGC 1068, the core is characterized by diffuse disk-like emission.

In general, the majority of the VLA nuclear flux density is resolved in jet-like complex sub-structures at VLBI resolution, as, e.g., in the Compton thick NGC 1068 (G04) and in the type 1 NGC 3227 (B12).

Only in two sources the disk-jet orientation can be guessed assuming that the disk is traced by water maser emission: the sub-pc jet in NGC 4258 is orthogonal to the disk (Cecil et al. 2000), while in NGC 1068 the core radio axis and the line traced by the water maser are slightly misaligned (G04). Interestingly, the presence of a jet/outflow structure does not correlate with the core radio-loudness parameter, as sources like NGC 1068, NGC 3079, NGC 3227 and NGC 4151 all have log R _ X < 5.5.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as a Seyfert 2 SED case, fitting its nuclear infrared data and noting a peculiar near-infrared bump and small torus-width posterior.

quotes.quote: For NGC 1068, we used the nuclear K-band flux reported by Thatte97 and we considered the H-band flux from Alonso01 as an upper limit, as it does not match the SED shape and it possibly includes some level of contamination from extended emission Prieto10 . The best-sampled SEDs, those of NGC 1068 and NGC 4151, define the wavelength grid, from 1.6 to 18.2 (see Table average_seds ). In the case of NGC 1068, all the posteriors but and Y are well-constrained when we fit J+M+N-band data (4th row). The SED of NGC 1068 is peculiar, showing a NIR bump that it is reproduced with a small torus width, more characteristic of Sy1 galaxies in general ( Ramos11 ).

sources.summary: NGC 1068 is described as a Seyfert comparison galaxy with high-J CO emission, substantial AGN contribution, and a lower mid-J CO-to-infrared ratio than star-formation-dominated LIRGs.

quotes.quote: Three are from the references listed above: M 82, an archetypical starburst; NGC 1266, a disk galaxy with possible AGN-driven molecular outflows (Alatalo et al. 2011); and NGC 1068, a well-known Seyfert galaxy with its CO line emission detected up to J = 30 (Hailey-Dunsheath et al. 2012) and f_ AGN 50 (Telesco Decher 1988).

On the other hand, both Mrk 231 and NGC 1068 have a lower R_ midCO in Fig. 4. They both have significant hot CO gas emitting at J > 10 (Gonz alez-Alfonso et al. 2014; Hailey-Dunsheath et al. 2012), likely associated with XDR (van der Werf et al. 2010; Spinoglio et al. 2012).
In the case of NGC 1068, we have L^ AGN _ IR /L_ IR 54 ( 10 ), in good agreement with an independent estimate in Telesco Decher (1988).

sources.summary: The paper reports H3+ absorption in NGC 1068, interpreting it as diffuse gas near the AGN flowing outward along the line of sight at about 1 solar mass per year.

quotes.quote: Spectra of the archetypal Type II Seyfert galaxy NGC 1068 in a narrow wavelength interval near 3.7 m have revealed a weak absorption feature due to two lines of the molecular ion H _3^+ . The observed wavelength of the feature corresponds to velocity of -70 km s ^ 1 relative to the systemic velocity of the galaxy, implying an outward flow from the nucleus along the line of sight. The absorption by H _3^+ along with the previously known broad hydrocarbon absorption at 3.4 m probably are formed in diffuse gas that is in close proximity to the continuum source, i.e. within a few tens of parsecs of the central engine. Based on that conclusion and the measured H _3^+ absorption velocity and with the assumption of a spherically symmetric wind we estimate a rate of mass outflow from the AGN of 1 M _ yr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as an early AGN reddening case, contrasting steep NLR Balmer decrements with Case B and citing Koski’s E(B-V) estimate.

quotes.quote: In the first study of the intensities of hydrogen lines in an AGN, Osterbrock+Parker65 found the Balmer decrement of the narrow-line region (NLR) of NGC 1068 to be steeper than Case B. This immediately raised the question of whether AGNs were reddened, or whether the intrinsic hydrogen line ratios were different from Case B. Osterbrock Parker assumed that there was insufficient internal extinction in NGC 1068 to explain the steep Balmer decrement and instead favoured the observed steep decrement being a consequence of high optical depths in the Balmer lines.
From the Balmer decrement of NGC 1068 Koski obtained E(B-V) = 0.52 0.04 which was consistent with Wampler's estimate of 0.5 from the [ S ii ] lines.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068 as a rejected Planck blackbox candidate: mainly Seyfert-powered with a starburst region, natural microwave components, and positive spectral curvature unlike a blackbox.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068 is mostly powered by its Seyfert nucleus, although it also has a prominent starburst region Telesco84 .

The 100 GHz luminosity of these galaxies is a combination of synchrotron emission from cosmic ray electrons, free-free emission from H II regions, and thermal emission from dust Williams10 . The 217 GHz luminosity is mostly the thermal glow of dust heated by starlight in the starburst (and Seyfert) regions Williams10,Peel11 .
This is demonstrated by the positive curvature in their microwave spectra, in contrast to the negative curvature expected of a blackbox spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 1068 as a key Seyfert torus example, with polarized broad lines, resolved ALMA torus images, and molecular outflow constraints matching clumpy torus mass estimates.

quotes.quote: The unified model was firstly supported through spectropolarimetric observations of the Seyfert 2 (Sy 2) galaxy NGC 1068 am85 , revealing the polarized broad emission lines, and followed by other polarized broad line observations in type 2 AGNs e.g., by tran92, tran95 .

Very recently, santi16 and ima16 presented the first resolved images of the torus of NGC 1068, the former using the continuum and the CO(6-5) emission observed with ALMA Band 9 and the latter using HCN(3-2) and HCO ^ + (3-2) emission lines.

A recent work from garcia was able to constrain M_ tor derived from clumpy models fitting with the mass of molecular outflow observations in NGC 1068 using ALMA observations in band 7 and 9. They estimate M_ tor 2 10^5 M_ , consistent to the estimated molecular gas mass detected inside the central aperture (=20 pc) derived from the CO(3-2) emission.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1068 as a Seyfert 2 galaxy central to hidden-BLR evidence, discusses scattering broadening, and notes its unusually low virial factor among the sample.

quotes.quote: The unified model antonucci1993 , which originates from the detection of broad emission lines (BELs) in the spectropolarimetric data of Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068 antonucci1985 , proposes that the type 1 (showing both BELs and narrow emission lines) and type 2 (showing only narrow emission lines) active galactic nuclei (AGNs) are the same type of objects viewed from different angles.

shows that, consistent with the conclusion of , thermal electrons dominate the scattering process in NGC 1068 and broaden the polarized emission lines. The virial factor of NGC 1068 is low compared to the other 5 objects (Table tab:obj ). If the width from the dust scattering were used, it would be more similar.

sources.summary: The paper treats M77 as a type-2 Seyfert whose continuum, [O III], soft X-ray, and hard X-ray morphologies show a high-S/N match, though low-brightness resemblance weakens after continuum subtraction.

quotes.quote: M77 : The continuum emission traces the spiral shape of the galaxy, but even with this continuum emission we can start to suspect a resemblance with the soft X-ray morphology. The good match is clearer in the [ O iii ] +continuum image and in the continuum-subtracted [ O iii ] image even clearer. Nevertheless, as we subtract continuum signal we lose the resemblance in the low brightness parts of the galaxy. The morphological good match is also present between the soft and hard X-ray images in the high S/N regions.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1068 as an AGN-containing galaxy whose ALMA molecular line ratios and HC3N abundance imply dense shielded gas in its circumnuclear disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068 is a well-studied AGN-containing galaxy. High-resolution observations of NGC 1068 with ALMA by 2014A&A...567A.125G and 2014A&A...570A..28V showed that the intensity ratio of HCN(1-0)/HCO ^+ (1-0) decreases within the central 30 pc. Also in NGC 1068, observations by 2014PASJ...66...75T and 2015PASJ...67....8N detected HC _3 N, HNCO, CN, CS, SO, CH _3 CN, and CH _3 OH in their ALMA cycle 0 observations.

This relatively high HC _3 N fractional abundance means that a few 100-pc scale circumnuclear disk (CND) in NGC 1068 is not a giant XDR, but contains a significant amount of dense shielded gas from radiation.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 1068 in the MALATANG dense-gas maps, identifies it as a barred spiral with a Seyfert 2 AGN, and notes its high central HCN/HCO+ ratio.

quotes.quote: Keck Observatory, 65-1120 Mamalahoa Hwy, Kamuela, HI 96743, USA Ying-He Zhao Yunnan Observatories, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Kunming 650011, China Ming Zhu National Astronomical Observatories, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Beijing 100012, China We present \ and \ maps of six nearby star-forming galaxies, NGC 253, NGC 1068, IC 342, M82, M83, and NGC 6946, obtained with the James Clerk Maxwell Telescope as part of the MALATANG survey. All galaxies were mapped in the central 2 2 region at 14 (FWHM) resolution (corresponding to linear scales of 0.2-1.0 kpc).
NGC 1068 is a barred spiral galaxy hosting a Seyfert 2 type AGN. f Reference for the source distance. (1) radburn11 ; (2) wu14 ; (3) dalcanton09 ; (4) poznanski09 ; and (5) olivares10 .

We see from Fig. fig:ratio (left) that the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068 shows a high HCN/HCO ^+ ratio with the highest value in the center, in contrast to the low ratio observed in the pure starburst, such as in M82.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 1068 as an archetypal Seyfert 2 whose torus has been spatially resolved by ALMA, with reported molecular gas masses for inner nuclear scales.

quotes.quote: Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) observations of the archetypal Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068 have spatially resolved for the first time the submillimeter (sub-mm) counterpart of the putative torus Garcia-Burillo16,Gallimore16,Imanishi18 .
For NGC 1068 the latter authors reported a mass of M _ gas ^ H_2 =2.3 10 ^7 M _ in the inner 30 pc.

Using the CO(6-5) line observed with ALMA, Garcia-Burillo16 reported a smaller gas mass (1.2 10 ^5 M _ ) for the inner 7-10 pc of NGC 1068. Finally, using ALMA/CO(2-1) data, Herrero18 found that the nuclear disk of the Sy2 NGC 5643 is a factor of 10 more massive and larger ( 26 pc of diameter) than that of NGC 1068.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 1068 as a prototypical Seyfert 2 with a jet-entrained molecular outflow, an ALMA-resolved molecular torus, non-circular turbulent gas motions, and an off-center AGN.

quotes.quote: In the prototypical Seyfert 2 NGC 1068, a clear molecular ouflow has also been detected, entrained by the AGN radio jets krips11, santi1068 . NGC 1068 is also the first case to resolve the molecular torus, using the continuum and the CO(6-5), HCN(3-2) and HCO ^+ (3- 2) emission lines observed with ALMA galli16,santi16tor,ima16 . The dynamics of the molecular gas in the NGC 1068 torus revealed strong non-circular motions and enhanced turbulence superposed on a slow rotation pattern of the disk. The AGN is clearly off-center with respect to the torus, implying an m=1 perturbation santi16tor .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1068 as a nearby type-2 Seyfert neutrino source whose GeV and neutrino fluxes require production near its central supermassive black hole corona.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068, a nearby type-2 Seyfert galaxy, is reported as the hottest neutrino spot in the 10-year survey data of IceCube. Although there are several different possibilities for the generation of high-energy neutrinos in astrophysical sources, feasible scenarios allowing such emission in NGC 1068 have not yet been firmly defined. We show that the flux level of GeV and neutrino emission observed from NGC 1068 implies that the neutrino emission can be produced only in the vicinity of the supermassive black hole in the center of the galaxy. The coronal parameters, such as magnetic field strength and corona size, making this emission possible are consistent with the spectral excess registered in the millimeter range.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1068/Messier 77 as the strongest clustered neutrino-source candidate, describing it as a nearby Seyfert active starburst galaxy with nuclear shocks, dense molecular clouds, and possible merger-fed black-hole activity.

quotes.quote: The strongest of these sources is the nearby Seyfert galaxy NGC 1068 (also known as Messier 77); the second is TXS 0506+056, despite its strong episodic emission. NGC 1068 is an active galaxy with starburst activity. There is evidence for shocks near the core and for molecular clouds with densities of more than 10^5 cm^ 3 . Its origin is a merger onto the black hole, either with a satellite galaxy or, more likely, with a star-forming region .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1068 as a promising nearby starburst and Seyfert source for IceCube neutrino tests, especially because its AGN winds may accelerate cosmic rays.

quotes.quote: The present data show no evidence for event clustering around known astrophysical objects (except for two tentative candidates, TXS 0506+056 and NGC 1068), requiring source classes that are more abundant than blazars.

The calorimetric model predicts that more luminous starbursts like NGC 1068 and NGC 2146 are the most promising, and NGC1068 is the best candidate among nearby starbursts in the northern sky . The IceCube data clearly indicate that cosmic rays need to be accelerated up to 100 PeV energies that is beyond the knee at 3 10 ^ 15 eV . This requires the existence of cosmic-rat accelerators that are more powerful than ordinary supernovae, and proposed possibilities include hypernovae, GRBs, jets and winds from AGNs . NGC 1068 is the most interesting source in this aspect, because it coexists with a Seyfert galaxy that may be accompanied by disk-driven winds .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as an AGN science example where SPHERE mapped obscuring circumnuclear material and revealed a torus-scale elongated structure at parsec resolution.

quotes.quote: Finally, the circumnuclear optically thick material hiding the central core of the Active Galactic Nuclei NGC 1068 (Figure fig:agns ) was mapped with SPHERE. These data reveal an extended nuclear torus at the center of NGC 1068 Gratadour2015 , consisting of a structured hourglass-shaped bicone and a compact elongated (20 pc 60 pc) nuclear structure perpendicular to the bicone axis.

However, because of AO sensitivity, there have been only very few successful attempts using adaptive optics in the near-infrared, with the most detailed results on NGC 1068 where a pc scale was indeed reached (e.g. rouan2004 using NaCo and Gratadour2015 using SPHERE) and on the nearby AGN Circinus (see mullersanchez2006 using SINFONI). With SPHERE, near-infrared polarization observations offers the advantage to not be spoiled by the dazzling central core. For NGC 1068, SHERE has revealed an elongated structure of 60 pc diameter in the same direction as the molecular torus and tracing presumably the outskirt of the torus through a double scattering process Gratadour2015,grosset2018 .

sources.summary: The review presents NGC 1068 as a Seyfert 2 galaxy whose polarized broad lines and HST polarization structure support obscuration and AGN geometry models.

quotes.quote: The detection of broad emission lines in polarized light in the galaxy NGC 1068, previously considered a typical representative of galaxies of Sy 2 type (Miller et al. 1991), was a good proof of the described assumption of obscuration and the UM in general.

Moreover, the polarimetric studies of NGC 1068 were conducted by HST with the Faint Object Camera in the ultraviolet and with the WF/PC-I in the visual (Capetti et al. 1997 and references therein). Observations have shown the polarization changing across the field. A few years after Kishimoto (1999) reanalyzed the HST polarimetric data and constructed a model under the assumption of light coming from a point source and scattering at clouds (in this case, NLR), and the two-dimensional polarization distribution was de-projected into a three-dimensional matter distribution in the AGN.

sources.summary: The paper fits one NuSTAR observation of NGC 1068, noting its consistently highly Compton-thick nature and prior detailed multi-epoch X-ray analyses.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068: Due to the complexity of the spectra of this source in the soft X-rays we fit the FPMA/FPMB spectra extracted from only one \ observation. The source is known to be consistently highly CT. Detailed analysis of the multi-epoch, X-ray spectra of this source are presented in and . We modeled the spectra using an absorbed power law plus a high-temperature Apec component and reprocessed emission.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068 as a distant active/starburst galaxy whose reported neutrino excess could fit enhanced cosmic-ray activity, though missing gamma rays challenge disk/halo interpretations.

quotes.quote: This is particularly relevant in connection with the recent claim by the Icecube collaboration of a 3 excess of very high energy neutrinos from the galaxy NGC 1068, located at a distance of 14.4 Mpc aartsen2020 . This galaxy exhibit both AGN and starburst activity, and therefore it seems to be an ideal neutrino emitter. However, the non-detection of very high energy rays from this object aartsen2020 challenges an interpretation of the neutrino excess based on proton-proton interactions from the extended disk/halo region. A possible combined explanation of ray and neutrino observations of NGC 1068 was recently proposed in . Deeper observations in both neutrinos and rays will help in clarifying this issue.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as a Seyfert comparison: prior photoionizing-continuum reconstructions were inconclusive, its normalized line luminosity matches external estimates, and [O IV] can dominate its mid-IR spectrum.

quotes.quote: Using this argument, 1999ApJ...512..204A,2000ApJ...536..710A combined all the UV, optical, and IR lines from the spectra of NGC 4151, Circinus, and NGC 1068 to derive the best-fitting photo-ionising continuum that reproduces the observed line fluxes in each case. The results are consistent with ionisation by a disc emission in Circinus, while the cases of NGC 1068 and NGC 4151 are still inconclusive due to the presence of abrupt absorption features in the reconstructed continuum.

Additional examples are NGC 1052 ( 3.7 vs. 3.4 from 2019MNRAS.485.5377F ), Centaurus A ( 2.7 vs. 2.4 from 2011A&A...531A..70B ), NGC 1068 ( 0.5 vs. 0.2 from 2020MNRAS.492.3872Z ), and 3C 120 ( 0.4 vs. 0.5 from 2016MNRAS.458.2454L,2018ApJ...856..120R ).

Regarding the primary ionising spectrum, we computed two main sets of simulations intended to reproduce the most extreme cases in the LED: i) low-excitation nuclei with bright [ Ne ii ] _ 12.8 emission and faint [ O iv ] _ 25.9 ; ii) the high-excitation nuclei where [ O iv ] _ 25.9 can be the most prominent line in the mid-IR spectrum (e.g. NGC 1068 and 3C 120 in 2010ApJ...709.1257T ).

sources.summary: The paper cites mid-IR interferometry of NGC 1068 to argue that crystalline silicate is detected in its central region, supporting an AGN origin for the crystalline material.

quotes.quote: However, Spoon2006 conclude that this possibility is unlikely because of the following two reasons: one is that crystalline feature is not detected in the central 2 pc of NGC 1068 in the mid-IR interferometric observation in Jaffe2004 . Raban2009 report the follow-up observation of NGC 1068 with a different position angle and longer baseline length than those in Jaffe2004 . We find that the 11 m crystalline feature is significantly detected in the mid-IR spectra shown in Raban2009 although Raban2009 do not mention the crystalline feature. Hence the crystallinity of the central region of NGC1068 rather supports the picture that crystalline silicate originates from AGNs.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 1068's central ionized gas, describing its strong-emission region, velocity field, and signs of nuclear outflow, rotation, density structure, or inflow.

quotes.quote: This well studied Sy 2 galaxy shows the peak of gas emission displaced by 0 5 (30 pc) towards the NE from the centre, with the SER extending along this direction, that makes an angle of 50 ^ with the PMA. Along this direction -- that can be identified with the AGN ionisation axis -- blueshifts are observed to the NE and redshifts to the SW reaching 800 km s ^ 1 in at 2 5 from the nucleus and somewhat lower values ( 600 ) in the lines. Beyond the high blueshifts, redshifts are observed to the NE, evidencing the presence of more than one kinematic component and that the high blue and redshifts are due to nuclear outflows . W _ 80 is enhanced to values as high as 2500 km s ^ 1 surrounding the regions with the highest velocities.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068 as a stage-N LIRG and possible past merger, adopting multi-observatory X-ray modeling that finds a heavily obscured AGN.

quotes.quote: IRAS F02401--0013 (NGC 1068): The LIRG NGC 1068 is a possible past merger Tanaka2017 categorized as a stage-N source in this work. The source has been well studied in X-rays, e.g., with Chandra e.g., Torres-Alba2018 and NuSTAR e.g., Marinucci2016 . We refer to the results reported in Bauer2015 , who perform a spectral analysis adopting a multi-component reflector model to the NuSTAR, Chandra, XMM-Newton, Suzaku, BeppoSAX, and Swift/BAT spectra. They find that the AGN is heavily obscured ( N_ H 10^ 25 cm ^ 2 ) with an absorption-corrected X-ray luminosity of log L_ 2-10 = 43.34 ^ +0.07 _ 0.08 .

sources.summary: The paper compares the Milky Way outflow to NGC 1068, describing its Seyfert ionizers, star-forming ring, radio structures, jet, bow-shaped lobe, and extended narrow-line-region deceleration.

quotes.quote: In prototypical Seyfert NGC 1068 , all of these ionizers are operating on a <100 pc radius within its 1 kpc star-forming ring. Figure fig:radioXray (d) highlights remarkable similarities between NGC 1068 radio structures and those in our GC: a core region delineated by elliptical green contours at a 60 pc radius that coincides with the bundle of vertical nonthermal, strongly polarized radio filaments 2021ApJ...920....6G in the MW that delineates its radio shells, knots in the compact jet within that have the same scale as the X-ray extension, radio lobes, and GC Lobe in the MW, and blobs at the end of the NGC 1068 jet that distend to become the NE bow-shaped radio lobe at the same scale as the FB in the MW. The latter shape with adjacent optical and X-ray emission (Figure 32) signifies a radiative, detached bow shock formed when a supersonic jet encounters a rapid decline in ambient pressure. In the extended narrow-line region of NGC 1068, this deceleration likely occurs near the ``North-East subpeak" bright at 10 m 1987ApJ...312..542T from localized inverse-Compton heating by relativistic electrons impacting warm dusty gas.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as a Seyfert 2 NLR benchmark, comparing ground-based and HST measurements of its nucleus and detached emission-line knots when interpreting dense inner NLR visibility.

quotes.quote: Ground-based observations of the Seyfert 2 NGC 1068 nucleus by Kos78 is represented by the black open octagon while the black open triangle corresponds to the HST-FOS measurement of the nucleus at a much higher spatial resolution of 0.3 \ archive data hereafter Kr98 Kr98 . - Spatially resolved ENLR emission The red filled dot stands for the average ratio from the ENLR of four Type II AGN (two are Seyfert 2's: ESO 362-G08 and MRK 573, and two are NLRGs: Pks 0349 27 and Pks 0634 20) which were studied by hereafter SB96 SB96 .

Two detached emission line 'knots' of NGC 1068 labelled 1 and 2 (red triangles) which were studied by Kr98 using HST-FOS archive data. The positions are off-centered from the nucleus by 0.2 \ and 0.7 , respectively.

A possible interpretation is that the super-luminous QSO 2's scale up in size to the extent that their inner NLR become partly visible as is the case with the high spatial resolution HST measurement of the nearby Seyfert 2 NGC 1068 (.f. red triangles in Fig. fig:fig1 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as an archetypal AGN where mid-infrared observations show polar, extended dust emission tied to the narrow-line region.

quotes.quote: In fact, the existence of AGN-heated dust on larger scales in the narrow line region (NLR), preferentially along the polar direction, was suggested long ago e.g., netzer1993, dopita2002 and directly revealed by, e.g., the early spatially-resolved mid-IR observations of the archetypal type-2 AGN NGC 1068 and type-1 AGN NGC 4151 where the extended dust emission at 10 is co-spatial with the NLR morphology e.g., bock2000, radomski2003 .

However, observations of nearby AGNs such as NGC 1068 bock2000 , NGC 4151 radomski2003 , and many more alonso2021 show that a substantial fraction of the 10 m emission comes from regions well removed from the nucleus, up to tens of pc for luminous systems.

This argument is illustrated by the GRAVITY gravity2020 , MATISSE gamez2022 , and imaging bock2000 observations of NGC 1068. The first reveals a ring-like structure at K-band ( 2.2 m), the second shows dust emission at 4 -12 mostly consistent with a polar wind, and the third shows extended emission on 100 pc scales to the full extent of the bright forbidden line region seen at 12.5 m and suggests even greater extent at 24.5 m.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1068 as an active-nucleus galaxy neutrino point source with about 80 detected neutrinos, but too nearby to strongly constrain neutrino magnetic moments.

quotes.quote: To date, IceCube has identified two point sources of high-energy neutrinos, the blazar TXS 0506+056 with 15 detected neutrinos and the active nucleus of the NGC 1068 galaxy with about 80 neutrinos . Both analyses are based on muon tracks only, so the detailed flavour structure of the neutrino flux from these sources is as yet unknown. Moreover, NGC 1068 is relatively close ( 14.4 Mpc ), so its neutrinos will experience much less _L N_R conversion than those from a source at O ( Gpc ) , as we have assumed in fig:flavour-triangles . Therefore, NGC 1068 is not optimal for constraining neutrino magnetic moments.

sources.summary: The review describes interferometric imaging of NGC 1068's central two parsecs, where K-band observations found a sub-pc ring-like dust structure linked to the maser disc.

quotes.quote: The advent of the 2 ^ nd generation VLTI instruments and the combination of four 8-m telescopes allowed for the first time to reconstruct images with GRAVITY and MATISSE. resolved the central 2 pc of NGC 1068 in K-band with a spatial resolution of 3 mas (Figure fig:AGN a) and found a ring-like structure on sub-pc scales. The size matches that expected for the dust sublimation region, and the apparent orientation is similar to that of the maser disc, arguing for a common origin. This scenario is at odds with a geometrically and optically thick clumpy torus and instead argues for the presence of a dusty thin disc around the AGN, which is screened by dense and turbulent gas distributed on scales of 1–10 pc, e.g. from AGN-driven outflows.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 1068 as a nearby active, radio Seyfert-like neutrino-emitter candidate with an edge-on warped disk, outflow-cloud interactions, and possible coronal cosmic-ray acceleration.

quotes.quote: The latter have yet shown their potential, leading to the claim for evidence of neutrino emission from the AGN NGC 1068 IceCube_10y_reprocessed:2022 .

5BZU 1819 6345 shares similarities with NGC 1068, that is a nearby ( 14 Mpc) active galaxy suggested as neutrino emitter IceCube10y:2020,IceCube_10y_reprocessed:2022 , among others an host galaxy morphology dominated by a strong, warped disk component that is observed edge-on. It has been suggested as an extreme example of the effects of outflow-cloud interactions in galaxies containing a rich interstellar medium, and perhaps a missing link between radio galaxies and radio-loud Seyfert galaxies Morganti:2011 .

For objects with a significant coronal activity (as proposed for NGC 1068), in the presence of highly magnetized and turbulent coronae, cosmic rays can be accelerated and subsequently depleted via hadronuclear and photohadronic interactions, providing targets for 1-100 TeV energy neutrino production.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as the archetypal Type II AGN comparison, incorporating MUSE spectroscopy and finding clumpy and skirtor models view it through the torus.

quotes.quote: We also employed, for comparison purposes, photometric points for NGC 4151 and NGC 1068 as representatives of Type I and II AGN, respectively. The VizieR photometry tool ^ myfootnote was used to gather the data. For NGC 1068 in the optical range, a spectrum of the inner 500 × 500 pc obtained from the Multi Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE) processed archival data was used and the stellar continuum was estimated using the starlight .

Test case with NGC 4151 and NGC 1068 In order to test the fitting procedure with clumpy , cat3d-wind and skirtor , we first fit the SED of the archetypal Seyfert 1 and 2 galaxies NGC 4151 and NGC 1068, respectively.
For NGC 1068, both skirtor and clumpy suggest that we are looking at this source through the torus.

sources.summary: The paper presents sub-parsec VLBA imaging and radio SED results for NGC 1068, describing its central radio morphology and spectral shape in the FRAMEx AGN sample.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068 [ 2 ^ h 42 ^ m 40 70890, 00 00 48 0062 ] : This source is detected in C and X bands but not in L and K. Note that NGC 1068 suffers from severe short spacing issues due to the bright, extended radio continuum emission and that the non-detection at L band may be due to these effects. However, all four band peak intensities, including 5 nondetections upper limits, show a flat (typically, if > - 0.5 ) spectrum. A little extended structure was detected in the C band image; as a result, a steeper spectrum was seen when was measured only between C and X bands integrated flux densities.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as a key obscured AGN neutrino-source example, citing its evidence and X-ray-inferred column density when motivating matter effects.

quotes.quote: Nevertheless, there is tantalizing evidence of a handful of extragalactic point sources - the active galaxies TXS 0506+056, NGC 1068 and PKS 1424+240 being the most significant, albeit limited to TeV energies and muon neutrinos only. The fact that these are all active galactic nuclei (AGNs) strongly indicates that AGNs are among the most promising candidate sources .
The evidence for NGC 1068 as a neutrino source and subsequent studies modeling its emission further strengthens the case for obscured AGNs as the main astrophysical neutrino sources.

Flavor conversion via the L resonance alone can be achieved at smaller N_ H 10^ 30 cm ^ 2 , which is still orders of magnitude higher than that ( 10^ 25 cm ^ 2 ) inferred for NGC 1068 from X ray studies .

sources.summary: The paper compares Henize 2-10 to NGC 1068, noting observed jet/outflow maser phenomena, multiple velocity components, and off-nuclear maser velocities offset from dense-gas tracers.

quotes.quote: Similar phenomena have been observed in NGC 4258 through VLBA observations Haschick1994, Miyoshi1995 , NGC 1068 via VLA observations Gallimore1996, Gallimore2001 and ALMA observations Hagiwara2024 , Mrk 348 through VLBA observations Peck2003 , and the Compton-thick AGN in IRAS F15480 0344 using the European VLBI Network Castangia2016 .

While outflow-driven \ masers often have a single broad line in their spectra, multiple velocity components are also seen in these objects, such as those found in NGC 1068 Hagiwara2024 , NGC 4258 Haschick1994 , and NGC 3079 Trotter1998 . Furthermore, we observe a velocity offset at S1 between HCN (1--0) and the \ maser. While the weighted mean velocities of HCN (1--0) range from 870 to 890 \ (depicted in Figure fig:h2o_molecular ), those of the \ maser range from 850 to 870 \ (as shown in Figure fig:moments ). Even considering the velocity resolution of HCN (1--0) of 10 , this discrepancy in velocity field may suggest that the molecular gas is being perturbed by the BH outflow. A similar phenomenon has been identified in NGC 1068 through MERLIN and ALMA observations Hagiwara2024 . In NGC 1068, the location of the off-nuclear \ maser displayed velocities ranging from 1050 to 1150 \ in HCN (3--2) and from 1000 to 1100 \ in HCO ^ + (3--2), while registering 980 \ in the \ maser.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC1068 as a Compton-thick Seyfert 2 template, noting reflected hard X-rays plus SCUBA nuclear and circumnuclear submillimeter components, with the latter linked to star-forming activity.

quotes.quote: NCG1068 is the archetype object for the class of Seyfert 2 galaxies as a whole and at the same time for the Compton thick subclass. In this object the hard X rays are due to reflection from both a cold and a warm mirror (Matt et al. ). SCUBA maps at 850 m and at 450 m of NGC1068 were obtained by Papadopoulos Seaquist ( ) and show a nuclear component associated to the AGN and a circumnuclear component associated to the starforming activity, which probably accounts for 2/3 of the sub-mm emission. The _ SX of NGC1068 is shown in Fig. 1; for this object, we also show the _ SX relative to the nuclear component alone (i.e. excluding the sub-mm flux of the circumnuclear starburst).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068 as a heavily obscured Seyfert/starburst composite with complex thermal and Fe-line X-ray components; its compact radio jet cannot explain the extended thermal X-rays.

quotes.quote: The complex spectrum of NGC 1068 includes two thermal and three Fe line components in addition to the power law of the scattered AGN.

We interpret the low foreground column density and lack of a second, absorbed (in the \ bandpass) power-law component in NGC 1068, Mrk 1066, Mrk 266, and NGC 7130 as evidence that the intrinsic AGN is completely buried and fully absorbed by N_H 10^ 24 cm^ 2 .

A radio jet of 500 pc radius is observed in NGC 1068 Wil82 . This linear radio structure is much smaller than the X-ray extent this galaxy, however, so the extended and thermal X-ray emission of NGC 1068 are not due to the jet.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1068 as a Seyfert 2 with a central starburst ring, wispy inner X-ray emission, and compact and lobe-aligned nuclear X-ray structures.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068 NGC 1068 is a Seyfert 2 galaxy harboring an intense starburst ring within its central 2 kpc. X-ray emission covers much of the inner region of the galaxy (Fig. \ 1 a ) and shows a wispy structure similar to the spiral arms (Fig. \ 1 b ). Figure 1 shows an enlargement of the central region. A bright compact X-ray source extends 1.5 (165 pc) in the direction of the nuclear radio continuum emission (Young, Wilson Shopbell 2001). Bright X-ray emission also extends 5 (550 pc) to the NE coinciding with the northeast radio lobe (Fig. \ 1 d ) and high-excitation ionized gas seen in [ O III ] 5007 images.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 1068 among four nearby intermediate-inclination spiral galaxies observed with major- and minor-axis long-slit spectroscopy for stellar disk kinematic modeling.

quotes.quote: We observed four galaxies: NGC 1068, NGC 2460, NGC 2775, and NGC 4030. All four are nearby, spiral galaxies of intermediate inclination ranging in Hubble type from Sa to Sbc. The main characteristics of each galaxy are listed in Table t:sample . Long-slit spectra were obtained along the photometric major and minor axes of each galaxy, in multiple exposures of 30 minutes each.

sources.summary: The paper notes discrepant TF distances for NGC1068, identifies it with LGG73, and adopts a 15.30 Mpc distance supported by group-member distance data.

quotes.quote: NGC1068. -- Two values of TF distances to NGC1068 show an appreciable discrepancy: 12.45 Mpc (Sch o niger Sofue 1994) compared with 18.10 Mpc (Takamiya Sofue 2000). The galaxy NGC1068 is a member of the LGG73 group (Garcia 1993). A distance of 16.07 Mpc to the galaxy NGC1055 (also a member of LGG73 group) was estimated by Paturel et al. (1998) using the method of ``sosies galaxies''. The mean TF distance of 15.30 Mpc is confirmed by the Paturel et al.'s data and is adopted here.

sources.summary: The paper presents GMOS integral-field spectroscopy of NGC 1068, describing its complex Seyfert nucleus, inner spiral arms, stellar bar, central CO ring, and line-emission kinematics.

quotes.quote: Below we briefly summarize the key morphological characteristics of NGC 1068 (see also Antonucci, 1993; Krolik Begelman, 1988). Although the Seyfert galaxy NGC 1068 resembles an ordinary Sb type galaxy at its largest scales, the central region exhibits considerable complexity.
