Figure f:fov shows a CO map reproduced from Schinnerer (2000) detailing the structure on a 1 kpc scale. Two inner spiral arms and the inner stellar bar first recognized by Scoville (1988, see also Thatte et al., 1997) can clearly be seen.

sources.summary: The paper applies a generalized Tremaine-Weinberg method to NGC 1068, finding a radially varying pattern speed and a short-lived grand-design spiral structure.

quotes.quote: Application of this method to CO observations of NGC 1068 reveal that the pattern speed of the spiral structure in this galaxy varies rapidly with radius, and that the lifetime of the spiral structure is correspondingly very short.

As an initial experiment, we have applied this algorithm to the BIMA Survey of Nearby Galaxies BIMA SONG; hetal03 CO observations of the grand-design Sb galaxy NGC 1068, which offers a test case well suited to this analysis. Although optically classified as a normal spiral RSA , albeit one with a Seyfert nucleus, the 2MASS near-infrared observations jccsh03 reveal a clear central bar, which provides a strong candidate for the driver of the grand design spiral seen in the CO emission. rw04 showed that NGC 1068's gas content is dominated by molecular material and that there is not much current star formation, so little of the molecular phase is being converted to or from other phases and the application of the continuity equation to this component alone is valid.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1068 as a prototype Seyfert 2 AGN-dominated source, fitting its infrared emission with a torus model constrained by mid-infrared data.

quotes.quote: One of the three objects, Mrk 231, was chosen because it represents a type-1 AGN-dominated ULIRG, while NGC 1068 was chosen as a prototype type-2 AGN-dominated source. NGC 1068 NGC 1068 is a very well studied prototype Seyfert 2 galaxy and the first one against which the Unified AGN Model was tested (see e.g. antonucci84 and antonucci85 ). The torus model that better fits the data (see Fig. fig:NGC 1068_fit ) is one with an outer-to-inner radial ratio of 20 which, rescaled to fit the observed datapoints, gives a value of about 17.4 pc for the external radius for a luminosity of 3.9 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 of the central source.
Anyway, in the model adopted to reproduce NGC 1068 emission in the mid-infrared, the dust reaches such a temperature within a radius of 8 pc.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as the key Seyfert 2 example: its hidden broad-line region and resolved electron-scattering region motivate compact-torus ESR geometry for Seyfert nuclei.

quotes.quote: The most important observational evidence for this unified model is the detection of a hidden BLR (HBLR) in the nearby S2, NGC 1068 in optical spectropolarimetry (Antonucci Miller 1985). Subsequent optical spectropolarimetric observations have detected the HBLR in more than a dozen of S2s [ Miller Goodrich 1990 (hereafter MG90); Tran, Miller, Kay 1992; Kay 1994; Tran 1995a, 1995b, 1995c; Inglis et al. 1993; Hines Wills 1993; Young et al. 1996; HLB97 ] . Although the discovery of the HBLR in the S2s has led to the unified model, the HBLR has been detected in only 20 of the observed S2s (Kay 1994; Tran 1995a).

Although the nature of the ESR is not clear, the ESR is spatially resolved in the nearby S2, NGC 1068; gaseous blobs with several tens pc are observed up to a radius of 300 pc (Capetti et al. 1995b; see also Kishimoto 1999).

Although the spatially extended ESR is observed in NGC 1068, there seems no reason to regard NGC 1068 as a standard S2. There may be a possibility for the majority of S2s that the ESR is hidden by a compact, nuclear dusty torus whose size is < 1 pc.

sources.summary: The paper uses archival VISIR data for NGC 1068, reports extended mid-infrared line emission, and cites a maser-based black-hole mass for the Seyfert 2 galaxy.

quotes.quote: In addition to the 8 objects, archival VISIR data of NGC 1068 and NGC 253 has been downloaded, extracted, and calibrated in the same way.

Except for the emission in the most nearby objects NGC 1068 ( 10 extension in [ S iv ] with respect to the continuum PSF) and Circinus ( 50 extension in [ S iv ] and [ Ne ii ] ), no spatial extension was detected in the continuum and the lines.
In addition, the black hole mass of the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068 could be determined by MASER cloud kinematics as 7.0 Gre96 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as a key clumpy-torus case, emphasizing its edge-on torus/accretion disk geometry and successful near-infrared modeling of the nucleus.

quotes.quote: Our clumpy torus models were also employed in the analysis of spatially-resolved, near-diffraction-limited 10 \ spectra of the NGC 1068 nucleus Mason06 . The geometry and kinematics of both water maser Greenhill97, Gallimore01 and narrow-line emission Crenshaw00 indicate that the NGC 1068 torus and accretion disk are oriented nearly edge-on. The Mason06 clumpy model for IR emission is the first to correctly reproduce the observed near-IR flux with an edge-on orientation.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068 as a Seyfert 2 with an opaque torus, one of the highest mass-accretion rates, and an initially massive opaque collisional disk.

quotes.quote: Moreover, 3 AGNs (Circinus, NGC 3227, and NGC 1068) have opaque tori ( < 2 ), 2 AGNs (NGC 1097 and NGC 7469) show moderately transparent tori ( 3-4 ), and 1 AGN (NGC 3783) has a transparent torus ( > 5 ).
The highest mass accretion rates (NGC 3227 and NGC 1068) are several ten times higher than that of NGC 3783.

Four of the initial collisional disks at the beginning of Phase II (Circinus, NGC 3227, NGC 1068, and NGC 7469) had Toomre Q parameters around Q=3 and close to unity, i.e. they were massive and opaque.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC1068 as one of nine disk galaxies hosting pseudobulges and places it among the pseudobulges lying below the paper's inferred scaling relations.

quotes.quote: By means of the S e rsic index, we identify nine disk galaxies as hosting pseudobulges: Circinus, IC2560, NGC1068, NGC3079, NGC3368, NGC3489, NGC3998, NGC4258, NGC4594.

Figure fg:mbh illustrates that 4 out of 9 pseudobulges (NGC3489, NGC3998, NGC4258, NGC4594) are consistent with the correlation for classical bulges, while the others (CIRCINUS, IC2560, NGC1068, NGC3079, NGC3368) are outliers, at more than 4 below the LINMIX ERR linear regression.
Of these, CIRCINUS, IC2560, NGC1068, and NGC3398 lie below all the \ scaling relations; NGC3368 deviates from , - but not from , while the contrary happens for NGC3998.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes X-ray spectra of NGC 1068, describing its best-fit components, Fe K emission or flux behavior, and comparison with previous modeling.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068 The PN and MOS1 XMM-Newton spectra for both observations showed evidence of pileup according to epatplot and were therefore not included in the spectral fit; archival Chandra data also exists for NGC 1068, but was not used in this study due to the effects of pileup. Also, as several strong emission features were present below 1 keV (which are not important for the purposes of this study), we fitted the spectrum from 1 keV to 8 keV. The MOS2 spectra were best fit by an absorbed double power law with a thermal model and Gaussian components at 2.0 keV, 2.43 keV, 6.4 keV (neutral Fe K ), 6.66 keV (likely ionized Fe K ) and 6.95 keV.

sources.summary: The survey reports a first HC3N detection for NGC 1068, classifies it as HC3N-poor by line-ratio limits, and notes its OH mega- or kilomaser activity.

quotes.quote: New detections This is not only the first text to put together a survey of all extragalactic HC _3 N emission line data, but it also reports the first HC _3 N detections in six galaxies: Circinus , IC 860 , IRAS 17208-0014 , Maffei 2 , NGC 1068 , and NGC 3079 . Some galaxies can definitely be seen as HC _3 N-poor, since they have HC _3 N/HCN ratios (or upper limits for this ratio) less than or equal to 0.10: NGC 253 , NGC 1068 , NGC 1808 , NGC 3256 , NGC 4945 , NGC 6946 , and UGC 5101 . A few of the HC _3 N-poor galaxies also have OH mega- or kilomaser activity ( NGC 253 , NGC 1068 , and UGC 5101 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as observational evidence for AGN toroidal obscuration, noting its hidden Seyfert 1 core and multi-temperature parsec-scale dusty structure.

quotes.quote: The detection of broad permitted lines in the polarized UV and optical spectrum of the nearby, luminous Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068, confirmed that a bright, Seyfert 1 core is hidden behind optically thick, obscuring material.

Direct evidence of the existence of toroidal obscuration comes from the mid- infrared observations of Seyfert 2 galaxies, such as the prototypical Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068, and the closest AGN, the Circinus galaxy. Observations of NGC 1068 using VLTI reveal a multi-component, multi-temperature dusty conglomerate: an inner, relatively small ( 1 pc) and hot ( 800 K) component embedded into an outer ( 3.5 pc) component which is much colder ( T 320 K) Jaffe2004, Raban09 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as a prototypical obscured AGN, describing Compton-thick blockage and a warped molecular disk that crosses the line of sight to its nucleus.

quotes.quote: The donut torus was invoked to explain AGNs such as NGC 1068, in which the direct central radiation was completely blocked by a Compton thick obscurer [ _ Compton =1 corresponds to N _H =2 10 ^ 24 cm ^ 2 ] .

In the prototypical type 2 AGN, NGC 1068 (for which the donut torus was largely originally created ), a Compton thick warped disk with an inner radius of 70 pc curls up to just cross our line of sight to the nucleus . This molecular disk appears to be disconnected from any smaller structure stretching down to the dust sublimation radius.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068 as a local AGN comparison, noting lower infrared luminosity, far-infrared shape similarity, weaker silicate absorption, and a sharp drop below 3 microns.

quotes.quote: We compare our silicate AGN composite SED with the SEDs of local AGN Mrk 231 ( L_ IR = 3.2 10^ 12 L _ ) and NGC 1068 ( L_ IR = 1.6 10^ 11 L _ ) in Figure agn_local_temp .

NGC 1068 looks fairly similar in shape in the far-IR to our composite, though it is intrinsically less luminous. Furthermore, NGC 1068, a local Compton-thick AGN, has less silicate absorption in the mid-IR and falls off sharply below 3 m, differing from both our high redshift SED and Mrk 231.

sources.summary: The review uses NGC 1068 as the prototypical type 2 AGN, describing a hidden type 1 nucleus, line-of-sight obscuration, and polar scattering of broad-line emission.

quotes.quote: The seminal work by Antonucci Miller showed that the prototypical type 2 active galactic nucleus (AGN) in NGC 1068 hosts a hidden type 1 nucleus in its center. However, due to significant line-of-sight obscuration of the optical and UV emission, the broad emission lines and ``big-blue bump'' emission are not directly visible to the observer. The authors found, however, that electrons in the polar region scatter the emission from the obscured nucleus and revealed the broad-emissions typical of a type 1 AGN in the polarized flux.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as a comparison megamaser system whose molecular circumnuclear disk is warped from tens of parsecs down to the maser disk.

quotes.quote: The molecular disk in NGC 1068 is warped, but in inclination rather than in PA.
However, in the megamaser galaxies NGC 1068, NGC 3079, Circinus, and NGC 2273, detailed gas observations do exist.
In NGC 1068, schinnereretal2000 probe 20 pc scales with CO, and show that the molecular gas disk has a warp that starts at 70 pc and extends all the way to the maser disk on 1 pc scales.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068 as a nearby prototype AGN with a star-forming molecular ring and a relatively small central SMBH embedded in a massive disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068, the prototype AGN in our local neighborhood, has been explored in the stellar light Telesco84, Davies07 , but also in molecular gas and dust continuum by Schinnerer2000, Galliano2003, Galliano05 , finding a 3 kpc-scale ring of molecular gas with intense star formation activity.
Other objects tb For each object (column 1), in column 2 the black hole mass is given as estimated from reverberation mapping except for NGC 1068, for which the dynamical mass estimate is given.
NGC 1068 is a case of a relatively small SMBH in the center of a very massive disk with a mass ratio similar to those we obtain for our models with unregulated star formation.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 1068's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068 is a face-on spiral-like luminous infrared galaxy at a distance 14.4 3.0 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with an AGN optically classified either as Sy 1.8-2 ho_search_1997-1,ho_search_1997,osterbrock_spectroscopic_1993 and has polarized broad emission lines antonucci_spectropolarimetry_1985 . NGC 1068 is one of the original six Seyfert galaxies seyfert_nuclear_1943 and thus its AGN is one of the most-studied at all wavelengths (see honig_high-spatial_2008 for a recent detailed multiwavelength study), and the discovery of polarized broad emission lines in NGC 1068 led to the formulation of the unification scheme for AGN antonucci_spectropolarimetry_1985 . It possesses a cone-like NLR in northern direction (length 10 700 pc; PA 35 ; pogge_extended_1988,evans_hst_1991 ) roughly coinciding with a bended radio jet wilson_radio_1982,gallimore_subarcsecond_1996-1 , and nuclear H _2 O and OH maser emission perpendicularly orientated to the jet (PA 94 ; claussen_water-vapor_1984,greenhill_vlbi_1996,gallimore_h_1996 ).

sources.summary: The review uses NGC 1068 as the historic spectropolarimetric case establishing AGN viewing-angle unification through scattered broad-line emission.

quotes.quote: Historically, polarimetric observations of the active Seyfert galaxy NGC 1068 helped to establish the -- nowadays standard -- viewing angle unification scheme of AGN. Spectropolarimetry at optical wavelengths shows that the total flux received from the galaxy is actually composed of two components: one -- unpolarized -- from directly observable gas with narrow emission lines, one -- linearly polarized -- from gas with much broader emission lines located within a dust torus and visible only indirectly via Thomson scattering toward the observer (cf., e.g., baek2007,lee2011 ). This observation eventually removed the distinction between narrow and broad emission line galaxies which were found to be different realizations of AGN miller1983,miller1991 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068 as a gamma-ray-loud Seyfert 2, notes its fully obscured direct X-ray emission, and discusses whether star formation or AGN activity powers the gamma rays.

quotes.quote: In NGC 1068, the direct X-ray emission of is completely obscured along our line of sight and we only see the reflected component.

Furthermore, noted that L_ measured in NGC 1068 exceeds by a factor of 10 the level expected for the total gas mass and supernova rate estimated in this galaxy, which led them to suggest that the rays may be produced in a jet.

Similarly, found that their detailed starburst model underestimates the observed ray flux and overestimates the radio flux for NGC 1068, and they argued that these problems would be resolved if the AGN were the primary source of rays.

sources.summary: Within the Seyfert sample, NGC 1068 is an extreme obscured AGN: its optical-extinction to X-ray-absorption ratio reaches about 200, hot dust lies several sublimation radii out, and it remains Compton-thick with hot dust.

quotes.quote: We find a large spread in the observed ratio with factors ranging from 1 (NGC 2992) to 200 (NGC 1068).
In NGC 1068, for example, Speckle imaging and long-baseline infrared interferometry show that the hot dust is located on scales (3-10) r_ sub weigelt2004,lopezgonzaga2014 .

All BAT sources without hot dust detections are Compton-thick, but the inverse is not true: some of the most nearby Compton-thick objects show evidence for hot dust (the Circinus galaxy, NGC 1068 and NGC 7582).

sources.summary: The overview discusses NGC 1068 molecular shock and chemistry diagnostics, including methanol clumps in the starburst ring, SiO concentration in the CND, and enhanced HCN/HCO+ ratios.

quotes.quote: In contrast, in the central kpc region of NGC 1068, there are striking differences between tracers of mild and strong shocks.

presented the first methanol image of NGC 1068, showing clumps across the starburst ring or arms, as seen in the case of CO and its isotopologs, suggesting that mild shocks are ubiquitous in the arms hosting bursts of massive star formation.

More recently, spatially resolved ALMA observations of HCN(4-3) and HCO ^+ (4-3) at the CND of NGC 1068 at a 50 pc resolution reveal that the HCN(4-3)/HCO ^+ (4-3) line ratios are indeed highly elevated at the CND but not significantly at the very position of the active nucleus ( ; ).

sources.summary: The paper detects strong H2CO 24-23 in NGC 1068, suggesting sufficient warm dense gas and possible influence from its AGN signature.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068 : In NGC 1068, the integrated intensity of \ 24-23 is about 2.0 K \ (in T_ mb ), which is stronger than that of \ 10-9 ( 1.1 K ) reported by . It may imply that there is sufficient warm and dense gas, which is able to excite the high transition \ 24-23 line. Besides, it could also be affected by the strong AGN signature of this galaxy Wang:2014, Tsai:2012 .

sources.summary: NGC 1068 is cited as a Seyfert-2 AGN whose polarized spectrum reveals a hidden Seyfert-1 nucleus, parsec-scale dusty torus, and maser/radio-axis misalignment.

quotes.quote: Antonucci1985 found, in the polarized optical light of the Seyfert-2 galaxy NGC 1068, a Seyfert-1 spectrum with broad emission lines, indicating that a Seyfert-1 nucleus exists in all Seyfert-2 AGN.

Jaffe2004 spatially resolved a parsec-sized torus-shaped distribution of dust grains in the galaxy NGC 1068, where a small hot structure is embedded in a colder (320 K) dusty cocoon extending up to 3.4 pc in diameter.

Using Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) milliarcsecond-scale resolution observations of NGC 1068, Gallimore2004 revealed a slight misalignment between the line traced by the H _2 O maser spots and the radio axis of the radio continuum source, believed to mark the location of the hidden nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper models complex reflection lines in NGC 1068, uses its ALMA-resolved molecular outflow, and places it between momentum- and energy-conserving flow regimes.

quotes.quote: In NGC 1068, which has the most complex reflection lines among the targets, we added six positive Gaussians at 6.4 keV (neutral Fe K ), 6.7 keV (He-like Fe), 7.0 keV (H-like Fe), 7.4 keV (neutral Ni K ), 7.8 keV (He-like Ni), and 8.2 keV (neutral Ni K ).
The molecular outflows in NGC 1068 and NGC 6240 are spatially resolved by Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA), and their published results gar14,sai18 are also listed.
NGC 1068 and NGC 6240 are located between the two types of flows.

sources.summary: The IceCube proceedings identify NGC 1068 as a nearby Seyfert II galaxy coincident with the strongest northern catalog neutrino excess and shown in a neutrino cluster map.

quotes.quote: The brightest neutrino source in the Northern Hemisphere sky coincides with the brightest catalog source, NGC 1068 (M77), a Seyfert II galaxy located at a distance of 14.4 Mpc.

The entire Northern source catalog is inconsistent with the isotropic background only hypothesis at 3.3 due to excesses in the directions of NGC 1068, and the blazars TXS 0506+056, PKS 1424+240, and GB6 J1542+6129.
Figure fig:pointsource shows the neutrino cluster seen by IceCube near NGC 1068 and the neutrino map from the southern sky combined analysis.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 1068 as a nearby Seyfert galaxy associated with an IceCube neutrino hot spot, arguing the hidden gamma-ray source points to accretion-corona rather than starburst production.

quotes.quote: In addition, the hottest spot of the IceCube point source search of high-energy neutrinos is associated to NGC 1068, a nearby Seyfert galaxy Aartsen:2019fau . If this neutrino signal is real, the neutrino flux is much higher than the upper limit of the TeV gamma-rays 2019ApJ...883..135A , which indicates that the neutrino source should be ``hidden'' in gamma-rays Murase:2015xka . This implies that the high-energy neutrinos should be produced at the accretion corona rather than the star-burst activities 2019ApJ...880...40I,Murase:2019vdl .

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 1068 met the PHANGS criteria, was initially excluded because of archival CO(3-2) mapping, and is now included with new CO(2-1) data.

quotes.quote: A final galaxy, NGC 1068, meets our criteria but was excluded from the Large Progrma due to the presence of archival CO (3-2) mapping GARCIABURILLO14 . New PHANGS CO (2-1) 7 m+TP mapping (PI: M. Querejeta) appear in this paper. We now formally include NGC 1068 in the main sample, bringing the main sample size to 75 galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1068's gamma-ray flux compatible with star-forming activity but says its possible IceCube neutrino excess may require AGN hot-corona activity.

quotes.quote: Finally, for NGC 1068, we find a good agreement both with the data and the SFR value given by . However, as will be discussed later, the corresponding neutrino emission predicted by our model is much lower than the one released by IceCube as a 2.9 excess Aartsen:2019fau . Therefore, while the gamma-ray flux could well be explained by the star-forming activity of the source, the explanation of its potential neutrino emission might require an intense AGN hot corona activity Inoue:2019yfs,Ajello:2020zna,Kheirandish:2021wkm,Anchordoqui:2021vms .

sources.summary: NGC 1068 may be a missed PHANGS outflow candidate; literature CO work finds a molecular outflow oriented along the inner stellar bar.

quotes.quote: Our study may have missed one potential outflow candidate among the PHANGS galaxies, namely NGC 1068, and for two of our candidates (NGC 1365, NGC 1672) high resolution CO (3--2) observations of the galaxy centers provided no clear evidence for molecular outflows.

GarciaBurillo2014 find strong evidence for an molecular outflow in NGC 1068 in CO (2--1) . The outflow is orientated along the inner stellar bar of the galaxy, as revealed by a careful modeling of the velocity distribution in their work.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 1068 as a nearby AGN and major IceCube neutrino hotspot, suggesting its near-Eddington accretion may fit the same particle-acceleration scenario as the studied flares.

quotes.quote: Accreting black holes have long been suggested as potential sources of high-energy particles fg08,murase_agn and this expectation was supported by the detection of a high-energy neutrino coincident (at the 3 level) with gamma-ray flaring from the blazar TXS 0506+056 txs_mm and from the nearby active galactic nucleus (AGN) NGC 1068 at the 4 level IceCube_NGC1068_22 .

This scenario might also provides an explanation for neutrino emission from NGC 1068, the most significant hotspot in the IceCube sky map at sub-PeV energies, detected at 4 post-trial Aartsen20,IceCube_NGC1068_22 . NGC 1068 is exceptional because it is the nearest example of the rare subset of AGN accreting near the Eddington limit Kawaguchi03,Lodato03 , similar to the accretion flares presented in this work.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1068 as a local Seyfert 2 with IceCube neutrino excesses and possible flare emission, grouped with other candidate neutrino sources.

quotes.quote: A search for point sources in the IceCube 10 year data collection, which includes events with energy typically 1 TeV, found an excess of neutrinos at the 2.9 level from the direction of the local ( z = 0.004 ) Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068 and a 3.3 excess in the northern sky due to significant p-values in the directions of NGC 1068 and three blazars: TXS 0506+056, PKS 1424+240, and GB6 J1542+6129 2020PhRvL.124e1103A .

A recent investigation aimed at the detection of flare neutrino emission in the 10 year IceCube data set reported a time-dependent neutrino excess in the northern hemisphere at the level of 3 associated with four sources: M87, a giant radio galaxy, TXS 0506+056 and GB6 J1542+6129, two IBL blazars, and NGC 1068.

A very recent search for neutrino flare emission in the IceCube 10 year data set Abbasi2021 reported possible flares at the level of 3 from NGC 1068, two IHBL blazars TXS 0506+056, GB6 J1542+6129, and M87, a giant radio galaxy with properties similar to that of IHBL objects that was already noticed as a possible counterpart of the track IceCube-141126A Giommidissecting .

sources.summary: The paper states that NGC 1068 has a complex star formation history, nuclear and extended hot dust emission, and stellar content dominated by young and intermediate-age populations.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068: This source has a very complex SFH, with the unresolved nuclear region being dominated by the HD component. Outside this region, large fractions of the HD are also observed. Its stellar content is dominated by young and intermediate age populations.

sources.summary: The review presents NGC 1068 as a prototypical Seyfert 2 where radio jets sweep inner-disk gas, producing a high-rate molecular outflow, edge-on torus, and obscuring dust cone.

quotes.quote: Frequently they can sweep out gas from the inner disk, as in NGC 1068 where the outflow rate reaches 63 M _ /yr (Garcia-Burillo et al. 2014, 2016). This prototypical Seyfert-2 reveals an edge-on molecular torus, perpendicular to the radio jet, and a molecular outflow, plus a hollow polar cone seen beautifully in polarised near-infrared light (Gratadour et al. 2015). The column density in the molecular torus and extended dust cone is sufficient to account for the BLR obscuration.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1068 as M 77, a ringed spiral Seyfert, deriving rotation curves and multi-band surface-brightness structure for the galaxy.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068 (M 77) is morphologically classified as a (R)SA(rs)b galaxy deVaucouleurs1991 for its outer rings structure and pronounced spiral arms.

Our modeled circular rotation for NGC 1068 is in agreement with the rotation derived using SAURON integral field spectrograph Emsellem2006 , the ^ 12 CO(1-0) velocity fields using the IRAM interferometer on Plateau de Bure, France Schinnerer2000 , and the rotation curve based on VLA HI data Brinks1997 to within 30 .
For NGC 1068, the modeling was done independently for B, V, and I band images, which yielded similar results for the Sérsic indices, effective radii, PA, and axis ratio.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1068 as a prototypical Seyfert II with hidden broad lines, Fermi-listed gamma-ray emission likely from starburst activity, and a reported neutrino excess.

quotes.quote: The seminal work by made this very clear, as it showed that NGC 1068, the prototypical Seyfert II galaxy, displayed narrow lines in total light but broad lines, like Seyfert Is, in polarised light.

A handful of Seyferts, including NGC 1068 and NGC 4945, are listed in the Fermi- 4FGL-DR3 catalogue 4LACDR3_2022 but their ray emission is thought to be related to starburst emission in their host galaxy and not to their SMBH Ajello_2020 .

This ties in with the 4.2 neutrino excess recently reported from the direction of the local ( z = 0.004 ) Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068 , an astrophysical source, which is very different from TXS 0506+056.

sources.summary: The paper uses IceCube's steady neutrino signal from active galaxy NGC 1068 to constrain neutrino self-interactions, emphasizing its event count, distance, and energy range.

quotes.quote: Recently, IceCube reported a significant detection of about 79 excess neutrinos from the active galaxy NGC 1068 among data taken from 2011 and 2020, with a best-fit power law spectral index 3.2 . For purposes of examining neutrino self-interactions, NGC 1068 has the advantage of a relatively large number of signal events and a spectrum that is assumed to be time-independent, in contrast with the flaring blazar. In contrast with SN1987a this signal is at higher energy ( 10^2 GeV vs. 1 - 10 MeV) allowing us to probe more massive mediators, it is more distant (at 14.4 Mpc versus 51.4 kpc), and it has a greater number of signal events (78 versus 22), possibly allowing more sensitivity to the coupling strength.

sources.summary: The paper links IceCube neutrinos from NGC 1068 to its central 50 pc, dusty torus, and status as a bright archetypical type II Seyfert galaxy.

quotes.quote: Gallagher Department of Astronomy, University of Wisconsin, Madison, WI, 53706 High-energy neutrinos are detected by the IceCube Observatory in the direction of NGC 1068, the archetypical type II Seyfert galaxy.

We report an analysis of the sub-millimeter, mid-infrared, and ultraviolet observations of the central 50 pc of NGC 1068 and suggest that the inner dusty torus and the region where the jet interacts with the surrounding interstellar medium (ISM) may be a potential neutrino production site.
NGC 1068 at a distance of 14.4 Mpc (1 = 60 pc) is the brightest Seyfert galaxy 1909LicOB...5...71F, 1997Ap&SS.248....9B .

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 1068 neutrino-emission evidence, measures its flux at 4.2 global significance, and projects the source significance at 4.5-5.5.

quotes.quote: The most recent time-integrated search in the Northern sky using 9 years of muon-neutrino track-like events found evidence for neutrino emission from the Seyfert II galaxy NGC 1068 at 4.2 .
Out of the same search on 110 objects, the flux from NGC 1068 was measured again using the new processing and analysis methods on 8.7 years of data with a global significance of 4.2 .
At the same time, the projected significance of the neutrino emission from NGC 1068, assuming it is a neutrino source, is expected to fall between 4.5 and 5.5 at a 68 confidence level.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1068 as a Seyfert II neutrino-emission case and reports that its direction contributes to Northern-sky p-value excesses, with L=4.41 in this analysis.

quotes.quote: In this pursuit, the IceCube Neutrino Observatory https://icecube.wisc.edu/ IceCubeJInst2017 has been playing a leading role for over a decade, from the detection of hundreds of astrophysical neutrinos with energies reaching above the PeV ( 10^ 15 eV), which marked the birth of high-energy (TeV -- PeV) neutrino astrophysics IceCube2013 , to the recently reported evidence for neutrino emission from the Seyfert II galaxy NGC 1068 IceCubeScience2022 .

Oikonomou_2022 . , based on the 3.3 excess in the Northern sky due to significant p-values in the directions of NGC 1068 and three blazars 10yearPS , namely TXS 0506+056, PKS 1424+240, and GB6 J1542+6129, with very high radio and ray powers, have suggested that blazars of the type IceCube has associated with neutrinos constitute at most 1 of the ray selected population. came exactly to the same conclusion using a completely different approach, namely by estimating the fraction of Fermi 4LAC-DR2 blazars compatible with the signal they detected.
We find L=4.41 for NGC 1068, L=2.70 for TXS 0506+056, L=3.15 for PKS 1424+240 and L=2.52 for GB6 J1542+6129.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068 as a Compton-thick AGN neutrino source, arguing that coronal radiation fields absorb hadronic rays and thermal protons cannot explain IceCube neutrinos.

quotes.quote: Recent observations of NGC 1068 show a significant neutrino flux IceCube-NGC1068 with a luminosity L_ 10^ 42 erg/s that dwarfs its expected ray counterpart aartsen+20,IceCube-NGC1068 .
Similar conditions are expected in the coronae of NGC 1068, a Compton thick AGN with a thin disk where matter obscures nuclear X-ray emission because of a large column density.

Our most robust conclusions are that i ) radiation fields in the corona lead to efficient absorption of hadronic rays within 100 r_g of the BH; ii ) protons accelerated from the coronal thermal pool cannot account for NGC 1068's neutrinos; ii ) the observed neutrinos stem from p interactions; iv ) explaining the neutrino signal requires injection of protons pre-accelerated to 10^4 , into the turbulent magnetically-dominated corona, where they are confined and reaccelerated.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 1068 as a prototypical Seyfert 2 with multiwavelength high-resolution observations, a compact cold molecular torus, and a warped, misaligned edge-on geometry.

quotes.quote: The prototypical Seyfert 2 NGC 1068 has been intensively observed, with high resolution at multi-wavelengths. While the near-infrared reveals clearly the hollow polar cone in warm dust Gratadour2015 , the cold component in the millimeter domain reveals a molecular torus of 7-10 pc in diameter Garcia-Burillo2016, Imanishi2020 . The torus is quasi edge-on, misaligned with the large-scale disk, warped, more inclined than the water maser disk, and may be suffers counter-rotation Impellizzeri2019, Imanishi2020 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1068 as an AGN MIR benchmark, noting hot central continuum, highly ionized neon lines, and a broad-band diagnostic signature typical of AGN emission.

quotes.quote: Such a continuum is found in the center of all galaxies in our sample known to be hosting an AGN such as NGC 1068, NGC 6814 or NGC 3147 ( ). Highly ionized species tracing the hard radiation field of the AGN (e.g. [ NeV ] 14.3 m and [ NeVI ] 7.6 m) are detected in NGC 1068.
Applying this criterion to the nucleus of M82, we clearly see that it is classified as a starburst while the nucleus of NGC 1068 has a typical AGN signature.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1068 as a barred group galaxy with complex, spatially extended X-ray emission whose soft and hard PSPC bands differ and require multi-component spectral modeling.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068 NGC 1068 is a member of a group Gar93 and contains a stellar bar Thr89 .
The X-ray emission from NGC 1068 has been studied in detail Wil92,Uen94,Iwa97,Net97 , and its spectrum is extremely complex.
NGC 1068 is clearly extended in X-ray emission, and the soft and hard PSPC bands are distinct.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1068 as an archetypal active galaxy whose X-ray spectrum constrains circumnuclear gas, permits detailed imaging spectroscopy, and gives a measured CNO emission-region temperature.

quotes.quote: kinkhabwala02a, brinkman02a and ogle02a have performed quantitative analyses of the X-ray spectra of the archetypal \ NGC 1068, and placed tight constraints on the column density and velocity distibution of the circumnuclear medium, as well as strict upper limits on the amount of collisionally ionized gas in this object.

In at least one object (NGC 1068), detailed imaging spectroscopy is possible with the \ grating spectrometers and the results are presented in brinkman02a and ogle02b , as well as in this Proceedings brinkman02b, ogle02b . The temperature of the H-like and He-like CNO emission region in NGC 1068 is measured to be kT 30,000 - 45,000 K kinkhabwala02a .

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 1068 has dust-powered infrared emission from clouds 23.6 pc from the center, needs high-velocity clouds for its X-rays, and lies in the Seyfert zone.

quotes.quote: NGC 1068, on the other hand, shows IR emission by dust from clouds with d/g=10 ^ 14 and =700 , located at 23.6 pc from the center.

Notice that NGC 1068, which has an H luminosity greater than the limit for LLAGN (10 ^ 40 ergs s ^ 1 ), does not follow the general trend (see Fig.2), since high velocity clouds at large distance from the center are required to account for the observed L _X (Table 2).
As expected, NGC 1068, included in the Halderson et al. sample, is located in the Seyfert galaxy zone.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1068 as the main comparison case, noting similar soft X-ray line and RRC spectra but lower ionic columns in NGC 4151's ionization cones.

quotes.quote: The soft X-ray spectrum of NGC 4151 is extremely similar to that of NGC 1068, both in terms of the atomic species present and in terms of the relative strengths of the observed emission lines and RRC (Kinkhabwala 2002), suggesting that the soft X-ray excesses observed in many Seyfert 2 galaxies may be composed of similar emission features. Modelling the RGS spectra in terms of emission from photoionized and photoexcited gas in an ionization cone reproduces all of the hydrogen-like and helium-like emission features observed in the soft X-ray spectrum of NGC 4151 in detail and confirms the correspondence between the soft X-ray emission in NGC 4151 and NGC 1068. NGC 4151 shows somewhat lower individual ionic column densities than those measured for NGC 1068 indicating that the material in the ionization cones of NGC 4151 may be somewhat more dense than the material in the ionization cones of NGC 1068.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC1068 as a prototype Seyfert 2 whose infrared spectrum separates AGN and starburst components and shows unusual FIR OH emission from dense nuclear clouds.

quotes.quote: The complete 2-200 m spectrum of the prototype Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC1068 has been decomposed in the AGN and starburst components. Moreover, in the FIR it shows the 79, 119 and 163 m OH rotational lines in emission, not in absorption as in every other galaxy yet observed. Modeling the three FIR lines of OH suggests the gas lies in small (0.1pc) and dense clouds ( 10^4 cm^ 3 ) most probably in the nuclear region (potentially a signature of the torus) (Spinoglio et al. 2005).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1068 as a Seyfert with enhanced nuclear HCN emission, a roughly 100 pc dense obscuring envelope, very high HCN/CO ratio, and kpc-scale jet or outflow activity.

quotes.quote: A striking enhancement of HCN(1 0) emission has been reported in the nuclear regions of some Seyfert galaxies such as NGC 1068 and NGC 5194 (Kohno et al. 1996 and references therein), implying extreme physical conditions of the molecular gas near active nuclei ( e.g. Matsushita et al. 1998).

We suggest that the observed R_ HCN/CO in Seyfert galaxies may be related to the size of dense obscuring material ; NGC 1068 and NGC 5194 have a relatively extended ( 100 pc scale) envelope of obscuring material, which consists of very dense interstellar medium and therefore shows extremely high R_ HCN/CO .
Note that the ``high R_ HCN/CO Seyferts'', i.e. NGC 1068, NGC 3079, and NGC 5194, possess kpc scale jet/outflows.

gmw_00000164.name: NGC 1097
gmw_00000164.overview: NGC 1097 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1905.03520, arXiv:2011.12592] hosting a low-luminosity Seyfert and LINER active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0911.4355, arXiv:astro-ph/0507071]. The nucleus is surrounded by a star-forming circumnuclear ring [arXiv:1004.5393, arXiv:2305.14437] and exhibits variable, double-peaked Balmer emission lines [arXiv:1612.06843, arXiv:2209.09037].

sources.summary: The paper contrasts NGC 1097 with NGC 1068, describing a barred disk galaxy whose inner spiral drives moderate, sustainable inflow that terminates before reaching the AGN.

quotes.quote: In one case, NGC 1068 ( ), the inflow rate is prodigious, due to chance events, and probably cannot be sustained. In the other, NGC 1097 ( ), the inflow is much more moderate and appears to be regulated in a way that is sustainable for timescales of a Gyr. Intriguingly, in neither case do we see gas flowing all the way down to the AGN: the inflow is terminated at spatial scales of a few to tens of parsecs, where it is instead expected to fuel a starburst.

Gas inflow in NGC 1097 NGC 1097 exhibits many classical features of disk galaxies: a large scale bar, a circumnuclear ring and starburst, an inner spiral, and a low luminosity AGN. Its one peculiarity is that the inner spiral has 3 photometric arms ( ). Integral field observations ( ) showed that this pattern is due to obscuration of stellar light by gas and dust in the molecular arms. Intriguingly, as seen in Fig. davies:fig:ngc1097 , the gas kinematics reveal a strong non-circular velocity residual in the form of a 2-arm spiral -- as expected from linear theory which indicates that the projected line-of-sight velocity pattern of an m arm spiral is an ( m-1 )-arm spiral. The properties of the arms allow one to derive an inflow rate along them of 1.2 M _ yr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 1097's AGN SED, treating infrared and UV measurements cautiously because compact and circumnuclear starbursts contaminate the nuclear emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 1097. -- The \ of the AGN in NGC 1097 and model fits to it are discussed by nemmen06 . Here, we adopted a subset of the data included in that paper. The IR measurements presented by prieto05 were taken through a very small aperture, which isolates the nucleus. However, the AGN is embedded in an compact, unresolved starburst storchi05 which may dominate the emission at these wavelengths, therefore, we have designated these measurements as upper limits to the flux of the AGN. We excluded measurements taken through large apertures since these were significantly contaminated by emission from the circumnuclear starburst ring.

A significant fraction of the nuclear UV flux appears to originate from a compact starburst, as suggested by absorption lines from hot stars detected in the \ spectrum storchi05,nemmen06 . By following the best-fitting \ model of nemmen06 , we adopt only the fraction of the UV flux that is attributed to the AGN. This model includes contributions from an inner, radiatively inefficient accretion flow, and outer, geometrically thin accretion disk, an obscured starburst and a jet.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes CO transitions in the central kiloparsec of NGC 1097, finding homogeneous circumnuclear gas properties, cold and warm molecular components, and X_CO values.

quotes.quote: We have used the \ conversion factor, Local Thermal Equilibrium and Large Velocity Gradient approximation to parametrize the cold and warm phase of the interstellar medium from five different low transitions of the CO molecule in the central 21 \ (kpc) region of NGC 1097. We have applied a one-component model and derived a typical kinetic temperature of about 33 K, a molecular Hydrogen density of 4.9 10^ 3 and a CO column density of 1.2 10^ 2 . A two-component model results in 85 cold-to-total gas fraction in the presence of a 90 K warm counterpart.

We have analysed single dish observations of five different transitions of the CO molecule in the central 21 \ (kpc) region of NGC 1097. We have used \ conversion factor, LTE and LVG approximation to parametrize the cold ( 30 K) phase of the interstellar gas from which we have identified and characterized the typical physical properties inside this region. All methods have resulted in comparable values, and in agreement with other estimations made by petitpas2003 and Hsieh2008 , who used a different set of line ratios and a different LVG approximation code. We obtained a typical kinetic temperature of about 33 K, a molecular Hydrogen density of 4.9 10^ 3 and a CO column density of 1.2 10^ 2 . We have further applied a scheme which involves the assumption that circumnuclear kpc region of NGC 1097 consists of two dominant components, a cold and a warm ( 90 K) component (cf.

sources.summary: NGC 1097 is presented as a nearby barred galaxy whose compressed bar fields, circumnuclear ring, and spiral ordered field can drive gas inflow toward its active nucleus.

quotes.quote: NGC 1097 (Fig. fig:n1097 ) is one of the nearest barred galaxies and hosts a huge bar of about 16 kpc length. The total and polarized radio intensities are strongest in the downstream region of the dust lanes (southeast of the center) by compression of turbulent fields in the bar's shock. The pattern of field lines in NGC 1097 is similar to that of the gas streamlines as obtained in numerical simulations (Athanassoula ). This suggests that the ordered magnetic field is aligned with the flow and amplified by shear. Remarkably, the optical image of NGC 1097 shows dust filaments in the upstream region which are aligned with the ordered field (Fig. fig:n1097 ).

The circumnuclear ring of NGC 1097 is bright in the optical, IR and radio spectral ranges. It has a diameter of about 1.5 kpc and an active nucleus in its center (Fig. fig:n1097_center ). The nonthermal and weakly polarized radio emission is a signature of strong turbulent magnetic fields (Beck et al. ).

sources.summary: The paper models new spectroscopy of NGC 1097's variable double-peaked H profile, attributing short-term changes to reverberation and a rotating spiral arm in an accretion disk.

quotes.quote: The broad (FWHM 10,000 km s ^ 1 ) double-peaked H profile from the LINER/Seyfert 1 nucleus of NGC 1097 was discovered in 1991, and monitored for the following 11 years. The profile showed variations attributed to the rotation of gas in a non-axisymmetric Keplerian accretion disk, ionized by a varying radiatively inefficient accretion flow (RIAF) located in the inner parts of the disk. We present and model 11 new spectroscopic observations of the double-peaked profile taken between 2010 March and 2011 March. This series of observations was motivated by the finding that in 2010 March the flux in the double-peaked line was again strong, becoming, in 2010 December, even stronger than in the observations of a decade ago.

We also discovered shorter timescale variations than in the previous observations: (1) the first, of 7 days, is interpreted as due to reverberation'' of the variation of the ionizing source luminosity, and the timescale of 7 days as the light crossing time between the source and the accretion disk; this new timescale and its interpretation provides a distance between the emitting gas and the supermassive black hole and as such introduces a new constraint on its mass; (2) the second, of 5 months, was attributed to the rotation of a spiral arm in the disk, which was found to occur on the dynamical timescale. We use two accretion disk models to fit theoretical profiles to the new data, both having non-axisymmetric emissivities produced by the presence of an one-armed spiral. Our modeling constrains the rotation period for the spiral to be 18 months. This work supports our previous conclusion that the broad double-peaked Balmer emission lines in NGC 1097, and probably also in other low-luminosity active nuclei, originate from an accretion disk ionized by a central RIAF.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1097 as a barred Seyfert 1 galaxy, reproducing its gas kinematics, bar, spiral arms, resonance radii, and an inward-migrating circumnuclear ring.

quotes.quote: We present the first galactic-scale model of the gas dynamics of the prototype barred Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 1097. We use large scale \ Fabry-Perot interferometric data covering the entire galactic disc and combine the distribution and kinematics maps with high resolution two-dimensional spectroscopy from the Gemini telescope. We build a dynamical model for the gravitational potential by applying the analytic solution to the equations of motion, within the epicyclic approximation. Our model reproduces all the significant kinematic and structural signatures of this galaxy. We find that the primary bar is 7.9 0.6  kpc long and has a pattern speed of 36 2   .

The circumnuclear star forming ring of NGC 1097 is located at 1 kpc which approximately coincides with the turnover radius see and Fig.  fig:rot right top panel 1979NZJS...22..325W . Hence, our results agree with this situation but place the circumnuclear ring between the two ILRs. This may point out an inward migration of the ring from the oILR.

sources.summary: NGC 1097 is modeled with a truncated thin disk, ADAF X-rays, radio jet emission, and possible unresolved old stellar contribution to its optical light.

quotes.quote: NGC 1097 The SED of this object was previously studied in detail by and the model displayed in Figure fig:n1097 corresponds to the model obtained by Nemmen06 . The transition radius is independently constrained from the modeling of the broad double-peaked H emission line ( r_ tr 225 ). Once the transition radius is fixed and requiring that the accretion rate in the outer thin disk is the same as in the outer parts of the ADAF, there is not much flexibility in the resulting thin disk spectrum; as such, the truncated thin disk spectrum self-consistently reproduces the HST optical data Nemmen06 . The jet emission accounts for the radio data point while the hard X-ray spectrum from the ADAF accounts for the Chandra observations. The jet emission is not able to reproduce the X-ray spectrum since it would require p<2 (see sec:fit ).

Our models do not take into account the contribution of the nuclear starburst in the UV. Therefore, they do not reproduce the ``UV bump'' between 1000 \ and 7000 \ (see Fig. 3 in Storchi-Bergmann05 ). Figure fig:n1097 also illustrates that we cannot rule out the possibility that the optical emission is produced by a unresolved, old stellar population located within 0.''1 with a mass 1.5 10^8 M_ (cf. sec:stars ).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1097 as an active-ring TIMER galaxy, finding a grand-design spiral with a nuclear ring, strong kinematic differences, young ring populations, and dust lanes.

quotes.quote: NGC 1097 is an SB(s)b galaxy and like NGC 5248 is a very well-studied grand-design spiral. It also harbours a 1 kpc-radius 2010MNRAS.402.2462C nuclear ring which has been observed in several ranges of the electromagnetic spectrum revealing the presence of numerous stellar clusters and a considerable amount of molecular gas e.g. 2019MNRAS.485.3264P .

In NGC 1097 we can clearly identify the shape of the ring, traced mostly by regions where the \ values are high. This ring spans a wide range of \ values, up to 70 km s^ 1 , while in the disc and nucleus the maximum variation is about 25 km s^ 1 .

In NGC 1097 and NGC 5248 we do not only find a well defined ring-like structure, but also relatively young stellar populations within them. This is especially noticeable in the ring of NGC 5248. In the two rings we estimate an average age below 4 Gyr ( 3.5 Gyr for NGC 1097 and 1.8 Gyr for NGC 5248). It is remarkable that in NGC 5248 we find a relatively small range of ages (from 0.5 Gyr to 5.0 Gyr) while in the ring of NGC 1097 it is much larger (from 0.8 Gyr to 10.7 Gyr), which clearly affects the obtained mean value.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1097 as a nearby southern Seyfert 1 SBb galaxy with a circumnuclear ring, nuclear bar, unresolved compact core, and dust/gas filaments feeding the AGN.

quotes.quote: NCC 1097 This is one of the few nearest Seyfert 1 objects in the South: 1 arcsec 70 pc. The galaxy is a SBb with a prominent circumnuclear ring at radius of 1.3 kpc enclosing a nuclear bar. Fig 2 shows a NACO Ks-band image showing all those features. At the resolution of this image, FWHM 0.14'' 0.19'' , the core of NGC1097 is unresolved which sets an upper limit to its size of 11 pc. The best detailed view of the core region is however seen in the NACO J-K image (Fig. 2, right).

It shows a complex network of filaments of dust and gas spiraling down towards the core. Some of the longest filaments seem to connect with the circumnuclear star forming ring. Presumably, material from the star forming regions is loosing angular momentum and falling straight to center to feed the AGN.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 1097 in radio continuum and polarization, finding bar-region radio ridges, compression/shear signatures, and a circumnuclear starburst ring with filamentary spiral structure.

quotes.quote: We present 3.5  cm and 6.2  cm radio continuum maps in total and polarized intensity of the barred galaxies NGC 1097 (at 2 -15 \ resolution) and NGC 1365 (at 9 -25 \ resolution). A previously unknown radio galaxy southwest of NGC 1097 is reported. Apart from a smooth faint envelope and a bright central region, both galaxies exhibit radio ridges roughly overlapping with the massive dust lanes in the bar region. The contrast in total intensity across the radio ridges is compatible with compression and shear of an isotropic random magnetic field, where the gas density compression ratio is approximately equal to 4 and the cosmic ray density is constant across the ridges. The contrast in polarized intensity is significantly smaller than that expected from compression and shearing of the regular magnetic field; this could be the result of decoupling of the regular field from the dense molecular clouds.

Both galaxies studied in this paper host a central starburst region. The circumnuclear ring of NGC 1097 is a prototype example of mass inflow and starburst ignition in a bar potential and has been studied in various spectral ranges (Gerin et al. \ , Barth et al. \ , Quillen et al. \ , P erez-Olea Colina \ , Kohno et al. \ ). In high-resolution infrared images, the ring is resolved into a complex network of filamentary spiral features (Prieto et al. \ ).

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1097 as an AGN whose inner gas and stellar kinematics become similar at small radii, implying that the nuclear gas and stars are physically mixed.

quotes.quote: NGC 1097 (Fig. dav:nearby:fig:n1097 ) is a clear example where this appears to be the case. The rotation curves of the gas and stars are completely different at radii >0.5^ . When interpreted with the dispersion, they indicate that the stars are in a spheroidal structure dominated by random motions (i.e. the bulge) whereas the gas is in a thin disk supported by ordered rotation. But at radii <0.5^ , the gas rotation curve drops rapidly and quickly becomes very similar to that of the stars. Similarly, the dispersion of the stars drops while that of the gas increases.
Thus the stellar and gas kinematics become remarkably similar at small radii. The only possible explanation is that in the nucleus, the gas and stars are physically mixed.

sources.summary: Herschel PACS imaging resolves NGC 1097 circumnuclear starburst ring, showing that it dominates far-IR emission and constraining dust heating and the inner AGN/starburst output.

quotes.quote: Using photometry of NGC 1097 from the Herschel PACS (Photodetector Array Camera and Spectrometer) instrument, we study the resolved properties of thermal dust continuum emission from a circumnuclear starburst ring with a radius 900 pc. These observations are the first to resolve the structure of a circumnuclear ring at wavelengths that probe the peak (i.e. 100 ) of the dust spectral energy distribution. The ring dominates the far-infrared (far-IR) emission from the galaxy---the high angular resolution of PACS allows us to isolate the ring's contribution and we find it is responsible for 75, 60 and 55 of the total flux of NGC 1097 at 70, 100 and 160 , respectively.

We compare the far-IR structure of the ring to what is seen at other wavelengths and identify a sequence of far-IR bright knots that correspond to those seen in radio and mid-IR images. The mid- and far-IR band ratios in the ring vary by less than 20 azimuthally, indicating modest variation in the radiation field heating the dust on 600 pc scales. We explore various explanations for the azimuthal uniformity in the far-IR colors of the ring including a lack of well-defined age gradients in the young stellar cluster population, a dominant contribution to the far-IR emission from dust heated by older ( > 10 Myr) stars and/or a quick smoothing of local enhancements in dust temperature due to the short orbital period of the ring. Finally, we improve previous limits on the far-IR flux from the inner 600 pc of NGC 1097 by an order of magnitude, providing a better estimate of the total bolometric emission arising from the active galactic nucleus and its associated central starburst.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 1097's central kiloparsec with AKARI near-infrared spectroscopy, describing a barred Seyfert spiral with a starburst ring and spatially varied PAH, aliphatic, and Br emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 1097 is a nearby barred spiral galaxy, which is categorized as Seyfert 1 with the starburst ring of 2 kpc in diameter and the primary and second bars of 15 kpc and 1 kpc in length, respectively ger88,sha93,sto93,koh03,hsi08 . Hence NGC 1097 is an ideal laboratory to study the ISM in galaxies showing both active galactic nucleus and circumnuclear starburst activities.

We presented the result of the near-infrared (2.5--5.0 m) spectroscopy of central 1 kpc area of NGC 1097 with the AKARI /IRC. From all the regions of the starburst ring, we detected the PAH 3.3 m feature and the aliphatic 3.4--3.6 m sub-features as well as the Br 4.05 m emission. They exhibit spatial distributions significantly different from each other, indicating that local conditions of the ISM vary much from region to region around the ring. The PAH 3.3 m emission shows a relatively uniform distribution as compared with the other two components, which indicates that they are not dominantly heated by young stars in active regions of the ring, but rather by old stars distributed widely over the central region. In particular, the aliphatic features are enhanced near the bar connecting the ring with the nucleus, where the structure of hydrocarbon grains seems to be relatively disordered.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 1097 as a barred spiral whose magnetic fields trace shearing bar flows and whose circumnuclear ring field can drive gas inflow feeding the active nucleus.

quotes.quote: The magnetic field lines in NGC 1097, one of the closest and brightest barred galaxies (Fig. fig:n1097 ), follow a pattern that resembles the flow of cold gas in a bar potential. As the gas rotates faster than the bar pattern of a galaxy, a shock occurs in the cold gas, which has a small sound speed, while the flow of warm, diffuse gas is only slightly compressed but sheared. The ordered field is also hardly compressed, probably coupled to the diffuse gas and strong enough to affect its flow beck05c .

NGC 1097 hosts a bright ring with about 1.5 kpc diameter and an active nucleus in its center (Fig. fig:n1097central ). The ordered field in the ring has a spiral pattern and extends to the nucleus. The orientation of the innermost spiral field agrees with that of the spiral dust filaments visible on optical images. Magnetic stress in the circumnuclear ring due to the strong total magnetic field (about 50 G) can drive gas inflow balbus98 at a rate of several solar masses per year, which is sufficient to fuel the activity of the nucleus beck05c .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1097 as a barred galaxy with strong inner attenuation and a nuclear starburst, making its integrated estimator behavior deviate from an exponential-profile assumption.

quotes.quote: We have therefore selected eight galaxies in our sample: NGC 628, NGC 925, NGC 1097, NGC 3351, NGC 4321, NGC 4736, NGC 5055, and NGC 7793. Properties As we can see in Table tab:sample , the sample is made of fairly regular star--forming galaxies in the nearby universe, spanning the Hubble sequence from Sab to Sd. This ensures different levels of star-forming activity (from sSFR=-10.76 yr ^ 1 for NGC 4736 to sSFR=-9.59 yr ^ 1 for NGC 7793) and different levels of diffuse dust emission sauvage1992a .

Conversely, NGC 1097 and NGC 3351 have a very strong attenuation in the inner regions but more moderate values further out in the disk. The higher attenuation is probably due to the high concentration of molecular gas and dust in the central parts of these two well--known barred galaxies.

The nuclear starbursts in NGC 1097 and NGC 3351 present very strong peaks compared to the typical star formation across the rest of the disk. Such nuclear starbursts are expected due to the central gas concentration in barred galaxies e.g., tabatabaei2013a .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1097 as a barred galaxy whose bar spans the modeled pitch-angle range, yet the outermost pitch angle and mean-field strength agree reasonably.

quotes.quote: Finally, Group III consists of two barred galaxies, NGC 1097 and NGC 1365, with bars as large as about 10 and 11 , respectively, thus extending into most (NGC 1365) or the whole (NGC 1097) of the range of galactocentric distances where the pitch angle of the mean magnetic field is known Beck+05 . The structure of the mean magnetic field in and near the bar is affected by the associated strongly nonaxisymmetric gas flow Moss+01,Moss+07 , so we do not expect our dynamo model, designed for axially symmetric galaxies, to be very successful at explaining such fields.

NGC 1097 and NGC 1365 The dynamo model used here is axially symmetric, so we do not expect it to be accurate in the case of the barred galaxies NGC 1097 and NGC 1365 in the sample. It is reassuring that the dynamo model performs better in the case of Group I and II galaxies. Since deviations from axial symmetry are stronger in the bar region, it is understandable that the disagreement between the axisymmetric dynamo model and observations is greater at small galactocentric distances in the two barred galaxies. In NGC 1097, the pitch angle for the outermost radial range is reproduced by the model to within the observational uncertainty. Likewise, the solution for the mean magnetic field strength in this annulus is within a factor of two of the observed value.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1097 as a low-luminosity, changing-look, double-peaked AGN whose broad H variability is fit by a TDE model, supporting disrupted debris as the Balmer-line material.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage In the Letter, interesting evidence is reported to support a central tidal disruption event (TDE) in the known AGN NGC 1097. Considering the motivations of TDE as one probable origination of emission materials of double-peaked broad emission lines and also as one probable explanation to changing-look AGN, it is interesting to check whether are there clues to support a TDE in NGC 1097, not only a changing-look AGN but also an AGN with double-peaked broad emission lines. Under the assumption that the onset of broad H emission was due to a TDE, the 13years-long (1991-2004) variability of double-peaked broad H line flux in NGC 1097 can be well predicted by theoretical TDE model, with a (1-1.5) M_ main-sequence star tidally disrupted by the central BH with TDE model determined mass about (5-8) 10^7 M_ .

Fortunately, there is an AGN, the low luminosity AGN NGC 1097, which has been classified as a double-peaked AGN and also as a changing-look AGN. Changing-look properties of NGC 1097 can be confirmed by the following spectroscopic features: no broad emission lines before 1990 as reported in wn86 but apparently double-peaked broad emission lines in the 1990s and in early 2000s as discussed in sb93, sn03 .

Under the assumption of double-peaked broad emission lines coming from TDE related debris, theoretical TDE model with a 1-1.5 M_ main-sequence star disrupted by the central BH with TDE model determined mass about (5-8) 10^7 M_ can be well applied to predict the 13years-long (1991-2004) variability of double-peaked broad H line fluxes in the well-known low luminosity AGN NGC 1097, providing interesting evidence to support a central TDE in NGC 1097 which has been classified as both a changing-look AGN and a double-peaked AGN. Therefore, NGC 1097 is so-far the best candidate to support the TDE related origin of double-peaked broad Balmer emission materials and to support TDE as the physical explanation to properties of changing-look AGN.

sources.summary: The paper applies MUSE gas-kinematic methods to NGC 1097, describing its barred nuclear ring, low-luminosity AGN, companion interaction, and coherent nuclear shocks.

quotes.quote: Using VLT/MUSE integral-field spectroscopic data for the barred spiral galaxy NGC 1097, we explore techniques that can be used to search for extended coherent shocks that can drive gas inflows in centres of galaxies. Such shocks should appear as coherent velocity jumps in gas kinematic maps, but this appearance can be distorted by inaccurate extraction of the velocity values and dominated by the global rotational flow and local perturbations like stellar outflows. This new method successfully reveals the presence of continuous shocks in the regions inward from the nuclear ring of NGC 1097, in agreement with nuclear spiral models.

NGC 1097 is an SBb galaxy with a strong bar at position angle 141 Prieto_05 and it contains a prominent star-forming nuclear ring in the inner 8 -10 Bittner_20 . The galaxy hosts a LINER LINER: Low Ionization Narrow Emission-line Regions, can be distinguished from the Seyfert type nuclei based on their [ O III ] luminosity and kinematics Kewley_06 type, low-luminosity active galactic nucleus LLAGN -- L_ bol 10^ 42 erg s^ 1 , Ho_08 with bolometric luminosity L_ bol =8.6 10^ 41 erg s^ 1 Nemmen_06 . The galaxy is interacting with a companion elliptical galaxy NGC 1097A located to its northwest. Because of its complex characteristics, NGC 1097 has been a prominent focus of many studies Quillen_95, Prieto_05, Fathi_06, Davies_09,vandeVen_Fathi_2010,Lin_13, Bowen_16, Izumi_17, Prieto_19, Legodi_21 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1097 as a nearby barred LINER/Seyfert galaxy whose near-infrared central spiral, nuclear ring, shocks, and unresolved nucleus constrain gas inflow toward the active nucleus.

quotes.quote: Near-infrared images of the prototype LINER / Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 1097 observed with the Very Large Telescope (VLT) using adaptive optics disclose with unprecedented detail a complex central network of filamentary structure spiralling down to the center of the galaxy. The structure, consisting of several spiral arms, some almost completing a revolution about the center, is most prominent within the radius of about 300 pc. Gas and dust may be channelled to the center of NGC 1097 along this central spiral. Some filaments can be traced further out, where they seem to connect with the nuclear star-forming ring at 0.7 kpc radius.

NGC 1097 is a SBb galaxy with an Active Galactic Nucleus (AGN), located at a distance of 14.5 Mpc (Tully, 1988; 1 arcsec   70 pc), which places it among the nearest AGN in the Southern Hemisphere. It has been originally classified as a LINER and further reclassified as a Seyfert type 1 (Sy 1) nucleus after the detection of broad, double peaked H emission by Storchi-Bergmann et al. (1993). However, it is very moderate AGN with L_ 2-10 keV 10^ 40 (Terashima et al. 2002).

sources.summary: The review uses NGC 1097 as an LLAGN example whose double-peaked Balmer line and optical/UV continuum both require a truncated thin disk at about 252 Schwarzschild radii.

quotes.quote: Fig. 5 shows another example of LLAGNs---NGC 1097. This source is also well known due to its double-peaked Balmer line. It has been known for a long time that to fit such a kind of line profile the thin disk must be truncated (Chen Halpern 1989). For NGC 1097, the innermost radius of the thin disk obtained is R_ tr =252 R_s (Storchi-Bergmann et al. 2003).

On the other hand, to fit the continuum spectrum at optical/UV band, we again require that the thin disk must be required. Although the value of the transition radius is hard to be determined as precisely as in the case of fitting the line profile, it is shown that the value of R_ tr =252 R_s can fit the optical/UV spectrum satisfactorily (Nemmen et al. 2006). So for this source, like XTE J1118+480, both two independent observations require a truncated thin disk and give the same value of the transition radius.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 1097 as a LINER/Seyfert galaxy with nuclear starburst signatures and inner-kiloparsec ionized-gas residuals tracing spiral inflow.

quotes.quote: Using HST-STIS spectra, Storchi-Bergmann et al. (2005) have found absorption lines from O and B stars in the nuclear spectrum of the LINER/Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 1097, within an aperture corresponding to a projected distance of only 9 pc at the galaxy. Fig. UVspec shows the nuclear UV spectrum of NGC 1097 as compared with those of starburst galaxies, showing absorption lines originated in the atmospheres of O and B stars, evidencing that the spectral features are characteristic of a starburst. Fig. sbcont shouws that, besides the absorption features, the continuum is also reproduced by the spectral distribution of a young starburst, confirming the interpretation based on the UV spectrum.

The first results of this project were published in Fathi et al. (2006) for the galaxy NGC 1097, in which we mapped the H and [ NII ] 6584 velocity fields within the inner kiloparsec of the galaxy. Although the kinematics is dominated by rotation in a plane, the subtraction of an exponential rotating disk model fitted to the data revealed residuals which traced the spiral arms seen both in an HST image and in near-IR images (Prieto et al. 2005). Notice that the nucleus of NGC 1097 is a LINER, with low activity, and this is probably the reason why we can see the inflows: because the outflows are weak or absent.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1097 as a barred spiral with a Seyfert nucleus, circumnuclear ring, nonthermal bar emission, and shock-compressed gas and magnetic fields.

quotes.quote: NGC 1097 , in the optical is a spectacular barred spiral galaxy with prominent dust lanes in the bar. The central bar extends for about 20 kpc and then continues into the two optical spiral arms. The galaxy has an active Seyfert nucleus, and a circumnuclear ring of about 1.5 kpc diameter.

NGC 1097: Our 333 MHz map (see Figure figure1 ) has an angular resolution of 16 11 , which corresponds to a spatial resolution of 1.1 kpc, if the galaxy is at a distance of 14 Mpc. The only other low frequency radio continuum study of this galaxy was done by and at 1465 MHz. They reported strong radio emission coincident with the narrow dust lanes in the bar. The central bar region is also clearly visible in our 333 MHz map, and is coincident with dust emission inferred from the far infra-red 24 m images from Spitzer , as seen in Figure 7. The bar pattern rotates in the clockwise direction (see ondre89 ), and as expected prominent shock fronts are visible on the leading side of the bar with respect to the sense of rotation.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes dense molecular gas streaming in NGC 1097, linking its bar, ring, nuclear spirals, and ALMA HCN kinematics to inflow toward the Seyfert nucleus.

quotes.quote: We present a kinematic analysis of the dense molecular gas in the central 200 parsecs of the nearby galaxy NGC 1097, based on Cycle 0 observations with the Atacama Large Millimeter/sub-millimeter Array (ALMA). We use the HCN(4--3) line to trace the densest interstellar molecular gas ( n_ H _2 10^8 ), and quantify its kinematics, and estimate an inflow rate for the molecular gas. We find a striking similarity between the ALMA kinematic data and the analytic spiral inflow model that we have previously constructed based on ionized gas velocity fields on larger scales.

The central region of the nearby barred Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 1097 displays a number of intriguing morphological and kinematic features. At 1 kpc, an almost circular ring-like feature marks the transition between the prominent R 8 kpc galactic bar and the relatively diffuse region interior to the ring. The bar hosts two prominent dust lanes, both originating at around the Corotation radius of the bar npf13 , cutting through the inner ring and transforming into nuclear spirals that continue down to 3.5 pc distance from the active nucleus lou01,f06 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1097 as a nearby Seyfert 1 laboratory where ALMA maps molecular species across the central 2 kpc, separating AGN chemistry from the star-forming ring.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: This position matches the measured position of the nucleus at 6 cm _ J2000 =02^h46^m18.96^s , _ J2000 =-30^ 16'28.9' ; Hummel1987

quotes.quote: The nearby Sy 1 galaxy NGC 1097 represents an ideal laboratory to explore the molecular chemistry in the presence and surroundings of an active galactic nucleus (AGN). Exploring the distribution of different molecular species allows us to understand the physical processes affecting the ISM both in the AGN vicinity as well as in the outer star forming molecular ring. We carried out 3 mm ALMA observations which include seven different molecular species, namely HCN, HCO ^+ , CCH, CS, HNCO, SiO, HC _3 N, and SO as well as the ^ 13 C isotopologues of the first two. Spectra were extracted from selected positions and all species were imaged over the central 2 kpc ( 30'' ) of the galaxy at a resolution of 2.2'' 1.5'' ( 150 pc 100 pc ).

CCH, showing the largest variations across NGC 1097, is suggested to be a good tracer of both obscured and early stage star formation. HNCO, SiO and HC _3 N are significantly enhanced in the inner circumnuclear disk (CND) surrounding the AGN.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1097 as the lone sample galaxy with shrinking-disc behavior, connecting its disturbed morphology, large disc scalelength, companion interaction, and likely merger state.

quotes.quote: For only one galaxy in our sample, NGC 1097, we clearly find the signature of a shrinking of the disc. It is interesting to notice, a posteriori , that this galaxy has a very disturbed morphology. This is likely due to a strong interaction with the companion NGC 1097A Note that NGC1097A cannot be seen in our online Atlas, since it has been masked out from our 3.6 m map. .

We also noted that NGC 1097 is the object with the largest derived disc scalelength (6.32 kpc) and we verified that this result is not changed if we exclude from the exponential fit the whole radial range occupied by NGC 1097A. NGC 1097 has also been suggested to have undergone other significant interactions in the recent past ( HigdonWallin2003 ). If our interpretation of a strong interaction state, likely a merger, for this system, is correct, then there is no surprise that it behaves differently from the regular evolution of isolated galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper presents KAT-7 spectropolarimetry of NGC 1097, a barred Sy1 spiral with a circumnuclear starburst ring and the most complex Faraday profile among the disk galaxies.

quotes.quote: NGC 1097 is identified as a Sy1 galaxy StorchiBergmann1997 with a circumnuclear star-burst ring Gerin1988 . It is a barred spiral, SBb, with its radio emission dominated by the circumnuclear ring and nucleus Beck2005 . The magnetic properties of NGC 1097 have been studied using narrow band ( 50 MHz) high resolution radio data ( 2^ 15^ resolutions) at 4.8 and 8.4 GHz, with the NRAO Very Large Array, VLA Beck2002,Beck2005 .

NGC 1097 : Displays the most complex Faraday depth profile amongst the two disk galaxy sources -- Figure NGC1097-rmclean , with thick components at low and some faint components just above the threshold at large | | > 500 rad m ^ 2 (particularly near 520, \ 590, and 790 rad m ^ 2 ). The most prominent component seems Faraday thick, with minor components spanning the range = -11 to 32 rad m ^ 2 . This source is classified as Faraday complex and displays the largest dispersion in Faraday space with _ = 120.2 rad m ^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1097 as a barred galaxy with a molecular ring, dust-lane shocks, and CO-traced magnetic fields bent into the circumnuclear ring and secondary bar.

quotes.quote: Circumnuclear region of NGC 1097: (1) A molecular ring in the nucleus and dust lanes in the bar: As an efficient driver of gas inflow, a strong bar with prominent dust lanes along the bar is present in NGC 1097. The orbiting gas loses angular momentum at the dust-lane shocks and falls toward the galaxy center . Despite the high density of gas due to shock compression, the formation of molecular clouds and stars are suppressed in the bar because of the strong shear along the bar . The inflowing gas settles in the nuclear ring (see Fig. ngc1097 ), serving as the raw material for molecular cloud and star formation when the velocity shear is mitigated.

We also see that the magnetic fields within the ring have a radial component (Fig. PM in the Supplementary) following the secondary bar of NGC 1097 reported by the ref. . The magnetic fields threading the secondary bar impose a magnetic braking effect on the gas spiraling into the innermost region and further remove its angular momentum, as suggested in the ref. .

sources.summary: The paper derives bar-driven molecular gas inflow in NGC 1097, a strongly barred galaxy with a star-forming nuclear ring and well-defined gas-rich bar lanes.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we build upon the methods of and and derive the bar-driven mass inflow rate in the barred galaxy NGC 1097 using PHANGS-ALMA CO data. NGC 1097 e.g. Hummel1987,Barth1995 is a strongly barred galaxy which hosts an intensely star-forming nuclear ring (SFR  1.8 2 ; Sandstrom2010,Hsieh2011,Prieto2019,Lopez-Rodriguez2021,Song2021 ) with a radius of 700 . NGC 1097 is an ideal candidate to determine the inflow rate thanks to its proximity which means high resolution (spatial resolution of 110 pc, see Section  sec:data ) and for having clearly defined gas-rich bar lanes.

We assume that NGC 1097 has a position angle of 122.4 ^ and an inclination of 48.6 ^ Lang2020 . We take =-21.6 km s^ 1 ^ 1 as our fiducial value of the bar pattern speed Lin2013 . This value is obtained by matching hydrodynamical simulations to the observed morphology of NGC 1097.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1097 magnetic-field observations, reproducing large-scale regular-field features and interpreting ridges, polarization offsets, and weak dust-lane enhancement through enhanced turbulent diffusion.

quotes.quote: Our work is largely motivated by recent high quality VLA radio observations of the barred galaxy NGC 1097, and we compare our results in detail with the regular magnetic fields deduced from these observations. We are able to reproduce most of the conspicuous large-scale features of the observed regular field, including the field structure in the central regions, by using a simple mean-field dynamo model in which the intensity of interstellar turbulence (more precisely, the turbulent diffusivity) is enhanced by a factor of 2--6 in the dust lanes and near the circumnuclear ring.

For NGC 1097, velocity data with sufficient resolution are not available. The total nonthermal radio emission, a tracer of the total (regular+turbulent) magnetic field, exhibits ridges near the dust lanes (see also Ondrechen van der Hulst 1983) that occur because of the strong gas compression downstream of the bar's major axis (A92).

Moreover, the polarization intensity (a measure of the strength of the regular magnetic field) in NGC 1097 has a broad local maximum upstream of the dust lanes, where gas density has deep minimum. The regular magnetic field shows hardly any enhancement in the dust lanes.
