sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1097 as a peculiar barred spiral with a morphologically peculiar nucleus, an annulus of radio H II hot spots, ring star formation, and Seyfert 1 nuclear emission.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy, NGC 1097, is a peculiar barred spiral listed in Arp's Catalogue (1966). Sersic (1973) noted that the galaxy nucleus was morphologically peculiar with a central condensation, surrounded by an annulus of hot spots, almost uniformly and symmetrically distributed (Fig. annulus ). Wolstencroft et al. (1984) found that this structure was composed of H II regions emitting at radio wavelengths. The observations suggest that a burst of star formation is taking place in the ring.

This is supported also by the discovery of the core--collapse SN 1992bd in this region (Fig. annulus ). The nucleus of the galaxy is a compact radio source and shows the emission line spectrum of a Seyfert 1 (Storchi--Bergmann et al., 1997).

sources.summary: The paper reports a previously uncatalogued radio galaxy southwest of NGC 1097, giving its nucleus coordinates, component fluxes, spectral behavior, southern jet steepening, and non-detection in polarization.

quotes.quote: A radio galaxy is visible about 90 \ southwest of the nucleus of NGC 1097 (Figs.  n1097s6 and   radiogal ). It is not included in the NED and NVSS catalogues. The position of the nucleus (which is still unresolved at 2 \ resolution) is RA, DEC(J2000) = 02 46 12.75, 30  17 05.3, its flux density is 753 10 Jy at 3.5  cm, and its spectrum is flat (with a spectral index +0.1 ) which is typical of an active galactic nucleus.

The northern component is located at RA, DEC(J2000) = 02 46 12.7, 30  17 01, its flux density is 283 36 Jy at 3.5  cm, and its spectum is steep ( 0.5 ). The southern jet shows a spectral steepening from +0.1 to 1.5 with increasing distance from the nucleus. No polarized emission was detected from any component.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1097's central star cluster, AGN, circumnuclear star-forming ring, warm dust emission, and weak central PAH emission, arguing the dust is mainly heated by the nuclear star cluster.

quotes.quote: The nucleus of the nearby galaxy, NGC 1097, is known to host a young, compact ( < 9 parsec) nuclear star cluster as well as a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus (AGN). It has been suggested both that the nuclear stellar cluster is associated with a dusty torus, and that low-luminosity AGN like NGC 1097 do not have the torus predicted by the unified model of AGN. To investigate these contradictory possibilities we have acquired Gemini/T-ReCS 11.7 m and 18.3 m images of the central few hundred parsecs of this galaxy at < 45 parsec angular resolution, in which the nucleus and spectacular, kiloparsec-scale star-forming ring are detected in both bands.

The small-scale mid-infrared (mid-IR) luminosity implies thermal emission from warm dust close to the central engine of this galaxy. Fitting of torus models shows that the observed mid-IR emission cannot be accounted for by dust heated by the central engine. Rather, the principal source heating the dust in this object is the nuclear star cluster itself, suggesting that the dust that we detect is not the torus of AGN unified schemes (although it is also possible that the dusty starburst itself could provide the obscuration invoked by the unified model).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 1097 emission-line kinematics, finding a nuclear spiral, weak two-arm gravitational perturbation, and inflow rate consistent with accretion onto its LINER/Seyfert nucleus.

quotes.quote: Application to the emission-line velocity field within the circumnuclear starforming ring of NGC 1097, obtained with the \ spectrograph, reveals a three arm spiral in the non-circular motions, which agrees with a two arm dust spiral in the surface brightness. This nuclear spiral is consistent with a weak perturbation in the gravitational potential due to a two-arm logarithmic spiral, with a pitch angle of 52 4 \ derived directly from the harmonic expansion of the velocity field. Next, we use a simple spiral perturbation model to constrain the fraction of the measured non-circular motions that is due to radial inflow.

This corresponds to a fraction of about 4.2 10^ 3 of the Eddington mass accretion rate onto the central black hole in NGC 1097, and is fully consistent with previous mass accretion models fitted to the observed spectral energy distribution in the nucleus of this LINER/Seyfert1 galaxy. We find that the three-arm spiral structure in the non-circular motions is consistent with a weak two-arm spiral perturbation of the gravitational potential, and use the model derived in Appendix app:gasorbits to constrain the inflow rate as function of distance from the center of NGC 1097.

sources.summary: The BLAST paper describes NGC 1097 as barred, spiral-armed, and ray-like, with a Seyfert/LINER nucleus and starburst nuclear ring; BLAST resolved its bar and spiral arms.

quotes.quote: NGC 1097 Features of NGC 1097, presented in Fig. fig:ngc1097 , include a bar 20 kpc in length that tapers off into two spiral arms beck2005 , as well as several fringe structures called jets or `rays' wolstencroft1975,lorre1978 . NGC 1097 has an active Seyfert 1/LINER nucleus, which is considered to have a low luminosity mason2007 , and is surrounded by a 1.5 kpc diameter nuclear ring, containing 1.1 10^9 M _ of molecular gas hsieh2008,gerin1988 . Thermal dust heating in the nuclear ring is dominated by a starburst mason2007 with a stellar production rate of 5 M _ yr ^ 1 hummel1987 . BLAST made two observations of a 0.25 \ area centered on NGC 1097, with a total integration time of 52 minutes. BLAST clearly resolves the bar and spiral arms of the galaxy, but has insufficient resolution to resolve the 1.5 kpc star forming ring.

sources.summary: The paper measures NGC 1097's equipartition magnetic field, finding an 18 G central field, lower northern-bar field strength, and weaker enhancement than earlier estimates.

quotes.quote: Comparison with existing studies NGC 1097 was studied in polarization at high resolution that revealed magnetic field in the bar to be aligned with the gas streamlines and thus a good tracer of gas flow beck99 . Strong radio emission is detected from the bar at 90 cm basu12a , however, due to poor resolution of 20 cm maps ( 40 arcsec), the enhancement of magnetic field in the bar is only about 10--15 percent higher than the disk. In this study, the field at the center is found to be 18 G and decreases to 10 G towards the edge. The field in the northern bar is found to be lower than that in the southern bar with B_ eq 9.8 G and 12.2 G respectively. Our estimated field is lower than what was estimated by beck05ea perhaps due to their assumption of 500 pc of synchrotron emitting region.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1097 as a radiation-dominated AGN example with small jets, a large-scale bar, no small-scale bar, and a ring of star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: For NGC 1097 and NGC 1386, the radiation dominates. The observations by Mezcua show that these sources have small jets, whereas the other sources have larger jets. Interestingly both NGC 1097 and NGC 1386 have a bar at large scales, but they show no evidence of a bar on small scales. They both also have a ring of star-forming regions. This indicates that the morphology of the galaxies will play a key role for future studies.

sources.summary: NGC1097 has a weak variable active nucleus, broad NaD absorption, a faint ENLR outside its star-forming ring, and evidence for radial inflow feeding activity.

quotes.quote: Broad NaD absorption is evident around the nucleus. The nucleus displays a high velocity dispersion with very strong . A faint ENLR re-appears outside the star-forming ring.

A study by Fathi06 using the GMOS-IFU as well as high resolution HST-ACS observations shows that the nuclear activity appears to be fed by radial flow from the star-forming ring towards the nucleus. The nuclear activity is variable, and the galaxy has been classified as a Seyfert 1 on the basis of broad H when more active Storchi-Bergmann97,Storchi-Bergmann03 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1097 as a monitored low-luminosity AGN with variable double-peaked Balmer emission explained by a rotating spiral arm in an accretion disk.

quotes.quote: In the particular case of NGC 1097, during the  30 years it has been spectroscopically monitored SB03,Schimoia12 , the relative intensity of the blue and red peaks has alternated between a stronger blue peak and a stronger red peak on timescales of months. This beheavior has been attributed to a spiral arm rotating in the disk, with a rotation period of about 18 months.

In order to search for possible shorter timescale variations, and to try to identify the driving source of the line variations, we subsequently monitored the double-peaked H profile in the nuclear spectrum of NGC 1097 with the SOAR telescope and the Swift satelite Schimoia14 . These observations confirmed that the integrated flux of the line and the velocity separation betwween the blue and red peaks vary on a timescale as short as 5 days. This timescale is compatible with the light-crossing time between the center of the disk and a typical radius of 1000 R_ g , where the emission line originates, according to our models.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1097 as an Sb nuclear-ring galaxy with high ring-mass fraction, available H-alpha and infrared SFR measurements, and the most massive ring in the sample.

quotes.quote: Six of our sample galaxies (NGC 1512, NGC 1097, NGC 3351, NGC 4314, NGC 6951, and NGC 7217) were observed through both H and infrared (3.6 m and 8 m) filters, so that we could determine their ring SFRs.

Of particular interest in this figure is that all Sb-type ( T=3 ) galaxies, i.e. NGC 1097, NGC 1672, NGC 3351, and NGC 7742, have the highest stellar ring-mass fraction with respect to the stellar mass of their host galaxies.

Our ring masses derived from broad-band SED fitting span a large range of more than two orders of magnitude, from 1.37 10^7 M_ for NGC 1512 to 8.5 10^9 M_ for NGC 1097. YMC samples with ages of up to 10 Myr nevertheless account for merely small fractions of the total ring masses. This is why more massive rings tend to have lower YMC fractions.

sources.summary: The paper contrasts NGC 1097 with M83, describing its circumnuclear ring as chemically less variable, with star formation seemingly independent of galactic orbits and possible weak cloud-collision variation.

quotes.quote: As mentioned earlier, NGC 1097 is an example where star formation seems independent from the galactic orbits. 2015A&A...573A.116M studied the circumnuclear ring of NGC 1097 for the spatial abundance variation of such species as SiO, CCH, HCN, HCO ^+ , HNCO, CS, SO, and HC _3 N. Unlike our results in M83, there is no large variation of the chemical composition in the circumnuclear ring of NGC 1097 except for CCH. This difference may be caused by the presence of collision-induced star formation in M83, although we caution that a similar large scale chemical variation due to cloud collision may be present also in NGC 1097 at a low level below the sensitivity of their cycle 0 observations. In the case of NGC 1097, the scenario by 2013ApJ...769..100S may be relevant because of the high gas inflow rate.

sources.summary: The paper varies NGC 1097 spline decomposition to test PAH-band robustness and later classifies it as a starburst/Seyfert 1 galaxy with prominent A-class PAH bands.

quotes.quote: Nevertheless, we varied the spline decomposition of the galaxy NGC 1097 and analysed possible differences in the results. The central wavelength and FWHM (full width at half maximum) remains practically the same with less than 1 per of variation. The amplitude can vary up to 20 per cent for the 6.2 and 8.6 m bands. The 7.6 and 7.8 m features are less affected and their flux ratio varied up to 5 per cent, which is lower than the respective uncertainty of their ratio.

The starburst galaxy Mrk 52 and NGC 1097 (starburst and Seyfert 1 galaxy) received the same ``A'' classification for the three bands (6.2, 7.7 and 8.6 m) and present prominent PAH features, despite of the predominance of the 7.6 m component.

sources.summary: NGC 1097 is used as a bar-driven star-formation example where middle-age clusters trace the bar, including a parallel string interpreted as a relic of past star formation.

quotes.quote: Another common feature of galaxies with bar-driven star formation is that middle-age clusters are found near the young cluster hot-spots, as well as throughout the bar (e.g., NGC 1672, NGC 2903, NGC 1097). Comparison with the distribution of the old globular clusters, which are more uniformly distributed, makes it clear that the middle-age clusters still reflect the dynamical features of their galaxy. Some galaxies show a string of middle-age clusters parallel to the bar (e.g., NGC 1097). This population seems to be a relic from a star-formation episode after which the star clusters remained on a similar orbit. In fact, this scenario is described by simulations in dobbs_age_2010 and their Figure 2 reflects a situation where 50 Myr old clusters are orbiting parallel to the bar.

sources.summary: The atlas presents NGC 1097 as a long-barred Seyfert galaxy with a circumnuclear starburst ring, spiral central magnetic field, strong upstream polarized emission, and Faraday rotation structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 1097 (Fig. 5) has one of the intrinsically longest bars in our sample ( 16 kpc) and is the most interesting galaxy concerning radio polarization. The nucleus (Seyfert 1 type, Storchi-Bergmann et al. \ ) and the circumnuclear starburst ring of 17 \ ( 1.3 kpc) radius (see Hummel et al. \ , Gerin et al. \ ) are prominent in total radio intensity. Polarized radio emission in this region reveals a spiral magnetic field extending from the circumnuclear ring towards the centre (Beck et al. \ ).

NGC 1097 also features strong polarized emission upstream As the gas moves faster than the pattern speed of the bar, upstream is defined as the region in front of the shock which lags behind with respect to the galaxy's rotation. of the shock fronts (identified with the dust lanes offset from the bar major axis), where the regular magnetic field makes a large angle with the bar's major axis and then, upstream of the dust lanes, turns to become aligned with the bar. This strong turning of polarization vectors leads to beam depolarization, so that the upstream regions appear as two elongated minima of polarized emission in Fig. 25.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1097 as a barred LINER/Seyfert with a bright star-forming ring; its nucleus contributes little to integrated line and MIR emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 1097 - This strongly barred Liner/Seyfert galaxy has a bright star-forming ring of diameter 20 \ e.g. Hum87, Kot00 . The nucleus, which is resolved and separable from the ring in our maps, contributes negligibly to the integrated H and Br emission Sto96, Kot00 , as well as to the total MIR emission. We used an aperture of 45 , encompassing the whole emission from the ring Rou01b . We corrected for the average extinction based on the results of Kot00 derived from H /Br ratios. We recomputed the weighting by the Br luminosity, with adjustments for the [ N ii ] contribution to their H data and the different extinction laws adopted.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1097 as a strongly barred spiral whose dust lanes, polarized emission, B-vectors, and gas streamlines show magnetic fields aligned with shearing bar flows.

quotes.quote: M83 (Fig. m83 ) hosts a small bar, NGC 1097 and NGC 1365 (Fig. n1365 ) host huge bars. The total radio intensity is strongest in the region of the dust lanes, consistent with compression by the bar's shock. The polarized intensity in NGC 1097 and NGC 1365 (Fig. n1365 ) has a completely different distribution. The B -vectors and the gas streamlines around the bar as obtained in numerical simulations are surprisingly similar, which suggests that the regular magnetic field is generally aligned with the shearing flow (Beck et al. \ ). Remarkably, the optical images of NGC 1097 and NGC 1365 show dust filaments which are almost perpendicular to the bar and aligned with the B -vectors.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1097 as an observed nuclear-spiral case, noting its three-armed structure and streaming motions consistent with shock-driven inflow models for AGN fueling.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, one of the best investigated nuclear spirals in NGC 1097 ( , ) has three arms (Fig.2, right panel), difficult to generate by the observed bisymmetric bar only.

Recently, the same algorithm has been applied to the kinematics of nuclear spiral in NGC 1097 observed with the Integral Field Unit GMOS on the Very Large Telescope . Strong streaming motions of amplitude about 50 \ that show spiral morphology, have been observed. That observation confirms presence of shocks in nuclear spirals.
The shift between the kinematical and morphological spiral arms predicted by the models is consistent with that observed in NGC 1097 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1097 as evidence that nuclear dusty spirals can channel gas inward, linking observed streaming motions to a young circumnuclear starburst and nuclear fueling.

quotes.quote: On the observational side, kinematic evidence for inflow along nuclear dusty spiral arms has been found so far in one case: NGC 1097 . The observations of NGC 1097 were obtained with the Integral-Field Unit of the Gemini Multi-Object Spectrograph and allowed the maping of the gas kinematics in the inner few hundred parsecs . After subtracting a circular velocity model, streaming motions along spiral arms with inward velocities of up to 50 km s ^ 1 were found. Another relevant finding on this galaxy is the young obscured starburst recently discovered very close to the nucleus (within 9 pc), in agreement with the suggestion that inflowing gas and dust gives birth to stars in the nuclear dusty spiral or disk . The velocity observed for the streaming motions along the nuclear spiral allows an estimate of a few Myr for the gas to flow from a few hundred parsecs to the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1097 as a pseudobulge galaxy, citing The bulge has a prominent nuclear ring; The nuclear ring is evident in the surface brightness profile from 5-15 even in the near-IR.

quotes.quote: NGC 1097 .SBS3../RSBbc(s) Pseudobulge. The bulge has a prominent nuclear ring. The nuclear ring is evident in the surface brightness profile from 5-15 even in the near-IR. The decomposition yields n_b=2.0 , and the bulges is actively forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =1.3 mag.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 1097 as a well-sampled LLAGN SED already studied previously, accounting for HST UV starburst contamination that its AGN models do not reproduce.

quotes.quote: NGC 1097 Note that even though the SED of NGC 1097 is as well sampled as many of the LLAGNs in Section sec:seds , we chose to include it in this section rather than discussing it in more detail in sec:seds because its SED was already well-studied by nemmen06 . We take into account here the HST UV spectrum which was not included in EHF10 because it contains contributions from an obscured nuclear starburst in addition to the AGN. This spectrum was obtained from sb05 who studied and modeled it in detail. Our models do not take into account the contribution of the nuclear starburst.

sources.summary: The paper reports extended jet-like radio emission in NGC 1097, resolving two mas-scale radio peaks and deriving jet properties for its LLAGN core.

quotes.quote: The results yield the first resolved radio structure of the pc-scale jet in NGC 4594 (or Sombrero) and the detection of jet-like extended emission in NGC 1097 and NGC 2911.

For NGC 1097 two peaks of radio emission of S/N 7 and S/N 6 , for the A and B components, respectively, are detected (Figure  ngc1097 ), revealing the first extended jet-like structure of total linear size 0.7 pc and P.A.= 23^ . Assuming component A is associated with the central black hole (BH) and component B is associated with a radio lobe suggests the jet is partially oriented into our line of sight. For NGC 1097, the detection of extended radio emission in a single frequency does not allow the determination of the radio spectral index.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1097 as a quenching massive galaxy whose central magnetic field and cosmic rays help support molecular clouds and suppress massive star formation.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, a strong magnetic field in the center of NGC 1097--a massive galaxy undergoing star formation quenching-- is found to be responsible for decelerating its massive star formation.

Investigating the ISM energy balance in the central kpc region of the quenching galaxy NGC 1097, find a major role of the magnetic field and cosmic rays in controlling the molecular clouds and star formation. Most of the giant molecular cloud associations (GMA) are magnetically supported against gravitational collapse and the star formation rate per free-fall drops with the magnetic field strength, while it is uncorrelated with the turbulence (Fig. 2). This explains the deviations from the star formation laws observed earlier by .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1097 as a nearby barred-galaxy comparison, noting its nuclear-ring magnetic field strength, bar-region field alignment, and large synchrotron-derived magnetic-field pitch angle.

quotes.quote: Measurements have been performed for a handful of galaxies for a review, see for example Beck2015 , the best studied example probably being NGC 1097 Beck2005,Tabatabaei2018,Lopez-Rodriguez2021,Hu2022c .

(iii) The equipartition magnetic field in synchrotron-emitting gas in the nuclear ring of NGC 1097 is 60 G, which is of the same order of magnitude as similar estimates for the diffuse gas in the CMZ LaRosa2005,Morris2006,Yusef-Zadeh2022 .

Implications for external barred galaxies Our finding that the magnetic field on large (kpc) scales in the bar region is approximately aligned with the gas velocity streamlines is consistent with observations of polarised radio continuum emission of nearby barred galaxies such as NGC 1097 and NGC 1365 Moss2001,Beck2005 . The comparison of the orientation of the magnetic field in the nuclear ring is more tricky. The observed pitch angle of the magnetic field inferred from synchrotron-emitting gas in NGC 1097 is rather large, 40^ .

sources.summary: The paper finds that NGC 1097 has a bright circumnuclear star-forming ring whose mid-infrared emission is little affected by its weak active nucleus.

quotes.quote: Non-stellar activity Of the SB galaxies, four are known to host a Seyfert nucleus: in order of decreasing flux fraction from the central condensation, VCC 836 = NGC 4388, NGC 1365, NGC 1097 and NGC 1433. For these, the high central color could arise from dust heated by non-stellar radiation from the accretion disk and halo of the central object and would thus not necessarily indicate the presence of massive stars. For NGC 1097, we have the direct visual evidence that the contribution from the active nucleus to the circumnuclear emission is negligible, since the central mid-infrared source is resolved into the well-known star-forming ring, which is very bright, and a faint point source at the nucleus. Correcting the images of NGC 1097 for dilution effects with a procedure analog to CLEAN (see the Atlas for more detail), we obtain fractions of the total circumnuclear fluxes contributed by the nuclear point source of less than 3 at 7 m and about 1 at 15 m.

sources.summary: The atlas treats NGC 1097 as an individually observed active or starburst galaxy, summarizing its prior classification and near-infrared continuum, emission-line, and absorption properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 1097 . Classified originally as LINER on the basis of its optical spectrum kee83 , NGC 1097 was later reclassified by sb97 as a Seyfert 1 galaxy after observing the appearance of broad Balmer line emission and a featureless blue continuum. It has a bright star-forming ring of diameter 20", with the nucleus contributing negligibly to the integrated H and Br emission, as well as to the total MIR emission kot00 . The NIR spectrum resembles anything but a Seyfert 1 galaxy. From 0.8 m to 1.7 m, a poor emission line spectrum is detected, with [ S iii ] 9068, 9531 \ the most prominent ones, although intrinsically weak.

sources.summary: The paper reports clear extra-nuclear mid-infrared emission in NGC 1097, distinct star-formation blobs, and an AGN-to-star-formation separation of about 700 pc.

quotes.quote: In addition to the central point source, in three sources -- NGC 1097 , NGC 5135 , NGC 7469 - we observed clear extra-nuclear MIR emission. In all of these objects we find distinct blobs of star formation (SF), in addition NGC 5135 and NGC 7469 also exhibit a weak diffuse component. Typical distances between SF blobs and the AGN are 8 2 ( 700 pc) in NGC 1097, 1 5 ( 400 pc) in NGC 5135 and 1 3 ( 400 pc) in NGC 7469.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1097 as a nearby Seyfert whose starburst age is unusually comparable to torus adjustment time and whose present torus is modeled as moderately transparent.

quotes.quote: This is smaller than the observed starburst ages (Tab. tab:input ) for all AGNs except NGC 1097 where the time of adjustment and the starburst age are comparable.
We assume for all AGN, except NGC 1097, a starburst duration of 10 Myr, i.e. the the starburst continues until the first SN explode.

Moreover, 3 AGNs (Circinus, NGC 3227, and NGC 1068) have opaque tori ( < 2 ), 2 AGNs (NGC 1097 and NGC 7469) show moderately transparent tori ( 3-4 ), and 1 AGN (NGC 3783) has a transparent torus ( > 5 ).

sources.summary: The paper reports Gemini GMOS-IFU observations of NGC 1097, finding emission-line gas kinematics and non-circular nuclear-spiral motions that reveal gas streaming toward the center.

quotes.quote: In fathi06 we have used the GMOS IFU to observe the inner 7 ^ 15^ of NGC 1097, along the galaxy major axis, in the spectral region 6000-7000 . We have obtained the gaseous kinematics in the emission lines of H + [ NII ] 6548,84 . After sutracting the velocity field of a circular disk model from the data, we found non-circular motions in association with a nuclear spiral, revealing streaming motions towards the center with velocities of 50 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1097 as an observational Seyfert test case, emphasizing its starburst ring, dense molecular gas, and central dynamical mass dominated by its black hole.

quotes.quote: In a similar manner, NGC 1097 shows a molecular ring combined with intense star formation activity on scales of 700 pc Hummel87, Telesco81, Kotilainen00 , and the presence of dense gas has been inferred via CO and HCN observations by Kohno03 and Hsieh08 .

NGC 1097 In order to evaluate the ability of our parametrized star formation law to calculate realistic star formation rates, we applied it to observational data of NGC 1097 Hsieh2011 , a nearby Seyfert 1 galaxy with a prominent star burst ring, and compared the obtained results with the observed star formation rates.

For NGC 1097 Davies2007 estimated the dynamical mass in the central 20 - 40 pc from the kinematics of the stars and gas to 1.4 10^8 M_ , where 1.2 10^8 M_ are contributed by the central black hole Lewis2006 .

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 1097's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 1097 is a low-inclination grand-design spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 17.0 5.3 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with a prominent nuclear starburst ring (diameter 18 1.5 kpc; hummel_circumnuclear_1987 ) and an active nucleus both containing a LINER/Seyfert nucleus with broad double-peaked Balmer lines storchi-bergmann_double-peaked_1993 , and a young massive nuclear star cluster ( storchi-bergmann_evidence_2005 ; see and for detailed studies of the AGN). The nucleus is also a flat-spectrum radio source wolstencroft_vla_1984 . A system of two symmetrical jet-like systems was found around the nucleus (e.g., lorre_enhancement_1978 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1097 as a cautionary double-peaked emitter whose H peak separation tracks line flux and could be mistaken for orbital motion over short intervals.

quotes.quote: A similar behavior is seen is NGC 1097, a double-peaked emitter not included in this study see Figure 5 of Schimoia2015 . In NGC 1097 the separation between the two peaks of the broad H line changes in accordance with the integrated flux of the lines; as the peak separation fluctuates the two peaks never cross.
A short segment of the velocity curve of NGC 1097 could easily be interpreted as a segment of a sinusoid and attributed (incorrectly) to orbital motion of two BL regions.

sources.summary: The simulation paper uses NGC 1097 as an observed barred-galaxy comparison, linking its circumnuclear spirals, strong bar, and central inflow velocity to modeled nuclear spiral shocks.

quotes.quote: Circumnuclear disks with spirals seen in external galaxies, for example, in NGC 1097 hsieh11,onish15 , could form by a similar process. We note however that NGC 1097 and Milky Way are strongly barred galaxies, so that nuclear rings produced by the strong bars might have affected the formation of circumnuclear disks.
The radial inflow velocity of 50 observed in the center of NGC 1097 fathi06 is consistent with our model.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC1097 as a prototypical barred galaxy whose bar-aligned radio polarization implies shear-amplified, gas-aligned magnetic fields useful for diagnosing barred-galaxy flows.

quotes.quote: Radio polarization observations of the prototypical barred galaxy NGC1097 Beck+2005 reveal strongly polarized emission along the bar with field orientations parallel to the gas streamlines. The observed polarization pattern suggests that the field is amplified and stretched by shear in the compression region, and that the field is frozen into the gas and aligned with the gas flow over a large part of the bar. If this result holds generally in barred galaxies, the polarization pattern in bars -- especially using a tracer that preferentially probes the dense interstellar gas -- would provide important complementary information on the plane-of-sky gas flows to the line-of-sight kinematic information obtained from molecular emission lines.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 1097 is modeled with one dense central Newtonian component and that the model fits despite simplified constant scale radii.

quotes.quote: Figs fig:fig8 and fig:fig9 , respectively, show NGC 1097 and NGC 2590 modeled with only one central dense Newtonian component and they fit the model very well. Deviations in most cases I believe can be attributed to the fact that no azimuthal dependence has been added to the model, nor does it allow for anything but constant scale radii over regions of the galaxy where the fits apply. That is clearly unphysical but seems to be a reasonable approximation.

gmw_00000165.name: NGC 1144
gmw_00000165.overview: NGC 1144 is a disturbed spiral galaxy [arXiv:1310.2770] containing a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0605463, arXiv:1310.2770] that exhibits X-ray variability [arXiv:2007.02616]. It is interacting with the nearby galaxy NGC 1143 [arXiv:astro-ph/0605463, arXiv:1310.2770], resulting in a collisional encounter [arXiv:2005.08569] and a complex radio morphology [arXiv:2005.08569].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC1142 as part of collisional Arp118 and finds complex 22/45 GHz radio morphology, including a southern ring, six 22 GHz components, and flatter nuclear component B.

quotes.quote: NGC1142 It is part of the system Arp118 , comprising also the spheroidal galaxy NGC1143 (separation 40 arcsec), and it is most probably the result of a collisional encounter e.g. JoyGhigo1988 . The system presents a morphology with a southern ridge of radio emission, corresponding to optical emission line knots, a compact component close to the optical position and a group of three compact components (further resolved into 6) 8 arcsec far in the NE direction e.g. JoyGhigo1988,Condon1990 , both having flat spectra Thean2000 . The triple system has been further by Thean2001 into 6 components with MERLIN observations.

Our radio maps reveal a complex radio morphology, in which the southern ring of radio emission is visible in the 22 GHz maps, but not in the 45 GHz map. We detect 6 components in the 22 GHz map, but only three are detected also in the naturally-weighted, tapered 45 GHz maps. The morphology of radio emission is similar to previous works at similar resolution e.g. Thean2000 , but we detect (with high significance) an additional component with respect to previous VLA studies, i.e. component C, only detected at 22 GHz. Only the components A1, A2 and B (where the nucleus is believed to reside) are detected at both bands. The spectral indices for A1 and A2 are steep, while the spectral index for component B is flatter (although borderline, = 0.5 0.13).

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1144 has a bright nuclear X-ray source, high intrinsic absorption, a measured Fe K line, and stronger hard X-ray luminosity than ROSAT soft-band data implied.

quotes.quote: NGC 1144: The XMM-Newton image of this object shows a bright nuclear source. The best fits of Model 1 to 0.5 -- 10 keV XMM-Newton spectrum yields high intrinsic absorption ( N_ H = 5.62 10 ^ 23 cm ^ 2 ). The EW of Fe K line at 6.4 keV is 252 ^ +81 _ 71 eV. The observed 2 -- 10 keV flux is 3.14 10 ^ 12 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 , and the intrinsic 2 -- 10 keV luminosity is 3.14 10 ^ 43 erg s ^ 1 . Prieto et al. (2002) reported this object as an unobscured one, and 0.2 -- 2.4 keV flux of 1.1 10 ^ 13 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 , on the basis of fits to ROSAT /PSPC spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 1144 with XMM-Newton and NuSTAR spectra, finding variability, changes in intrinsic luminosity and absorption, and a NuSTAR column density near the Compton-thick limit.

quotes.quote: NGC 1144: We fitted the \ EPIC-pn and the \ FPMA/FPMB spectra of this source using the four models that are described in Section sec:spectralfit . The source shows variability. Hence, we fitted both observations assuming the same power law slope but we let the power law normalization, the column densities, and the reflection fraction (in the case of Pexmon) free to vary. The fits indicate changes in the intrinsic power law luminosity and the absorbing N_ H , confirmed by all models. The column density increases from 6 10^ 23 cm^ 2 in the \ observation consistent with the results found by Winter08, Winter09 to 10^ 24 cm^ 2 in the \ observation, at the lower limit of CT.

sources.summary: The atlas treats NGC 1144 as an individually observed active or starburst galaxy, summarizing its prior classification and near-infrared continuum, emission-line, and absorption properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 1144 . A CfA Seyfert 2, with NGC 1143 forms an interacting pair separated by 0.7''. Our NIR spectrum is very similar to that of NGC 1097. It displays a steep blue continuum in the interval 0.8 m--2.4 m, dominated by stellar absorption features. The emission line spectrum is rather poor, with only [ S iii ] 9069, 9531 , 2 1.257 m and 2.2033, 2.121 m being detected.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes X-ray spectra of NGC 1144, describing its best-fit components, Fe K emission or flux behavior, and comparison with previous modeling.

quotes.quote: NGC 1144 The XMM-Newton spectra were best fit by a double absorbed power law with a thermal component and a Gaussian component for the Fe K emission. When fitting the 3 - 8 keV continuum to obtain a local fit for the Fe K component, a double power law was needed to accommodate the spectrum shape; the power law indices of the two components were tied together with the normalizations and an absorption component attenuating the second power law allowed to vary. Winter et al.

sources.summary: The paper reports broad H2CO absorption in the Seyfert galaxy NGC 1144, decomposed into three velocity components and consistent with earlier weak CO and HCN spectra.

quotes.quote: NGC 1144: Gao2004a reported broad yet weak CO and HCN J= 1-0 emission (T _ mb 50 and 2 mK, respectively) toward this Seyfert galaxy. Our detection of broad H _2 CO 1_ 10 1_ 11 absorption (Figure fig:ExgalFormSingles-OnePage ) which decomposes into three velocity components is consistent with the HCN J= 1-0 spectra reported by Gao2004a . There do not appear to be any imaging studies of the dense gas in this galaxy.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 1144's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 1144 is a disturbed spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0288 ( D 128 Mpc) interacting with the elliptical NGC 1143 (also named NGC 1141) with a nuclear separation of 44 26 kpc towards the north-west hippelein_arp_1989 . It possesses a Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 that belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample. Apart from , NGC 1142 was observed in the MIR with IRTF edelson_broad-band_1987 , with Palomar 5 m carico_iras_1988 , with charmandaris_mid-infrared_2001,ramos_almeida_mid-infrared_2007 and with /IRAC, IRS and MIPS.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1142's proton luminosity falls outside the theoretical range and suggests its low cosmic-ray luminosity could imply a low-spin AGN black hole.

quotes.quote: Comparing both results, it is possible to see that NGC 1142 fails to produce a proton upper luminosity that remains in the L_ CR ^ Theory calculated range. This is explained by the fact that the observed NGC 1142 mass must drive a great magnetic luminosity L_B , since the greater the mass, the greater the luminosity L_B . To explain the low cosmic ray luminosity from NGC 1142 one can assume that the AGN black hole possibly has a small spin, since the smaller the spin, the smaller the luminosity L_B and consequently the smaller the cosmic ray luminosity.

gmw_00000166.name: NGC 1194
gmw_00000166.overview: NGC 1194 is an inclined lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1405.1430, arXiv:2311.15447] hosting a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1708.02683, arXiv:2111.10036] and a massive black hole [arXiv:1405.1430, arXiv:2311.15447]. The galaxy contains a large, variable water megamaser disk [arXiv:2302.02572, arXiv:2311.15447] and exhibits an X-shaped extended narrow-line region [arXiv:1708.02683].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1194 as an inclined S0 megamaser disk galaxy with a massive black hole, little central gas, a large-scale disk, and limited spatially resolved kinematic information.

quotes.quote: NGC 1194 is an inclined ( i 90 ) S0 galaxy at D=57.9 Mpc. The galaxy PA is 145 , and we aligned the IFU along the major axis. Given our PSF of 0 16 , we are resolving 40 pc scales with our AO-assisted observations.

Thus we achieve very comparable spatial resolution to the NGC 4388 observations presented above. We have detected the large-scale disk in NGC 1194 in H 1 , and find a circular velocity of 220 20 \ sunetal2013 . From SDSS imaging, we find a bulge-to-total ratio of 0.5 0.2 greeneetal2010 . The BH in NGC 1194 has a mass of 6.5 0.4 10^7 , one of the most massive in the megamaser disk sample.
In NGC 1194, an S0, there is very little gas, and with our S/N ratios, we cannot say much about the two-dimensional velocity or dispersion fields.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC1194 as a high-inclination S0 with a bright round inner region, dust and boxy residuals, plus a bar-like component and envelope that change the bulge fit.

quotes.quote: NGC 1194 NGC1194 (Figures fig:NGC1194-1 and fig:NGC1194-2 ) appears to be an S0 seen at relatively high inclination. The innermost region ( 1'' nucleus) is round, bright, and distinct from the flatter ( q 0.5 ) outer regions, which can be visually traced to about 90'' ( 20 ). The ellipticity profile features a local peak at 3'' ( 0.7 ), followed by a trough at 8'' ( 2 ). Subtracting the basic bulge+disk model additionally reveals a dust lane that is parallel to the major axis but offset by 4'' to the south-west, as well as a minor-axis boxy light excess at 6'' / 1.5 . Turning to the basic model first, we see that there is no need for an AGN component as the central light is already slightly overestimated by the bulge+disk model.

Our adopted model includes a bar-like component and an "envelope", in addition to a bulge, disk, and point source. The envelope, which has a best-fit \ profile with n=2 , reduces the flux excess and axis ratio at the largest scales ( 100'' ) compared to the basic bulge+disk model, as well as reducing the bulge \ index ( n=3.2 ). The envelope is intermediate in flattening between bulge and disk: q=0.5 , versus 0.7 and 0.4 , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1194 as an outlier whose bolometric correction is too high, possibly because intrinsic X-ray luminosity is underestimated or bolometric luminosity overestimated.

quotes.quote: For NGC 1194, both estimates lie significantly away from the relationships, 2 for the torus model and 1.5 for the mytorus model. For our analysis of \ henceforth, we use the \ estimate from the torus model with the exception of NGC 424 and NGC 3079 where we use the \ estimate from the mytorus model. For NGC 1194 the fact that neither \ estimates are in agreement with the relationships may imply that the intrinsic \ has been underestimated by 0.5 dex. Alternatively \ may have been overestimated by the same amount. We discuss and investigate the inclusion of NGC 1194 in our sample in later analysis.

For NGC 1194, our \ estimates lie significantly above the relationships by 0.5 dex. This could be due to a systematic underestimation of the intrinsic , possibly caused by the underestimation of . Alternatively, this could have been caused by an overestimation of \ in the SED fitting by , perhaps due to contamination by star formation in the host galaxy. Finally, it is possible that the \ value for NGC 1194 lies at the extreme of the intrinsic ditribution of \ for its luminosity.

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC1194 across the 0.5-30 keV band, modeling emission features and reflection, and concludes the source is a Compton-thick AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC1194 spectral fits Once verified that the \ and \ spectra provide a similar description of the NGC1194 properties and in absence of a significant flux variation in the 3--10 keV common band, being the values 1 10^ 12 \ according to both satellites, we were able to fit the full-band 0.5--30 keV spectra. Figure fig:NGC1194_spectra shows the simultaneously fitted spectra (top-left panel) and the relative phenomenological transmission model (top-right panel); pn data are in black, MOS (summed) in red, \ FPMA in green and FPMB in blue. All the spectral features identified in the pn spectrum are reproduced in the MOS data, except for what may be the Ni K emission line (visible from the residuals around 7.5 keV), possibly due to the lower effective areas of the MOS detectors.

The resulting N_ H,2 is 2.08 ^ +0.21 _ 0.24 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 , hence in the Compton-thick regime. After having tested different values for the incident angle, we adopted _ obs =65^ , where the fit seems to converge. The absorption in the soft (Galactic+extra) has a column density of 0.4 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 ; the thermal plasma component has a temperature 0.8 keV, and the scattered nuclear radiation results to be 1 (consistent with the scattering reported in Marchesi2018 blue 2018 , hereafter M18). The prominent Fe K emission line is properly reproduced using the MYTorus model. Its EW has been measured to be relatively high ( 650 eV rest-frame).

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1194 as a bright local Seyfert 1.9 with a water maser black-hole mass, Compton-thick X-ray classification, and a patchy torus geometry.

quotes.quote: The hardest AGN offer the most insight into understanding the circumnuclear gas, and one of the hardest sources in the tatum13a sample is NGC 1194. This nearby (z=0.0136) Seyfert 1.9 galaxy hosts a water maser, which has allowed a precise measurement of the mass of the black hole at M=6.5 0.3 10^7 M_ kuo11a . NGC1194 is also a bright X-ray source, detected in the BAT catalogue. NGC 1194 has been classified as X-ray Compton-thick and has been discussed by various authors in the literature, e.g. and references therein dellaceca08a, georgantopoulos19a, marchesi19a .

The hard spectral form of this AGN was modeled using the toroidal reprocessor mytorus . The decoupled model form provides a good description of the spectrum, with reflection arising from gas with a global average column density > 4 10^ 24 cm^ 2 and transmission of the continuum through an order-of-magnitude lower column. In this model, the reflection strength is a factor 3 higher than expected from a simple torus. Such a result may indicate that much of the intrinsic X-ray continuum is hidden from view. An alternative model is that of a patchy torus, where 85 of sight-lines are obscured by Compton-thick gas and the remaining 15 by Compton-thin gas.

sources.summary: NGC 1194 is discussed as a confirmed disk maser with a large variable 22 GHz disk and 183 GHz systemic plus redshifted high-velocity maser features.

quotes.quote: NGC 1194 The 22 GHz maser system in NGC 1194 was discovered by the Megamaser Cosmology Project (MCP), and VLBI observations presented in Kuo_2011 confirm that it is a disk maser system. Relative to other 22 GHz disk maser systems, NGC 1194 is notable for its large apparent disk size ( 0.54-1.33 pc across; see Kuo_2011 ) and for having several strong (up to 1 Jy) and narrow (linewidths < 1 ) individual maser lines exhibiting a large degree of variability Pesce_2015 . Our 183 GHz spectrum of NGC 1194 is shown in the upper lefthand panel of fig:hex1 , along with a 22 GHz spectrum reproduced from Pesce_2015 (see Fig. 1 in that paper).

The most prominent component in the 183 GHz spectrum of NGC 1194 is the central set of systemic maser features, which span a velocity range of 10 around a central velocity of 4104 . The peak flux density of the systemic features is 16 mJy, which is a factor of 2 fainter than the typical peak flux density of the 22 GHz systemic masers. A second component in the 183 GHz spectrum of NGC 1194 is a single narrow ( FWHM 0.69 ) line at a velocity of 4593.5 , which peaks at a flux density of 35.2 mJy. This spectral line coincides with the redshifted complex of high-velocity maser features seen at 22 GHz.

sources.summary: The H I map presents NGC 1194 as an SA0 water-megamaser galaxy with high enough SNR for inclination fitting, a 4075 km/s systemic velocity, and elongated gas organized in the disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 1194 is an SA0 galaxy RC3 at a distance of 53.2 3.7 Mpc. It is one of our water megamaser galaxies Kuo11 . The SNR is sufficiently high to determine an inclination of 69.1 , compared to 71.1 from HyperLeda Paturel03 . The systemic velocity as measured from the map is 4075 km s ^ 1 . The moment-0 map shows an elongated morphology with angular diameter 2 8 at the 3 level, showing that the gas is organized within the galaxy disk.

sources.summary: The S7 notes reclassify NGC 1194 as a Seyfert 2, report an X-shaped extended narrow-line region, specify lobe velocity asymmetry, and find no recent star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 1194: This galaxy had been classified as a Sy 1, but in our data we see no evidence of broad Balmer lines, and it is now classified as a Sy 2. Schmitt03 imaged this galaxy with HST and found the \ emission to be only mildy extended over 2 arcsec. However, we observe an X-shaped ENLR extending over 36 arcsec in . The opening angle is 30^ , and the PA = 170^ . The southern lobe is blueshifted, and the northern lobe is redshifted.

sources.summary: NGC 1194 is modeled as a three-group maser system consistent with a thin disk; the fit estimates its mass-to-distance ratio, black-hole mass, recession redshift, and x offset.

quotes.quote: NGC 1194 is a system with three groups of maser features (see Fig. fig:posteriors1 ) and the information of this maser system is consistent with a thin disc that is distributed in the sky with a P.A. of approximately 157 . We perform the statistical fit to the mass-to-distance ratio, the recessional redshift, and the BH x offset, while we fix its y offset aligned with the redshifted and blueshifthed masers. The estimated M/D =(1.31 0.02) 10^ 6 M_ /Mpc implies M = (7.26 0.52) 10^ 7 M_ for the BH mass when taking into account the distance reported in Table tab:prev . Besides, by considering the v_ rec estimation of the general relativistic approach and the reported recession velocity in (see Table tab:prev ), we find that these results exclude each other. The scattering angle =11 renders _ red ^ 2 =1.206 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1194 as a lenticular Seyfert 2 megamaser galaxy with a large maser disc, massive SMBH, disturbed molecular gas, and unsuitable CO kinematics for new SMBH modelling.

quotes.quote: NGC 1194 NGC 1194 is a lenticular galaxy harbouring a Seyfert 2 AGN 2018ApJS..235....4O , for which we adopt a distance D=58 6 Mpc. This distance was estimated from NGC 1194's Local Group-centric redshift 1996AJ....111..794K by Kormendy+2013ARAA51.511 and Saglia+2016ApJ818.47 assuming a cosmology derived from the Wilkinson Microwave Anisotropy Probe ( WMAP ) 5-year data 2009ApJS..180..330K . NGC 1194 was first reported to harbour H _2 O megamasers by 2008ApJ...686L..13G and is part of the MCP. It has a relatively large maser disc with an inner radius of 0.51 pc and an outer radius of 1.33 pc. It hosts the most massive SMBH derived using the maser method to date, M_ BH =(7.1 0.3) 10^7 M _ at our adopted distance above Kuo+2011ApJ727.20 .

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1194 is the sample exception where X-ray scaling gives a lower black-hole mass than the megamaser value, despite a large Keplerian maser disk.

quotes.quote: The only noticeable exception is NGC 1194, for which the X-ray scaling method yields values significantly lower than the maser one.
NGC 1194 displays one of the largest maser disks (with inner and outer radii of 0.54 and 1.33 parsecs) which appears to be slightly bent and is consistent with Keplerian rotation kuo11 .

The only object of our sample for which the \ inferred from the X-ray scaling method is statistically inconsistent with the megamaser value is NGC 1194, which is the AGN with the lowest accretion rate ( _ Edd 7 10^ 3 ). NGC 1194: The starting model for the spectral fit of this source is provided by the work of mas16 , who fitted the \ spectrum with the \ model in the decoupled mode, with the addition of a Gaussian line at 6.8 keV, and a scattering fraction of the primary continuum of f_ s 3 .

sources.summary: NGC 1194 is an SA0 Seyfert 2 galaxy with weak inner featureless continuum, old central stellar population, younger outer regions, stronger nuclear reddening, and roughly constant stellar kinematics and metallicity.

quotes.quote: NGC 1194 NGC 1194 is an SA0 galaxy, with a Seyfert 2 AGN. We detected a very weak ( < 10 ) contribution of a FC in the inner 1 0 region, with the whole datacube dominated by an old stellar population. The outer regions are younger, with a mean age of 4 Gyr, if compared to the inner 2 0 diameter region, that has a mean age of 10 Gyr. The reddening is also higher in the nucleus, peaking at 2 mag, and the stellar and metallicity are roughly constant within our data ( 180 km s ^ 1 and solar metallicity).

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 1194's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 1194 is a highly-inclined early-type spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0136 ( D 58.2 Mpc) with an AGN, optically classified as a Sy 1.9 veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . It features one-sided weak extended radio emission cospatial with emission (extension 1 0.3 kpc; PA 124 ; schmitt_jet_2001,schmitt_hubble_2003 ). After , NGC 1194 was observed in the MIR with Palomar 5 m/MIRLIN gorjian_10_2004 and /IRAC and IRS.

gmw_00000167.name: NGC 1365
gmw_00000167.overview: NGC 1365 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9909117] hosting a Seyfert nucleus [arXiv:1112.3658, arXiv:2004.03616] that exhibits rapid X-ray absorption variability [arXiv:0901.4809, arXiv:1503.03109]. The galaxy features a central starburst ring [arXiv:2404.19473] and asymmetric dust lanes driven by its strong bar [arXiv:1305.1919, arXiv:2004.03616].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1365 from two rotation curves, finding disk mass profiles and low declining B-band M/L values consistent with no non-baryonic dark matter.

quotes.quote: NGC 1365 Now we consider two rotation curves bib:sofue and bib:zanmar the latter suitably smoothed out in a way to conform with data points and error bars. We have noticed that surface mass density corresponding to the rotation curve has approximately exponential falloff (see figure fig:1365 ). Therefore, for a while one may assume that surface mass density is a superposition of two exponential profiles, in order to check whether such mass distribution accounts for the observed rotation. We find, this is indeed the case, however the mass density becomes larger than that of gas (although quickly falling off).

In general, this is very interesting, that for this galaxy already the doubly-exponential disk alone suffices to account for the rotation curve of bib:zanmar . Irrespectively of which rotation curve is considered, the obtained mass-to-light ratio is low ( 2.91 to 3.15 in B-band depending on which rotation curve and method is used). This ratio falls-off with increasing radius in every case. As so, galaxy NGC 1365 can be modeled without non-baryonic dark matter.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1365 as a barred Seyfert test case, adopting observed velocity fields, rotation curves, surface-density decomposition, orientation, and systemic velocity for analytic gas-flow modeling.

quotes.quote: To evaluate the limitations and abilities of  we want to compare it to hydrodynamic simulations of a nearby barred galaxy. For this, the strongly barred Seyfert galaxy NGC 1365 (Fig. fig:1365 ) would be suitable. This galaxy has been extensively observed (see the review by P.O. Lindblad1999 ) and a hydrodynamic simulation of the gas flow pattern was derived by LLA96. Also Halphangc1365 observed and modeled the gas flow in the bar.

A total hybrid velocity field has been derived by Lindbladpo1996 based on the HI velocity field of HIngc1365 and complemented with optical long slit spectra. We have here accepted the rotation curve based on this as derived by LLA96 (Fig. fig:input ). It agrees closely with the rotation curve given by Halphangc1365 . We have derived the perturbed potential from the total surface density following the analysis using Bessel functions described in Ch. 2.6.2 BinneyTremaine2008 and LLA96 (their Appendix A).

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1365's inner Lindblad resonance region, resolving its Seyfert nucleus, star-forming ring, infrared clusters, dust properties, and dusty ongoing star formation.

quotes.quote: We present new far-infrared ( 70-500 m) Herschel PACS and SPIRE imaging observations as well as new mid-IR Gemini/T-ReCS imaging (8.7 and 18.3 m) and spectroscopy of the inner Lindblad resonance (ILR) region ( R<2.5 kpc) of the spiral galaxy NGC 1365. We complemented these observations with archival Spitzer imaging and spectral mapping observations. The ILR region of NGC 1365 contains a Seyfert 1.5 nucleus and a ring of star formation with an approximate diameter of 2 kpc.

The strong star formation activity in the ring is resolved by the Herschel/PACS imaging data, as well as by the Spitzer 24 m continuum emission, [ Ne ii ] 12.81 m line emission, and 6.2 and 11.3 m PAH emission. The AGN is the brightest source in the central regions up to 24 m, but it becomes increasingly fainter in the far-infrared when compared to the emission originating in the infrared clusters (or groups of them) located in the ring. We modelled the AGN unresolved infrared emission with the clumpy torus models and estimated that the AGN contributes only to a small fraction ( 5 ) of the infrared emission produced in the inner 5 kpc. We fitted the non-AGN 24-500 m spectral energy distribution of the ILR region and found that the dust temperatures and mass are similar to those of other nuclear and circumnuclear starburst regions. Finally we showed that within the ILR region of NGC 1365 most of the on-going star formation activity is taking place in dusty regions as probed by the 24 m emission.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes three Suzaku observations of the Seyfert galaxy NGC 1365, finding variable cold and warm absorbers, inner disk reflection, super-solar iron abundance, and eclipse prospects for mapping its nucleus.

quotes.quote: We present jointly analyzed data from three deep Suzaku observations of NGC 1365. These high signal-to-noise spectra enable us to examine the nature of this variable, obscured AGN in unprecedented detail on timescales ranging from hours to years. We find that, in addition to the power-law continuum and absorption from ionized gas seen in most AGN, inner disk reflection and variable absorption from neutral gas within the Broad Emission Line Region are both necessary components in all three observations. We confirm the clumpy nature of the cold absorbing gas, though we note that occultations of the inner disk and corona are much more pronounced in the high-flux state (2008) than in the low-flux state (2010) of the source.

The basic spectral components observed in all three datasets are a constant circumnuclear starburst, a highly-ionized, outflowing warm absorber, a clumpy cold absorber along the line of sight to the nucleus, hard X-ray emission from the corona, neutral reflection from distant material in the outer disk and/or torus, and broadened, skewed reflection features from the inner disk. The spectral changes between observations are most evident in the XIS data, particularly from 2-6 (factor 8 ). The PIN data also display significant variability between observations (factor 2 ), though not to the degree present in the XIS data. Incorporating both the relativistic reflection component and the cold absorber is necessary in order to adequately model the spectrum in all three epochs and flux states. In keeping with recent work on other type-1 AGN (e.g., NGC 3783, Brenneman2011 ) and previous work on NGC 1365 Walton2010 , we find Fe/solar =3.5^ +0.3 _ 0.1 . This super-solar iron abundance can readily explain the so-called ``hard excess'' noted in NGC 1365 in previous works (e.g., Risaliti2009c ) without invoking an additional, ad hoc absorber partially covering the source.

sources.summary: The paper maps cold dust in NGC 1365, describing its barred structure, starburst and Seyfert nucleus, asymmetric dust lanes, dust mass, star-formation rate, and rapid bar-driven central gas flow.

quotes.quote: We investigate the central mass concentration and the physical properties of the cold dust in the ``Great Barred Spiral Galaxy'' NGC 1365. With a diameter of twice the Milky Way ( 60 kpc) and a mild inclination ( 41^ ), NGC 1365 is among the best studied barred galaxies from the X-ray to the radio regimes, providing a rich multi-wavelength data archive ideal for in-depth studies. This galaxy hosts a Seyfert 1.5 AGN Schulz as well as strong star formation activity (starburst) in the center e.g. see and references therein Lindblad_99 .

NGC 1365 has a nuclear bar of about one kpc embedded in the large-scale bar Jungwiert . This galaxy does not host a circumnuclear ring unlike many barred galaxies. The shape of the central starburst region is asymmetric, with two massive dust lanes, with strong and aligned magnetic fields Beck_etal_05 .

Integrating the dust SED, we derive a total infrared (IR) luminosity, L _ TIR , of 9.8 10^ 10 L _ leading to a dust-enshrouded star formation rate of SFR _ TIR 16.7 M _ yr ^ 1 in NGC 1365. This indicates that the current central mass in NGC 1365 is evolving fast due to the strong bar.

sources.summary: Swift observations show NGC 1365 has long-term X-ray spectral variability, large absorbing-column changes, and behavior interpreted through a variable wind-scattering model.

quotes.quote: We present long-term (months-years) X-ray spectral variability of the Seyfert 1.8 galaxy NGC 1365 as observed by Swift , which provides well sampled observations over a much longer timescale (6 years) and a much larger flux range than is afforded by other observatories. At very low luminosities the spectrum is very soft, becoming rapidly harder as the luminosity increases and then, above a particular luminosity, softening again. At a given flux level, the scatter in hardness ratio is not very large, meaning that the spectral shape is largely determined by the luminosity. The spectra were therefore summed in luminosity bins and fitted with a variety of models.

Swift X-ray observations of NGC 1365 over a period of 6 years show a large amount of spectral variability. These variations are best explained by a two-component model consisting of an unabsorbed power law and a more luminous absorbed power law; for both components, the spectral index was fixed. The normalisations of the two power laws vary together. The absorbing column of the absorbed power law varies inversely with its luminosity, an effect which is not simply due to increased ionisation.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1365 as an absorbed Seyfert with high-frequency iron K reverberation and low-frequency soft lags interpreted as changes in neutral absorption.

quotes.quote: Another example of a low-frequency soft lag is in NGC 1365, which is a source that is known to have complex absorbers that can eclipse the intrinsic emission from the nucleus. When the source was in a largely unobscured state, we found clear evidence for a high-frequency iron K lag (and possibly even the Compton hump lag; kara15a ). However, at low frequencies, the soft band lagged behind the hard band. This low-frequency soft lag was only present in this particular observation. In that work, we suggested that the low-frequency soft lag was due to a decrease in the column density of the distant absorber during the observation (i.e. from a neutral cloud moving out of our line-of-sight).

NGC 1365 was recently observed by XMM-Newton for several orbits risaliti13 . High-frequency iron K reverberation was found during the most unobscured observation. In this observation, there was a low-frequency soft lag.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1365 as an SB Seyfert 1.9 with strong X-ray variability, variable absorption from BLR clouds, and optical/NIR broad-line components.

quotes.quote: NGC 1365 is a SB galaxy hosting a Seyfert 1.9 nucleus trippe10 . It displays strong X-ray variability (on time scales of hours to years) and evidence for a relativistically broadened iron line, indicative of a a rapidly rotating black hole ( risaliti09 ; risaliti13 ). Furthermore, it displayed a complex and variable absorption, with rapid variations in column density, attributed to an occultation event originating in the BLR clouds ( risaliti05 ; risaliti07 ).

In the optical spectrum of we have found clear indication of a BLR component both in H and in H (FWHM 1586 and FWHM 1700 , respectively), in agreement with the results of . The ISAAC MR NIR spectrum of NGC 1365 shows evidence of a broad Pa component having a FWHM = 1972 . In Figure fig:ResNGC1365 the fit with and without the inclusion of this broad Pa component is shown. The F test gives a probability of 1 10 ^ 89 that the improvement of the fit is due to statistical fluctuations. We attribute this component to the BLR emission.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1365 with TYPHOON 3D optical spectroscopy, finding a negative oxygen-abundance gradient plus strong azimuthal variations tied to spiral arms and explained by local enrichment and spiral-driven mixing.

quotes.quote: We present here a case study in the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 1365 using 3D optical data obtained in the TYPHOON Program. We find systematic azimuthal variations of the \ region oxygen abundance imprinted on a negative radial gradient. The 0.2 dex azimuthal variations occur over a wide radial range of 0.3 to 0.7 R_ 25 and peak at the two spiral arms in NGC 1365. We show that the azimuthal variations can be explained by two physical processes: gas undergoes localized, sub-kpc scale self-enrichment when orbiting in the inter-arm region, and experiences efficient, kpc scale mixing-induced dilution when spiral density waves pass through.

We have investigated the oxygen abundance distribution of the ISM in the spiral galaxy NGC 1365 using a new 3D data cube constructed in the TYPHOON Program. About 200 \ regions were identified from the H map extracted from the data cube. We derived their oxygen abundances and ionization parameters using flux ratios of strong emission lines. We found the following. There is a well-defined negative oxygen abundance gradient in NGC 1365, with a slope consistent with other local star-forming galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1365 as a key AGN case where variable absorption and reflection-dominated X-ray states support reflection-based black-hole spin measurements despite complex obscuration.

quotes.quote: Three different variable absorbers with column densities in the range 5 10^ 22 6.5 10^ 24 cm^ 2 and variable covering factors would be needed to explain the spectrum of the source below 10 keV. However, this absorption-only model fails to explain the hard X-ray spectrum in the 10--80 keV band, where the inclusion of relativistic reflection provides a statistically better description of the data Risaliti13, Walton14 . Furthermore, the latter model is also preferred on physical grounds, as the inferred bolometric luminosity in absorption-only scenarios is significantly higher compared to other indicators such as the [ O iii ] 5007 line. The case of NGC 1365 lends weight to the idea that X-ray reflection is indeed an effective means of measuring the spin of SMBHs, even when absorption is present.

NGC 1365 is one of the unique sources showing frequent changes in its obscuration state. Recently, summarized the various observational aspects of this source and proposed a multi-layer structure of the circumnuclear medium to explain all the observed absorption states and their variability. NGC 1365 has been observed several times in reflection-dominated states, suggesting the presence of a layer of neutral Compton-thick ( N_ H >10^ 24 cm^ 2 ) absorber located at a distance of the order of or larger than that of the broad line region Risaliti07 . The source is usually caught in a Compton-thin state, N_ H 10^ 23 cm^ 2 , but the column can even occasionally drop down to N_ H 10^ 22 cm^ 2 Braito14 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1365 as an archetypal barred Seyfert with a star-forming nuclear ring, central continuum source, and compact molecular ring interpreted as a torus.

quotes.quote: NGC 1365 is a strongly barred spiral galaxy with the characteristic dust lanes delineating the bar, which are particularly contrasted and curved. As an archetypal barred galaxy, it has been extensively observed e.g., Lindblad1999 . While the Seyfert 1.8 nucleus is obvious in optical and X-rays Nardini2015 , it is hardly seen in radio. discuss a marginal radio jet, the center of which corresponds to the central X-ray position.

At 350 GHz, we detect a central continuum point source, as can be seen in Figure fig:1365-1433 . Our large-scale CO(3-2) map reveals the contrasted nuclear ring of radius 9 = 770 pc. Inside this ring, which corresponds to the ILR of the bar, we detect a more compact molecular component, a CND with a ring shape, of radius 0 3 = 26 pc. This rotating ring just encircles the central continuum source, and might be interpreted as the molecular torus.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1365 as the massive barred spiral anchoring an infalling Fornax subgroup, with asymmetric H I morphology, high gas content, and environmental interaction signatures.

quotes.quote: NGC 1365, the large barred spiral galaxy (SBbc(s)I by FergusonFornax89 ), is the most massive galaxy in our sample. The optical morphology shows a well defined spiral structure, three northern arms and two more compressed southern ones. The morphology (Fig. fig:allmorph ) shows a very prominent extension to the north of the system and more dense contours corresponding to the two compressed optical arms. A very detailed study on the structure of NGC 1365 is made by . They also suggested that the motion of the galaxy through the ICM can account for its unwinding arms.

NGC 1365 has a = (1.5 0.3) 10 ^ 10 M _ and a = (6.2 1.3) 10 ^ 10 M _ . Fig. fig:himfraction shows that NGC 1365 is the only galaxy in our sample which is at the upper edge of the comparison sample of non-cluster galaxies due to its high / . Besides evidence of environmental interactions from its morphology, the strong gravitational potential appears to have been able to hold a sufficient amount of to make it comparable with the general behaviour of local galaxies (see also Fig. fig:hionh2 ).

sources.summary: The paper maps PAH band ratios across NGC 1365, comparing its 3.3, 7.7, and 11.3 micron behavior with the other PHANGS-JWST targets and linking ratio changes to PAH size and ionization.

quotes.quote: We present maps of the , and band ratios in NGC 628 (Figure FigRatioNGC628 ), NGC 1365 (Figure FigRatioNGC1365 ), and NGC 7496 (Figure FigRatioNGC7496 ). The color scale for each band ratio is the same across the three figures, with limits chosen using the 5 ^ th and 95 ^ th percentiles of all values. We overlay the regions cataloged in GROVES_HIICAT, PHANGSMUSE2022HII with black contours. Overall, each ratio shows variations both within and across galaxies, spanning values within a factor of 4--5 for and , and less than a factor of 2 in the maps.

Through analysis of the ratio of the 11.3 m to the 7.7 m PAH features, changes in the ionization fraction of the PAHs can be determined, while the strength of the 3.3 m compared to the 7.7 m or 11.3 m features provides information on the average PAH size. We present maps of these ratios across nearby galaxies NGC 628, NGC 1365, and NGC 7496. Based on the relative consistency of these ratio across each galaxy disk, we conclude that the population of PAHs is fairly uniform. regions and star-forming spiral arms show lower 11.3/7.7 and higher values, indicating the presence of smaller, highly charged grains in these regions.

sources.summary: NGC 1365 is one of three PHANGS-JWST galaxies mapped in 3.3 micron PAH emission and is described as a barred galaxy with a central gas-rich star-forming ring.

quotes.quote: We present maps of the 3.3 \ polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon (PAH) emission feature in NGC 628, NGC 1365, and NGC 7496 as observed with the Near-Infrared Camera (NIRCam) imager on JWST from the PHANGS-JWST Cycle 1 Treasury project. We create maps that isolate the 3.3 \ PAH feature in the F335M filter (F335M _ PAH ) using combinations of the F300M and F360M filters for removal of starlight continuum. This continuum removal is complicated by contamination of the F360M by PAH emission and variations in the stellar spectral energy distribution slopes between 3.0 and 3.6 . We modify the empirical prescription from Lai2020 to remove the starlight continuum in our highly resolved galaxies, which have a range of starlight- and PAH-dominated lines-of-sight. Analyzing radially binned profiles of the F335M _ PAH emission, we find that between 5-65 of the F335M intensity comes from the 3.3 \ feature within the inner 0.5 r_ 25 of our targets. This percentage systematically varies from galaxy to galaxy, and shows radial trends within the galaxies related to each galaxy's distribution of stellar mass, interstellar medium, and star formation.

Both NGC 1365 and 7496 are barred galaxies with high central gas surface densities associated with circumnuclear star forming rings. These regions produce strong PAH emission, which causes the upturn in the F335M _ PAH /F335M in their centers. NGC 628, on the other hand, shows a monotonic increase in F335M _ PAH /F335M with radius, suggesting that starlight surface brightness falls more rapidly than PAH surface brightness as a function of radius, which may reflect varying scale-lengths for the stellar mass and ISM distributions.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1365 as a nearby barred spiral Seyfert with nuclear and star-forming activity, finding steady NUV AGN emission but variable absorbed X-rays with diffuse soft emission.

quotes.quote: We selected a nearby obscured AGN NGC 1365 as it has both nuclear and star formation activities. This galaxy is a member of the Fornax cluster with a distance of 18.6 Mpc 0.6 Madore1999, Silbermann_1999, springob2009, polshaw2015, jang2018 . NGC 1365 has a Seyfert nucleus, a prominent bar, and is an archetype barred spiral galaxy (SBb(s)I; ).

Secondly, the excellent spatial resolution of UVIT allowed us to separate the AGN from the host galaxy emission by fitting the radial profile of NGC 1365 for each observation separately in the NUV and FUV bands. We found no substantial variation in the UV emission from the AGN at a 2 level in the NUV band. Thus, the intrinsic NUV flux of NGC 1365 is not significantly variable over the two-month period.

Third, we found that the X-ray emission of NGC 1365 consists of a powerlaw component with a photon index of 2.25 _ 0.40 ^ +0.35 that is modified by partial covering with variable column density N _H . Our data are consistent with the presence of diffuse emission in soft X-ray band with temperature kT 0.8 keV and X-ray reflection from a distant material.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1365 as a low recent-SF-change barred Seyfert with a strong local SF increase, central SF drop, and bar-driven central starburst ring.

quotes.quote: NGC 628, 4254, 4303, 4321, 4535, and 5068 belong to the high end of the distribution, while NGC 1365, 1512, 1566, 3351, and 3627 belong to its low end.This is also summarised in the last column of Table tab:yes_no . Looking at the distribution across the galactic disks in the 3 ^ rd column of Figure fig:all_maps , we observe large areas of increasing recent SF in NGC 628, 4254, 4303, and 4321, while NGC 1512, 1566, and 3627 exhibit recent SF activity that ranges from roughly unchanged to decreasing. The most active galaxy in terms of recent SF changes is the low-mass galaxy NGC 5068. NGC 1365 shows a region with a very strong recent SF increase, visually similar to the headlight cloud in NGC 628 headlight_cloud .

AGN feedback Among AGN hosts in the sample, there are Seyfert galaxies NGC 1365 veron_06 and NGC 1566 Shobbrook_66 , low ionization nuclear emission region (LINER)/Seyfert type 2 galaxies NGC 4303 and NGC 3627 agn_3627 , and the starburst NGC 4321 which is another LINER galaxy Wozniak_99 . For NGC 1365, showed that the feedback from the AGN or hot shocked gas can push away molecular and atomic hydrogen gas at a speed up to 100 km s ^ 1 . They saw that the outflow of the molecular gas is faster than the SF, suggesting a negative feedback scenario. indicates a drop in the SF activity in the central region of NGC 1365, in agreement with this statement.

NGC 1365 is dubbed the Great Barred Spiral Galaxy and has the highest global SFR and stellar mass in our sample, with most of the SF occurring in the central starburst ring. found that the large-scale stellar bar in NGC 1365 drives gas inwards and might be solely responsible for the massive SF in the central 5 kpc, without any significant AGN feedback on the gas disk.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1365 has numerous expanding-shell velocity gradients and bar-region H and CO velocity jumps exceeding 100 km/s, rising after inclination correction.

quotes.quote: Such shapes of high velocity gradients are especially numerous in NGC 1365. We find that H sources and PAH emissions at 7.7 are located at the center of these circles and arcs (see Figure fig:NGC1365_vg_comparison ). Thus, we infer that these are due to the expanding shells from HII regions ionized by young stars or stellar associations ( relano_05, bresolin_20 ).

Figure fig:vel_jump shows the velocity changes along the slits in selected regions for NGC 1365. We find that the velocity jump exceeds 100 within 2''(190 pc) in both H and CO in the slit 1 and 3. Within 5''(475 pc), the velocity of CO changes up to 200 , and that of H changes up to 150 in slit 1. The inclination of NGC 1365 is estimated to be 55.4 ^ ( lang_20 ). Thus, the velocity jump would become even higher ( 1.2 for NGC 1365) if we correct for inclination.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1365 as a prototypical barred galaxy, presenting high-resolution radio polarization observations that resolve magnetic fields in its bar ridges and circumnuclear starburst region.

quotes.quote: We present 3.5  cm and 6.2  cm radio continuum maps in total and polarized intensity of the barred galaxies NGC 1097 (at 2 -15 \ resolution) and NGC 1365 (at 9 -25 \ resolution). A previously unknown radio galaxy southwest of NGC 1097 is reported. Apart from a smooth faint envelope and a bright central region, both galaxies exhibit radio ridges roughly overlapping with the massive dust lanes in the bar region. The contrast in total intensity across the radio ridges is compatible with compression and shear of an isotropic random magnetic field, where the gas density compression ratio is approximately equal to 4 and the cosmic ray density is constant across the ridges. The contrast in polarized intensity is significantly smaller than that expected from compression and shearing of the regular magnetic field; this could be the result of decoupling of the regular field from the dense molecular clouds.

NGC 1365 , at 18.6 Mpc distance ( 1 93 ), is probably the best studied barred galaxy (see the review by Lindblad \ ). The \ emission and velocity field have been studied in detail by Ondrechen van der Hulst ( ) and J ors ater van Moorsel ( ). The galaxy plane is inclined by about 40^ to the line of sight and its line of nodes has a position angle of about 40^ with respect to the north-south direction. The north-western side is closer to the observer.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1365 as a strongly barred spiral with symmetric I-band light but asymmetric dust, gas kinematics, anomalous HII regions, and a fast bar in concentrated halos.

quotes.quote: We present new observations of the strongly-barred galaxy NGC 1365, including new photometric images and Fabry-Perot spectroscopy, as well as a detailed re-analysis of the neutral hydrogen observations from the VLA archive. We find the galaxy to be at once remarkably bi-symmetric in its I-band light distribution and strongly asymmetric in the distribution of dust and in the kinematics of the gas in the bar region.
