For NGC 1365, the extinction map shows high obscuration in the north with respect to the central AGN where there is also a bright clump suggesting that the star formation in this clump might be obscured. In fact, the presence of dust in NGC 1365 has also been confirmed by 24 m Herschel and SPIRE (Spectral and Photometric Imaging Receiver) observations in alonso-herrero12 where a comparison between the SFR derived from the 24 m continuum and the narrow H line shows that nearly 85 of the ongoing star formation in the central regions of NGC 1365 is taking place in the dust obscured regions.
From the electron density map of NGC 1365, it is clear that the electron density is high in sites of star forming regions and is spatially uncorrelated with the emission.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1365 as a barred local spiral Seyfert with ongoing star formation and MUSE/Chandra evidence for circumnuclear gas phases and kpc-scale biconical outflow.

quotes.quote: NGC 1365 (Seyfert 1.8 - D_ L 18.6 Mpc - 1" 90.2 pc) is a local spiral, characterised by a strong bar and prominent spiral structure, displaying nuclear activity and ongoing SF. A comprehensive review about the early works on this object is given by lindblad1999 . Our MUSE data was already published by venturi2018 , who studied the circumnuclear gas in its different phases by comparing MUSE and Chandra data and analysing the nuclear and extended gas outflows.

Specifically, venturi2018 revealed that emission that traces the kpc-scale biconical outflow, ionised by the AGN, is nicely matched with the soft X-rays. However, the hard X-ray emission from the star-forming circumnuclear ring suggests that SF might in principle contribute to the outflow.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1365 as an X-ray-absorption changing-look AGN whose spectral evolution resembles other CL AGNs and links absorber variability to accretion-mode change.

quotes.quote: We also present the L_ 2-10 keV /L_ Edd relation of NGC 1365, a CL AGN defined by variation of X-ray absorption column density, in bottom-left panel of Figure 1. It can be found that there are negative and positive correlations between and L_ 2-10 keV /L_ Edd for L_ 2-10 keV /L_ Edd 10^ 3 and 10^ 3 respectively, even there are only two observational points in the negative part.

We also explore the X-ray spectral evolution for NGC 1365, which is a CL AGN as defined based on the variation of the column density. It is interesting to note that the X-ray spectral evolution in NGC 1365 is also quite similar to other CL AGNs, where the negative and positive correlations L_ 2-10 keV /L_ Edd are found when the Eddington-scale X-ray luminosity is less and larger than 10^ 3 respectively. This result suggest that the accretion mode also changed in this X-ray defined CL AGN, and the variable absorbing material should be correlated with the evolution of the accretion mode.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1365 as a nearby barred spiral with strong central star formation, a Seyfert nucleus, prominent dust lanes, massive stellar and dust content, and gas reservoirs.

quotes.quote: NGC 1365 (Fig. subfig:ngc1365_maps ), also known as the Great Barred Spiral Galaxy , is one of the best studied barred galaxies in the nearby Universe and is located in the Fornax cluster at a distance of 17.9 Mpc 2010PASP..122.1397S . NGC 1365 has been classified as an SB(s)b type galaxy by 1995yCat.7155....0D with a Hubble stage of T=3.2 . This truly impressive galaxy, with a major axis twice as large as of the Milky-Way ( 60 kpc), displays strong ongoing star formation in the centre 1999A&ARv...9..221L and hosts a bright Seyfert 1.8 nucleus 2006A&A...455..773V . Two massive prominent dust lanes along the nuclear bar can be seen in optical images 1986MNRAS.221....1T , while the well developed spiral arms extend from the bar edges with the tendency to turn inwards at the outer edges of the galaxy 1996A&AS..120..403L . According to 2019A&A...624A..80N , this massive galaxy contains more than 8 10^ 10 M _ of stars, 10^8 M _ of dust, and shows a SFR of 13 M _ yr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper finds bright polar mid-IR emission in NGC 1365, roughly perpendicular to the ALMA torus and aligned with the ionization-cone direction, favoring dusty-outflow conditions.

quotes.quote: The PSF-subtracted images of six out of the twelve galaxies present bright extended mid-IR emission (Fig. fig:midIRimages ). These are NGC 1365, NGC 3227, NGC 5506, NGC 5643, NGC 6300, and NGC 7582. The contours show that the extended emission is detected up to at least a 20 _ bk level. For NGC 4388 we also detected extended mid-IR emission at a lower significance but in a similar orientation as that derived by using a different dataset. The sizes of the extended mid-IR emission range from approximately 50 pc in NGC 6300 to 190 pc in NGC 5506.

In NGC 1365, NGC 3227, and NGC 7582 as well as the inner regions of NGC 5506, the extended mid-IR emission appears to be approximately perpendicular (in projection) to the extended ALMA 870 m continuum emission. The latter for these four galaxies is identified as the torus. In NGC 5643, the orientation of the extended mid-IR emission is mostly along PA_ MIR = 60-70 ^ , which is nearly perpendicular (in projection) to the nearly north-south ALMA 870 m continuum emission. Finally, in NGC 6300 the extended mid- and far-IR emission show similar orientations, and thus the extended mid-IR emission is along the equatorial direction of the torus.

The extended mid-IR emission in NGC 3227, NGC 4388, and NGC 7582 is more prominent on the optical bright side of the ionization cone. In NGC 1365, NGC 5506, and NGC 5643 the mid-IR extended emission is more symmetric around the AGN position and approximately along the direction of the ionization cone.

sources.summary: The review uses NGC 1365 as an archetypal AGN where rapid Compton-thin to Compton-thick transitions imply dense BLR-scale absorbing clouds and cometary cloud structure.

quotes.quote: In the archetypical case, NGC 1365, Compton-thin (N _ H 10^ 23  cm ^ 2 ) to thick (N _H>10^ 24  cm ^ 2 ) state transitions were observed on time scales as short as 10 hours. Assuming that the absorbing gas is moving with Keplerian velocity, these rapid events imply the presence of clouds at 10^4 _ g _ g =GM/^2 is the gravitational radius of an object of mass M , where G is the Gravitational constant and the speed of light. from the BH, rotating at velocities larger than 10^3  km s ^ 1 . Estimates for the size and density of the clouds can be also derived, being of the order of 10 ^ 13  cm and 10 ^ 10 10 ^ 11  cm ^ 3 , respectively. The overall scenario is therefore in agreement with the identification of this component of the X-ray absorber with the BLR clouds responsible for broad emission lines in the optical/UV.

Again for NGC 1365, studied the time evolution of its covering factor and its column density, unveiling a ``cometary'' shape for the obscuring cloud, i.e. a high density head, followed by an elongated, lower density tail.

sources.summary: The paper models the changing-look AGN NGC 1365, connecting accretion-rate changes, corona geometry, failed disk winds, and X-ray absorption features.

quotes.quote: Here, we study the spectral properties of a CLAGN, NGC 1365 using combined XMM-Newton and NuSTAR observations to understand the CL behavior. The model fitted mass accretion rate varied between 0.003 0.001 and 0.009 0.002 M_ Edd and the dynamic corona changed from 28 3 to 10 1 r_g . We found that the variable absorption column density correlates with the mass accretion rate and the geometry of the corona.
NGC 1365 is a Seyfert 1.8 MaiolinoReike1995 galaxy at z=0.0055 with a 10^ 6.5 M_ BH Risalitietal2009 .

The source is a CLAGN due to its Compton-thin to Compton-thick transition, that can be due to variation in the LOS absorber rather than to extreme intrinsic emission variability MattEtal2003,Risalitietal2005 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1365 as a changing-look AGN with optical turn-off, later broad-line reappearance, another turn-off by 2021, and complex X-ray absorption/accretion behavior.

quotes.quote: NGC 1365 We present a newly discovered `turn-off' event in the CL AGN NGC 1365 (BAT 184). NGC 1365 was observed to show broad emission lines in both \ and \ in 1993 August 1999A&A...346..764S , but in a spectrum taken in 2009 January 2010ApJ...725.1749T report only narrow , with any broad \ emission being very weak, suggesting that the source `turned off' between 1993 and 2009. In BASS DR1 we released a 2010 September spectrum which showed no change from 2009 January, with NGC 1365 still displaying only narrow \ and . However, we then see broad Balmer lines in our 2013 December and 2017 June spectra, suggesting that the source has turned back on between 2010 and 2013. 2017MNRAS.464.1783O find broad near-infrared Paschen lines in 2011 October observations with ISAAC, suggesting that either the turn-on event happened rapidly between 2011 September and 2011 October, or that the type 2 behaviour observed in the optical in 2009-10 was due to dust obscuration which attenuated the broad Balmer lines e.g. 1995ApJ...440..141G .

sources.summary: The paper reports early PHANGS-JWST imaging of NGC 1365, noting its bright AGN, saturated central pixels and starburst-ring regions, high star formation activity and gas surface density, and barred structure.

quotes.quote: These saturated pixels are primarily an issue in the centers of NGC 1365 and NGC 7496, which both host bright AGN. Removing the restriction did not recover the very central pixels coincident with the AGN themselves, but it did allow us to recover a number of the brighter pixels surrounding the central point source. However, some of these sources, especially the brightest compact, massive star-forming regions in the starburst ring of NGC 1365, still show artifacts that have structure suggesting that the source brightness remains underestimated by the imaging.

Some of the brightest sources in the MIRI imaging, particularly in NGC 1365, are saturated, show artifacts resulting from poor ramp fitting, and the surrounding areas are affected by diffraction spikes.

The four galaxies bracket the full range of star formation properties in the sample (with NGC 1365 having the highest star formation activity and gas surface density and IC 5332 among the lowest) and feature varied dynamical structures (e.g., bars in NGC 1365 and NGC 7496; strong spiral arms in NGC 628 and weak spiral structure in IC 5332).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1365 as a PHANGS-JWST target with a bright AGN, broad central CO line wings, masked diffraction spikes, and relatively coarse 150 pc physical resolution.

quotes.quote: We study the first four PHANGS-JWST targets: NGC 628, NGC 1365, NGC 7496, and IC 5332 and compare mid-IR emission at 7.7 m, 10 m, 11.3 m, and 21 m obtained as part of the PHANGS-JWST survey PHANGSJWST22 to CO (2-1) observed by ALMA as part of the PHANGS--ALMA survey PHANGSALMA21 and extinction-corrected H obtained as part of the PHANGS--MUSE survey PHANGSMUSE22 . Both NGC 1365 and NGC 7496 have bright AGN in the inner galaxy, which lead to diffraction spikes that extend well into the surrounding galaxy disks.

The integration also includes all bright ( S/N>3 in 2 channels) emission in extended line wings, which effectively captures the broad wings in the centers of NGC 1365 and NGC 7496. We focus this paper on normal galaxy disks and leave the contrast between disk and starburst environments for future work, but note that several other papers in this issue focus specifically on this rich region in NGC 1365 LIU22JWST,SCHINNERER22JWST,WHITMORE22JWST . For these first results, we used a fixed 1.7'' , and our physical resolution for NGC 1365 and NGC 7496 remains fairly coarse at 150 pc.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 1365 as showing recent central star formation, a sharp age drop at the bar radius, and a clear bar age gradient not explained by stellar mass.

quotes.quote: Some galaxies show evidence of recent star formation in their inner-most region ( R 0.2 R_ 25 ), leading to a sudden decrease of stellar age (e.g., \ NGC 1300, NGC 1365 among others).

Some galaxies show a sharp drop in LumW age at the outer radius of the bar (NGC 1300, NGC 1365, NGC 1672, NGC 7496), consistent with a scenario where the oldest populations dominate in the inner and rounder part of the bar, while young populations are located on more elongated orbits, populating the bar ends, where they spend a larger fraction of their orbiting period Beuther2017, Wozniak2007, Neumann2020 .

Out of the 14 barred galaxies in our sample, the only noticeable exceptions are NGC 1365 and NGC 5068, with a very clear age gradient present also in the bar. It is interesting to note that these two galaxies represent the high- and low-stellar mass extremes of our sample ( M _ * /M_ = 10.99 and 9.40, respectively). Therefore, this similarity is likely not connected to their total stellar mass.

sources.summary: The paper finds seven ALMA components around NGC 1365's center, several matching earlier radio components that were suggested to host super clusters.

quotes.quote: 17: NGC 1365 The image at a resolution of 0 2 shows seven significant components, labeled b , , d , e , f , g , and h around the central component labeled a . Radio maps at different frequencies were presented in past studies 5 GHz 15 GHz; Sand82,Ste99,The00,Sak07 . At a , there appears to be a radio component according to a 1 \ resolution map created by . Furthermore, the five mm-wave components of b , , d , e , and f appear to have radio counterparts that have been labeled D, E, G, H, and F, respectively, in previous studies e.g., Sand82 . suggested that each of the D, E, G, and H regions possibly hosts a super cluster.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1365 as a changing-look Seyfert 1.8 AGN whose UV-to-IR SED requires graphite ring, torus, and polar-wind dust components.

quotes.quote: We present a 3D radiative transfer model for the Spectral Energy Distribution (SED) of NGC 1365, which is a ``changing look" Seyfert 1.8 type AGN. The SED from the ultraviolet (UV) to the infrared (IR) is constructed using archival data from the UVIT on-board AstroSat , along with IR data from the literature. The skirt radiative transfer code is used to model the SED and derive the geometry and composition of dust in this AGN. Similar to our earlier SED model of NGC 4151, the nuclear region of NGC 1365 is assumed to contain a ring or disk-like structure concentric to the accretion disk, composed of large (0.1 m - 1 m) graphite grains in addition to the two-phase dusty torus made up of ISM-type grains (Ring And Torus or RAT model). We also include, for the first time, an additional component of dusty wind in the form of a bipolar cone.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1365 as a nearby Fornax barred spiral Seyfert with enhanced inner circumnuclear star formation, PAH-based IR indicators, and minor AGN contribution to inner IR emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 1365 NGC 1365 is a member of the Fornax cluster Lindblad1999 . It is a nearby archetype of barred spiral SBb(s) Seyfert 1.8 galaxy at an approximate distance of 19.6 Mpc Anand2021 . Studies by Gao2021 shows that, when compared to the outer regions, NGC 1365 has an increased star-formation activity in the inner circumnuclear ring. Alonso2012 reported an intense star-formation activity in the inner parts of the galaxy using various IR star-formation indicators such as 6.2 and 11.3 m PAH emission. They also observe that within the inner 5 kpc region of NGC 1365, the contribution of AGN to the IR emission is of the order of 5 percent.

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 1365 as poorly fit by its models, potentially contaminated by NGC 1386, highly variable during BeppoSAX, yet consistent with prior model-C results.

quotes.quote: NGC 1365: NGC 1365 is one of the few sources for which our models do not provide a good fit ( ^2_ =1.26 in model C). A more careful analysis of this object was performed by Risaliti et al. (2000). According to this work, the PDS data of NGC 1365 could be contaminated by the nearby active galaxy NGC 1386. Moreover, NGC 1365 shows an high and complex variability during the BeppoSAX observation. However, the best fit values in Risaliti et al. 2000 are in agreement with those obtained with our model C.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1365 as the largest and radio-brightest sample galaxy, with a 29 kpc bar, Seyfert nucleus, starburst region, polarized upstream emission, and central ring evidence.

quotes.quote: NGC 1365 (Fig. 7) is the intrinsically largest and radio-brightest galaxy in our sample. Its bar length is 29 kpc. It has a Seyfert 1-type nucleus surrounded by a starburst region (see review by Lindblad \ ). Similarly to NGC 1097, it has significant (though weaker) polarized radio emission upstream of the shock fronts and relatively weak emission enhancements on the dust lanes. The turn of magnetic field lines towards the dust lanes near the bar major axis is much smoother than in NGC 1097.

sources.summary: The paper finds that apparent abundance-gradient breaks in NGC1365 are likely false, caused by overestimated outer-disk oxygen abundances from inappropriate R23/P calibrations.

quotes.quote: The break in the slope of the (O/H) _ R_ 23 abundance gradient in the disk of barred spiral galaxy NGC1365 was obtained by Roy Walsh (1997). Does the break in the slope of the (O/H) _ R_ 23 abundance gradient in NGC1365 have the same nature as that in NGC5457? Around seventy measurements of the strong oxygen lines intensities in H ii regions in the disk of NGC1365 are available (Alloin et al. 1981, Pagel et al. 1979, Roy Walsh 1997).

As can be seen in Fig. figure:izl1365 a,b there is break as in the slope of the (O/H) _ R_ 23 abundance distribution as in the slope of the (O/H) _ P abundance distribution at the galactocentric distance R ^* 0.7R _ 25 although the exact position of the point of break is not beyond question due to large scatter in oxygen abundance at any fixed radius. This suggests that the breaks in the slopes of (O/H) _ P and (O/H) _ R_ 23 abundance distributions within the disk of NGC1365 is an artifact caused by the wrong oxygen abundances in H ii regions with galactocentric distances larger than R ^* .

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 1365 has a Seyfert nucleus whose contribution to H-line and MIR dust emission is small, while star formation dominates MIR diagnostics.

quotes.quote: NGC 1365 - We combined the Br measurement of Pux88 with the H flux integrated within the same region from an H + [ N ii ] map to derive the extinction, and assumed that it represents accurately the extinction within our larger aperture. The Seyfert nucleus does not contribute importantly to the H line and MIR dust emission. The total H flux in the central 4 4 Ver86 , which also includes emission from adjacent ``hot spots,'' is only 8 of that in 40 . MIR diagnostic line ratios suggest that star formation activity dominates the low excitation ( 50 eV) line spectrum at these wavelengths as well as the MIR and far-infrared continuum luminosities Stu02 . The nucleus is unresolved in the ISOCAM maps, preventing an accurate estimate of its contribution to the MIR fluxes, but the ISOCAM CVF data do not provide evidence for a significant AGN contribution based on the diagnostics of Rig99 and Lau00 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1365 as a strongly barred spiral whose dust lanes, polarized emission, B-vectors, and gas streamlines show magnetic fields aligned with shearing bar flows.

quotes.quote: M83 (Fig. m83 ) hosts a small bar, NGC 1097 and NGC 1365 (Fig. n1365 ) host huge bars. The total radio intensity is strongest in the region of the dust lanes, consistent with compression by the bar's shock. The polarized intensity in NGC 1097 and NGC 1365 (Fig. n1365 ) has a completely different distribution. The B -vectors and the gas streamlines around the bar as obtained in numerical simulations are surprisingly similar, which suggests that the regular magnetic field is generally aligned with the shearing flow (Beck et al. \ ). Remarkably, the optical images of NGC 1097 and NGC 1365 show dust filaments which are almost perpendicular to the bar and aligned with the B -vectors.

sources.summary: The paper reports a Chandra occultation of NGC 1365, using rapid Compton-thin/reflection-dominated X-ray variability to infer a compact source and broad-line-region-scale obscuring cloud.

quotes.quote: We present an occultation of the central X-ray emitting region in the Seyfert Galaxy NGC 1365. This extreme spectral variation (from Compton-thin to reflection-dominated and back to Compton-thin in four days) has been caught in a ten days Chandra monitoring campaign consisting of six short (15 ks) observations performed every two days. We discuss the implications of this occultation within the scenario of a Compton-thick cloud crossing the line of sight of the X-ray source. We estimate a source size R 10^ 14 cm and a distance of the cloud from the source D 10^ 16 cm. This direct measurement confirms the theoretical expectations of an extremely compact X-ray source, and shows that the Compton-thick circumnuclear gas is located at a distance from the center on the scale of the Broad Line Region.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1365 as a Seyfert galaxy with low-luminosity AGN activity and circumnuclear star formation; BLAST observed it and prominently detected its bar and spiral arms.

quotes.quote: NGC 1365 NGC 1365, presented in Fig. fig:ngc1365 , is is usually classified as a Seyfert 1.5, but some authors list it as Seyfert 1 or Seyfert 2 e.g., galliano2005,veron1980,alloin1981 . The AGN has low luminosity, but there is evidence from optical studies to suggest that there is star formation surrounding the nucleus komossa1998 . BLAST made three observations of a 0.25 \ area centered on NGC 1365, with a total integration time of 30 minutes. The brightest of our sample, BLAST's observations prominently show both the bar and spiral arm structure of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper presents Suzaku, Swift-BAT, and Integral-IBIS evidence for excess hard X-ray emission from NGC 1365, interpreting it as complex circumnuclear absorption or possibly a double active nucleus.

quotes.quote: We present a new Suzaku observation of the obscured AGN in NGC 1365, revealing an unexpected excess of X-rays above 20 keV of at least a factor 2 with respect to the extrapolation of the best-fitting 3-10 keV model. Additional Swift-BAT and Integral-IBIS observations show that the 20-100 keV is concentrated within 1.5 arcmin from the center of the galaxy, and is not significantly variable on time scales from days to years. A comparison of this component with the 3-10 keV emission, which is characterized by a rapidly variable absorption, suggests a complex structure of the circumnuclear medium, consisting of at least two distinct components with rather different physical properties, one of which covering > 80 of the source with a column density N_H 3-4 10 ^ 24  cm ^ 2 . An alternative explanation is the presence of a double active nucleus in the center of NGC 1365.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC1365 as a strong case where metallicity-gradient breaks are real and related to corotation, also noting observed radial gas flow along spiral arms.

quotes.quote: NGC1365 : Pilyugin collected about 70 points in the literature for NGC1365, most of which (55) are due to , who were the first to detect the break in the metallicity slope of this galaxy. Later put in doubt the reality of the break, which could be an artefact due to the existence of two branches in the calibration of the R23 method. We have several arguments telling us that the break is real: 1-) Roy and Walsh compared 3 different methods of calibration for his data, and not all of them necessarily affected by the same systematic error; 2-) added new data points which allow a comparison of the P-method with the Te-method.
A recent detailed study of NGC1365 By is of particular interest, since they detected a radial flow of gas along the spiral arms, in opposite directions on the two sides of corotation.

sources.summary: The paper places its weak H2CO absorption detection in NGC 1365 alongside previous CO, HCN, HNC, HCO+, CN, and circumnuclear-torus measurements.

quotes.quote: NGC 1365: Baan2008 report single-position J= 1-0 CO, HCN, HNC, HCO ^+ , N= 1-0 CN, J= 2-1 CO, J= 3-2 CS, and N= 2-1 CN observations with resolutions ranging from 13^ to 57^ from this barred spiral galaxy. Perez2007 report HCN J= 3-2 emission in addition to the J= 1-0 and J= 2-1 transitions of CO, HCN, HNC, and N= 1-0 and N= 2-1 transitions of CN. Gao2004a report relatively bright (160 and 8 mK, respectively) and extended CO and HCN J= 1-0 emission from this galaxy, while Sandqvist1999 mapped the CO J= 3-2 emission and found extended ( _s 20^ FWHM) structure dominated by a circumnuclear molecular torus ( _ torus 13^ ). Our detection of weak H _2 CO 1_ 10 1_ 11 absorption (Figure fig:ExgalFormSingles-OnePage ) is consistent with these previous molecular tracer measurements.

sources.summary: NGC 1365 is presented as an extreme changing-look AGN whose rapid X-ray spectral transitions are attributed to varying cold absorption near the broad-line region and accretion disc.

quotes.quote: For example, the type 1.8 AGN NGC 1365 (z = 0.00547), also known as the Great Barred Spiral Galaxy, is an intriguing source displaying the most dramatic X-ray spectral changes observed so far in an AGN: the source switched from reflection-dominated (Compton-thick) to transmission-dominated (Compton-thin) and back in just a few days to week time-scales (Risaliti et al. 2005). NGC 1365 can be classified as one of the most extreme examples of ``changing look`` AGN. This has been attributed to variation in the line-of-sight of cold absorber rather than to extreme intrinsic emission variability. Such rapid time-scales of these X-ray eclipses suggest that these absorbers (N _ H 10 ^ 23 - 10 ^ 24 cm ^ 2 ) are located on compact scales consistent within the inner BLR and the outer part of the accretion disc.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1365 as an ambiguous borderline AGN whose rapid Compton-thin and Compton-thick transitions complicate its Seyfert type classification.

quotes.quote: ESO 323-G077 (type-1), NGC 1365 (type-1), IRAS 13349+2438 (type-2) and the Circinus galaxy (ESO 97-G13, type-2) are in the intermediate zone between the two classifications. While the type-2 classification of the Circinus galaxy is undisputed, the cases of ESO 323-G077, NGC 1365 and IRAS 13349+2438 are more ambiguous. Spectropolarimetric observations achieved by Schmid2003 showed that, at least, a fraction of the inner region of ESO 323-G077 must be hidden behind the torus horizon, classifying this AGN as a borderline Seyfert 1 galaxy. NGC 1365 is another intriguing, borderline object, showing rapid transition between Compton-thin and Compton-thick regimes due to X-ray eclipses Risaliti2005 . Such behaviour leads to a difficult classification of NGC 1365, either type-1 Schulz1994 , type-1.5 Veron1980 , type-1.8 Alloin1981,Risaliti2005 or even type-2 Chun1982,Rush1993 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1365 as a stage-N LIRG with an AGN showing extreme absorption variability and adopts averaged NuSTAR/XMM-Newton absorption and luminosity values.

quotes.quote: IRAS F03316--3618 (NGC 1365): The LIRG NGC 1365 is a stage-N object. The source has an AGN showing extreme absorption variability observed with e.g., Chandra Torres-Alba2018,Venturi2018 , XMM-Newton Risaliti2005,Whewell2016 , and NuSTAR e.g., Walton2014a,Risaliti2016 . Rivers2015 study the variable absorption seen in the four long-look joint observations of NuSTAR and XMM-Newton with a multi-layer absorber model. In this work, we refer to their averaged values of N_ H 1 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 and log L_ 2-10 = 41.90 0.01.

sources.summary: NGC 1365 is a PHANGS outflow candidate, though high-resolution CO(3-2) work found no clear molecular outflow while other gas phases show biconical or central outflows.

quotes.quote: Our study may have missed one potential outflow candidate among the PHANGS galaxies, namely NGC 1068, and for two of our candidates (NGC 1365, NGC 1672) high resolution CO (3--2) observations of the galaxy centers provided no clear evidence for molecular outflows.

NGC 1365 : CO (3--2) emission Combes2019ALMATori do not find clear evidence for a molecular outflow but rather for an inflow. However, NGC 1365 has long been known to exhibit a biconical outflow in, for example, ionized gas or X-ray as reported by e.g., Lena2016, Venturi2017, Davies2020 . Recent work by Gao2021 reports evidence for an outflow in the central 5.4 kpc in molecular ( CO (2--1) ) and ionized gas.

sources.summary: PHANGS-MUSE uses NGC 1365 as a massive, strongly barred, AGN-dominated example, finding AGN-driven ionization signatures, older bar stellar ages, and a central velocity-dispersion turnover.

quotes.quote: These three galaxies represent the lowest stellar mass (NGC 5068), an average stellar mass flocculent morphology (NGC 4254) and the highest stellar mass, AGN-dominated, strongly barred morphology (NGC 1365).
In NGC 1365, where the central AGN contributes to the high \ intensity pixels, the assumed intrinsic / = 2.86 is probably not appropriate, resulting in an overestimation of A_V for these pixels.

NGC 1365 presents a more complicated star-formation history, with older stellar ages dominating across the bar. While we find good consistency between expectations based on morphology and the dynamical structure revealed by MUSE kinematics, it also provides new insight into the nature of various dynamical structures. For example, we detect a turn-over in the stellar velocity dispersion towards the centre of NGC 1365, at the highest densities, suggesting a cold, and potentially older, central dynamical structure.

sources.summary: The paper maps PAH fraction in NGC 1365, masking its bright AGN center and noting a small-radius PAH-ratio rise in this barred Seyfert galaxy.

quotes.quote: We find average R _ PAH = (F770W+F1130W)/F2100W values of 3.3, 4.7, 5.1, and 3.6 in IC 5332, NGC 628, NGC 1365, and NGC 7496, respectively.
In NGC 1365 and NGC 7496, we mask pixels in the center due to IR brightness of the active galactic nuclei (AGN), which saturate the signal mostly in the F2100W band.

There is however, a slightly different trend in NGC 1365 and NGC 7496, which both show an increase in abundance ratio at small radii before exhibiting a steady decrease. Both are Seyfert galaxies NGC 1365: 1.8, NGC 7496: 2.0; e.g., GarciaBernete2022 hosting an AGN as well as central bars that feed high density regions at their centers.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 1365 as an intermediate Seyfert with short-timescale X-ray variability, elevated line luminosities, and a kiloparsec-scale AGN-ionized biconical outflow.

quotes.quote: No significant X-ray variability has been observed in the Seyferts of our sample, with the exception of a couple of objects (NGC 1365 and NGC 2992), which showed evidence for relatively short-timescale variability, both in flux and spectral features see and Salvestrini et al., in prep.

Two targets, namely IRASF05189-2524 (Sy2) and NGC 1365 (int-Sy) flagged as (E) and (F), respectively, in Fig. fig:LoiiiLIR , have \ and \ luminosities higher than derived from the mean relations with .

Spatially resolved \ emission from MUSE/VLT observations of NGC 1365 has revealed a kiloparsec-scale biconical outflow ionized by the AGN Venturi2018 . Shocks and outflows in systems as such could influence the relative luminosities of the lines, resulting in a higher deviation from the mean relations presented above.

sources.summary: The paper notes NGC 1365 lacks CN faint-line coverage, treats it as a non-merger with known inclination, and identifies it as an obscured-AGN system.

quotes.quote: We note that we did not recover both CN (1-0) lines in NGC 1365, as the faint line was not covered in the spectral window set up.

The galaxies which are not mergers are NGC 4418, ESO 320-G030, and NGC 1365 Stierwalt2013 . For reference, the inclination angles of these three galaxies are i=62^ for NGC 4418 Sakamoto2013 , i=43^ for ESO 320-G030 Pereira2016 , and i=40^ for NGC 1365 Sakamoto2007 .
Finally, Swain2023 confirmed the presence of an obscured AGN in NGC 1365 using multiwavelength observations.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1365 as a Fornax galaxy with a Cepheid distance, a foreground-core location, and the largest neutral-hydrogen mass in the cluster sample.

quotes.quote: There are Cepheid distances available for three Fornax galaxies: NGC 1365 at 18.6 Mpc (Madore ), NGC 1326A at 18.7 Mpc (Prosser ), and NGC 1425 at 22.2 Mpc (Mould ). Drinkwater ( ) have shown that NGC 1326A is part of a subcluster in front of the main cluster (see Section tf ) and they also argue that NGC 1365 is in front of the cluster core.
NGC 1365 ( T = 4 ) has the largest \ mass in Fornax and is one of the most massive galaxies known.
FCC 121 (NGC 1365) had been used as a calibrator (among others) and the spectra have been analysed separately for each polarisation.

sources.summary: The paper calls NGC 1365 a Seyfert galaxy in Fornax and discusses nearby BL Lac and X-ray sources that enter the ULX catalog criteria.

quotes.quote: NGC 1365: This is a well known Seyfert Galaxy in the Fornax Cluster. About 12 arcmin to the SE in the direction of its extended spiral arm lies a very strong X-ray BL Lac (Arp 1998, p. 49). The distance to the Fornax Super Cluster is about the same as to the Virgo Super Cluster in the opposite direction in the sky, or about 15 Mpc (or see Arp 2002 for a Cepheid distance of 18 Mpc). With this distance to NGC 1365, the BL Lac object comes within the acceptance radius of the IXO Catalog and is included in Table 1.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1365 as an observational comparison, saying its arm shapes and SE-arm dust-lane shift support the barred-galaxy manifold model and corotation estimate.

quotes.quote: A quantitative comparison to the arm shapes of NGC 1365 proved successful.

Such a shift has indeed been observed in the SE arm of NGC 1365 Ondrechen.VanDerHulst89,JorsaterVanMoorsel95 , thus bringing corotation to 1.4a , where a is the bar semimajor axis. This value is in good agreement with the value found from the hydrodynamical modelling of NGC 1365 Lindblad.LA96, ZanmarSSWW08 , and is within the bracket set by the gas flow simulations of , namely r_L = (1.2 0.2)a .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1365 as its best eclipse template, citing common Compton-thin and Compton-thick occultations observed in long X-ray observations.

quotes.quote: The model chosen for the simulations is based on the already observed eclipses in our best example, NGC 1365. As briefly summarized in the previous Section, and reported extensively in several publications (Risaliti et al. 1999, 2005, 2007, 2009A, 2009B, Maiolino et al. 2010), we observed several eclipses in this source, both total and partial, and by both Compton-thin and Compton-thick clouds. In particular, eclipses by clouds with N _H 3-5 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 , and with covering factors C _F 50-90 are rather common, and are seen in almost all the observations longer than a few tens of ks.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1365 as strongly barred, with central spiral structure and a main logarithmic spiral emerging from the bar ends with high outer m=2 amplitude.

quotes.quote: NGC 1365: A spiral structure occupies the central parts, while the bar is seen in the range 40-92 \ with an amplitude a_2 up to 73 . The main spiral starts at the ends of the bar and follows a logarithmic spiral with i = 33.1 \ from 99 \ to the edge of the frame around 220 . The amplitude of its m=2 component exceeds unity in the outer parts owing to its strongly peaked, azimuthal shape.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC1365 as a nearby strongly barred spiral with an AGN; its nuclear star-forming region and dust lane host massive embedded cluster complexes.

quotes.quote: Three examples of massive star clusters in this an evolutionary stage have been discovered in the star-forming nuclear region of the strongly barred spiral NGC1365 GA05 Galliano05a . The relatively small distance of NGC1365 (18.6 Mpc, 90 pc per arcsec), the fact that this galaxy has been extensively observed and modelled see for a review on the specific galaxy Lindblad99 , and the fact that the galaxy hosts an active galactic nucleus (AGN) that can feed an adaptive optics (AO) system make these clusters preferred targets for detailed studies. They are located in the central star-forming region, close to, or within, a well-defined dust lane.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 1365's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 1365 is an infrared-luminous low-inclination barred spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 17.9 2.7 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with an AGN and intense circum-nuclear star formation (see lindblad_ngc_1999 for a global review, and alonso-herrero_probing_2012 for a dedicated infrared study of the nuclear region). The AGN has been classified optically either as Sy 1.5 schulz_clues_1999 , Sy 1.8 edmunds_nuclear_1982 or as Sy 1.9/H II trippe_multi-wavelength_2010 . At radio wavelengths, the most prominent feature is the kiloparsec-scale star formation ring ( 8 20 ; PA 30 , sandqvist_radio_1982 ), which contains numerous bright super star clusters kristen_imaging_1997,galliano_mid-infrared_2005 .

sources.summary: The paper finds NH3 emission from NGC 1365 in two velocity components and says the kinetic-temperature fit is well constrained by detected NH3 transitions.

quotes.quote: NGC 1365: The NH _3 emission from this galaxy appears to emanate from two velocity components; at v_ hel 1575 and 1730 km s ^ 1 .
The H _2 CO 1_ 10 1_ 11 absorption spectrum reported by is suggestive of this same two-velocity component structure.
The quality of the kinetic temperature fit for this galaxy is good, well constrained by the NH _3 (1,1) and (2,2) transitions detected towards both velocity components.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 1365 as a Seyfert 2 with significant spectral variability and sub-10-minute flux doubling or halving times in all three observed epochs.

quotes.quote: Among the Seyfert 2 galaxies NGC 1365 has shown flux doubling/halving time less than 10 min in all three epochs it has been observed.
Significant spectral variations are seen in the BL Lacs object Mrk 421, the FSRQ 3C 273, the Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 5548 and the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1365.
In the radio-quiet category, two Seyfert galaxies, namely NGC 5548 and NGC 1365 showed spectral variations with their spectra becoming softer with increasing brightness.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1365 as a type-1 Seyfert with a good soft X-ray/[O III] match, while its diffuse soft X-ray morphology follows the inner spiral pattern rather than simple categories.

quotes.quote: NGC 1365 : This is an example of a soft X-ray emission with a diffuse morphology that does not match neither cone-shaped nor spheroidal categories mentioned in Sect.  sec:xray_morph . The emission better resembles the inner regions of the spiral pattern that shapes the galaxy morphology in the continuum.
This is also the case in the type-1 Seyfert NGC 4151, but not in NGC 1365, both presenting a good soft X-ray/ [ O iii ] match.

sources.summary: The appendix describes NGC 1365 as highly absorbed and rapidly variable, but finds no strong outflow evidence despite earlier reflection and upper-limit studies.

quotes.quote: NGC 1365 is a highly absorbed source, with a rapidly spinning black hole, that has been found to show strong relativistic disk reflection Risaliti13 and extreme variability on timescales of days and weeks, where it changed from being Compton-thick to Compton-thin Risaliti2007 . However, not many UFO studies have been conducted, other than Gofford13 who found an upper limit of < 0.014c. We find no strong evidence for an outflow due to the lack of significant features and alignment (see text for more detail).

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 1365's central ionized gas, describing its strong-emission region, velocity field, and signs of nuclear outflow, rotation, density structure, or inflow.

quotes.quote: The FoV of our observations of this nearby Sy 1 galaxy covers only 300 400 pc at the galaxy, the SER being extended beyond the FoV. An increase in the electronic density is observed towards the SE, in agreement with the measurements by lena2016 who have also obtained GMOS-IFU spectroscopy of this galaxy over a somewhat larger FoV. Over an even larger FoV, a cone-shaped structure has been observed extending by 1.5 kpc to the SE in by Storchi-Bergmann1991 and associated outflows have been observed in previous studies Sharp2010 .

sources.summary: The PHANGS-HST pilot sample describes NGC 1365 as a dusty barred galaxy at 20 Mpc, with very high star formation and the dustiest, highest-SFR role in the sample.

quotes.quote: The galaxies are NGC 1433 (a relatively dust-free, low SFR barred spiral), NGC 3351 (a barred spiral with low dust content and SFR in the outer region, but a high SFR ring with extensive dust in the inner region), NGC 628 (a grand-design spiral with moderate SFR and dust), and NGC 1365 (a dusty barred galaxy with very high SFR). Galaxy distances range from 10 Mpc (NGC 628 and NGC 3351), to 18 Mpc for NGC 1433, and 20 Mpc for NGC 1365 gagandeep21 . The four galaxies are shown in Figure fig:4x2_image in the order they will be discussed in this paper, from the lowest SFR (0.56 M _ /yr), least dusty galaxy (NGC 1433) to the highest SFR (16.90 M _ /yr) dustiest galaxy (NGC 1365).

sources.summary: NGC 1365 is used as the high-mass PHANGS example, with central starburst H-alpha luminosities and enriched regions that trace the spiral pattern across the disk.

quotes.quote: These four galaxies in Figure fig:radial_gradients represent a low stellar mass (IC 5332) and high stellar mass (NGC 1365) galaxy, and systems with a regular spiral pattern and no bar (NGC 0628) or strong bar and widely separated arms (NGC 7496).

In many cases central starbursts are also apparent, covering scales of a few 100 pc and exhibiting high H luminosities (e.g. in NGC 1365), though we note that these measurements could be biased by our ability to deblend neighbouring \ regions given our angular resolution ( 70 pc).
While in some galaxies the enriched regions (in red) appear to strongly trace the spiral pattern (e.g. NGC 1365, NGC 1566, NGC 1672), they can also generally be found throughout the entire disk.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1365 as a local barred spiral whose inner 5 kpc shows asymmetric gas, lopsided star formation, and transient streaming motions reproduced by hydrodynamical simulations.

quotes.quote: Moreover, schinnerer23 study the inner 5 kpc of the local barred spiral galaxy NGC 1365 with James Webb Space Telescope/Mid-Infrared Instrument (JWST/MIRI) imaging alongside ALMA CO(2-1) mapping from PHANGS-ALMA (PHANGS: Physics at High Angular resolution in Nearby GalaxieS ). They find asymmetric gas distributions along the bar lanes and a lop-sided star-formation distribution as well as evidence of streaming motions along the bar lanes. Using hydrodynamical simulations, they are able to replicate these observations, with transient streaming motions that occur within a dynamical time (which in the case of NGC 1365, is 30 Myr with streaming motion timescales as small as 6 Myr).

sources.summary: NGC 1365 is described as a barred spiral outside the Fornax core, with HST Cepheid observations giving a distance modulus of 31.35 mag.

quotes.quote: NGC 1365, a large barred spiral galaxy, is situated well outside the core. The HST has been used to find Cepheids and to derive a distance based on the period-luminosity relation. Madore et al. quote a distance modulus of 31.35 0.07 mag.

gmw_00000168.name: NGC 1386
gmw_00000168.overview: NGC 1386 is an edge-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:1401.1989, arXiv:1804.07311] hosting a heavily obscured, Compton-thick Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1401.1989, arXiv:1811.07935]. The galaxy features a circumnuclear star-forming ring [arXiv:1906.08187, arXiv:2011.04677], a compact nuclear radio jet [arXiv:1905.00994, arXiv:2101.11625], and a parsec-scale gas outflow [arXiv:1706.01370, arXiv:2107.07635].

sources.summary: The paper newly analyzes NGC 1386 with NuSTAR, requiring Fe-complex components and finding a highly covered Compton-thick AGN poorly described by the sphere model.

quotes.quote: Our sample consists of 10 local ( z<0.1 ) CT AGN observed by : NGC 424, NGC 1068, 2MFGC 2280, NGC 1320, NGC 1386, NGC 3079, IC 2560, Mrk 34, NGC 4945 and the Circinus galaxy. With the exception of 2MFGC 2280, these sources were selected to be known CT AGN and to cover a wide range in X-ray luminosity. Particular attention was paid to selecting sources in the low ( 10^ 42 ) and high ( 10^ 44 ) luminosity regimes, thus enveloping the bulk of the local CT AGN population, which is at moderate luminosities, so that our sample could be as representative as possible, despite its small size.
The \ observations of NGC 1386, NGC 3079 and 2MFGC 2280 are presented here for the first time.

For two sources, NGC 1386 and NGC 4945, we find that the best-fit \ reaches the lower limit on this parameter (26 ), which implies that both the sources are highly covered. We also find that the values for these fits deviate significantly from the canonical value of 1.9 (steep at 2.52 for NGC 1386 and flat for NGC 4945 at 1.58). The fact that the deviations from the canonical value occur at the edge of the \ parameter space suggests that the true \ of these sources lies outside of the range of the torus model (i.e. < 26 ). also present a model of a fully covered source (i.e. 0 \ opening angle), which includes variable iron and elemental abundances, known as sphere . We fit this model, with the iron abundance free, in place of the torus model for NGC 1386 and NGC 4945, and present the results in Table table_specpar . This is also done for NGC 3079 since the small opening angle also provides a good fit to that source. An improvement in the fit statistic is found for both NGC 3079 and NGC 4945, where the spherical model produces a significantly better ( > 90 significance) fit than the torus model for both ( ^2 =4 and 136, respectively). The iron abundance has been estimated to be slightly higher, albeit statistically consistent with solar metalicity. The inferred from the sphere model is also consistent with the canonical value for both sources. For NGC 1386, the sphere model produces extreme and iron abundances values and iherefore it is not likely to be a good description of the spectrum.

sources.summary: FCC 179 is described as a Seyfert 2 early-type spiral recent infaller, with quenched outer disc star formation, central spiral structure, circumnuclear star formation, and AGN-linked ionisation.

quotes.quote: FCC 179 (NGC 1386) -- Fig. fig:FCC179map FCC 179 is a Seyfert 2 early-type spiral at R_ proj =0.7^ from the cluster centre on the south-western side. Although it is close in projection to the cluster core and located in the high-density region, FCC 179 is one of the recent infallers of the cluster. This is consistent with the absence of star formation in the outer parts of the disc, probably quenched by ram pressure stripping, while it is still ongoing in the inner regions. This galaxy shows a prominent spiral structure for R 40 arcsec which disappears outwards.

The H map reveals the presence of a circumnuclear ring where most of the central star-formation of FCC179 is found. Elsewhere in the disc other sources of ionisation are likely present, in particular in the north-south direction where the impact of AGN activity would be consistent with the central gas outflows in the same direction Lena2015 . The stellar disc is rotationally supported with v_ max 250 km s ^ 1 at R 90 arcsec. The stellar velocity dispersion is large in the centre ( 150 km s ^ 1 ) but decreases steeply outwards.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1386 as a Seyfert 2 spiral with ionization lobes, a small radio jet, a circumnuclear star-forming ring, and perpendicular shock-like turbulent gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 1386 ( D 16.4 Mpc; 1 '' 80 pc) is a radio-quiet ( P_ 1.4 GHz 4.6 10^ 20 W Hz ^ 1 , Ulvestad:1984aa ) Seyfert 2 spiral galaxy, inclined by 65 ^ with respect to the line of sight ( Lena:2015aa ; see also Fig. fig:maps_n1386_1 a). reported two elongated emission-line structures over 2 '' to the N and S of the nucleus from [ O iii ] and H + [ N ii ] images, likely due to the AGN ionising radiation field illuminating the gas in the disc ( Lena:2015aa ). Elongated radio emission, suggestive of a radio jet, is observed to the S of the nucleus across 1 '' ( 80 pc), and it is also marginally resolved to the N ( Nagar:1999aa , mundell2009 ).

In Figs. fig:maps_n1386_1 and fig:maps_n1386_2 we report our MUSE flux and kinematic maps for NGC 1386, respectively. The red box in Fig. fig:maps_n1386_1 a shows the 5.1 5.1 kpc ^2 FOV of our MUSE maps superimposed on a large-scale image of the entire galaxy. Fig. fig:maps_n1386_1 b displays our MUSE three-colour image. H (red) dominates in a prominent circumnuclear star-forming ring.

The [ O iii ] line velocity width map (Fig. fig:maps_n1386_2 b) instead shows a strong and extended (over 1.5 kpc) enhancement, up to W70 > 600 km/s, almost perpendicular to the ionisation lobes, the ionised outflow, and the putative small-scale radio jet, indicating fast/turbulent motions away from the nucleus in this direction.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1386 as an edge-on Seyfert 2 galaxy, emphasizing Compton-thick obscuration, spiral-arm emission, star formation, nuclear silicate absorption, and torus modeling.

quotes.quote: NGC 1386 is a nearly edge on galaxy with a morphological classification of Sa or S0 depending on the authors Tully1988,Weaver1991,DeVaucouleurs1964,Sandage1978 and a nuclear activity type Sy2 Weaver1991 . X-Ray spectral data suggest that this galaxy is Compton-thick, having a column density of N_ H 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 Maiolino1998,Levenson2006 . Optical studies of NGC 1386 have shown the presence of spiral arms and a decoupling between the kinematical and optical nuclei Weaver1991,StorchiBergmann1996,Rossa2000 which can be interpreted as additional evidence for heavy obscuration of the AGN. The H image of this galaxy shows extended emission in the form of spiral arms (see fig 2 in StorchiBergmann1996 ). Star formation is also reported for this galaxy Weaver1991,StorchiBergmann1996 .

T-ReCS high spatial resolution data reveal silicate absorption at 9.7 in the central tens of parsecs of the Seyfert 2 NGC 1386, and silicate emission in the Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 7213. In the case of NGC 1386 this feature is confined to the inner 20 pc, implying that the silicate might be linked to the putative dusty torus.

sources.summary: NGC 1386 is presented as a nearby Sy2 with resolved narrow-line clouds and a water megamaser; clumpy models reproduce its SED and imply strong torus extinction.

quotes.quote: NGC 1386. NGC 1386 is one of the nearest Sy2 galaxies and therefore extensively studied. Mauder92 resolved its NLR on a 0.3 scale ( 15 pc) using speckle interferometry, detecting individual NLR clouds and claiming a clumpy structure. The detection of a water vapor megamaser in NGC 1386 Braatz97 constrains the inclination angle of the torus to be i 90 , as in the case of Circinus and Mrk 573. The fit with the clumpy models reproduces the observed SED; we find N_0 = 11 3 , = 50 _ 19 ^ 16 , _ V = 95 _ 51 ^ 66 , and q = 1.5 _ 1.0 ^ 0.9 at a 68 confidence level.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1386 as a radiation-dominated AGN with small jets and morphology marked by a large-scale bar and star-forming ring.

quotes.quote: For NGC 1097 and NGC 1386, the radiation dominates. The observations by Mezcua show that these sources have small jets, whereas the other sources have larger jets. Interestingly both NGC 1097 and NGC 1386 have a bar at large scales, but they show no evidence of a bar on small scales. They both also have a ring of star-forming regions. This indicates that the morphology of the galaxies will play a key role for future studies.

sources.summary: The paper excludes NGC 1386 from the main megamaser AGN relation because a required parameter is poorly constrained, then discusses degenerate mytorus fits and low NuSTAR counts.

quotes.quote: In order to test the \ relationship for the megamaser AGN, we require good constraints on , thus we exclude sources where the uncertainty on is >0.25 , which excludes NGC 1386 and NGC 2960 from our sample.

Lastly, we discuss the two sources that we excluded from our analysis, NGC 1386 and NGC 2960. As with NGC 2273, fits with mytorus to NGC 1386 yielded two degenerate solutions, one with a low and one with a high masini16 . With the torus model, found =2.9 0.4 , which is very high for any value of .
We estimate that around 2000 counts at minimum in \ FPMA and FPMB are required for an accurate determination of in CTAGN, where NGC 1386 and NGC 2960 have less than 1000.

sources.summary: The paper reports parsec-scale forbidden-line outflows in the low-luminosity AGN NGC 1386, interpreting symmetric shells as shock-driven by an incipient core jet.

quotes.quote: This paper reports the first direct measurement of powerful mass outflows traced by the forbidden high ionization gas in the low luminosity AGN NGC 1386 at scales of a few parsecs from the central engine. The high angular resolution of the data allows us to directly measure the location, morphology and kinematic of the outflow. This has the form of two symmetrical expanding hot gas shells moving in opposite directions along the line of sight. The co-spatiality of the gas shells with radio emission seen at the same parsec scales and with X-rays indicates that this is a shock-driven outflow presumably induced by an incipient core-jet. With a minimum number of assumptions, we derive a mass outflow rate of 11 M _ yr ^ 1 , comparable to those of powerful AGN.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1386 as an edge-on spiral Sy2 galaxy whose PAH and [SIV] behavior near the nucleus agrees with earlier work, with optical evidence for heavy obscuration.

quotes.quote: NGC 1386 is an edge-on spiral galaxy with a Sy2 nucleus. Ruschel14 found that the 11.3 m PAH feature is more pronounced at 100 pc distance of nucleus. They also found that the [ SIV ] line emission is only detected in the nucleus at distances <100 pc. Our observations are in agreement with these results. Optical studies show evidence of heavy obscuration Weaver91,Storchi96,Rossa00 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1386 as an inclined Sb/spiral Seyfert 2 with nuclear structures, AGN-photoionized emission, a bipolar outflow, and possible perpendicular outflow or rotation.

quotes.quote: NGC 1386 (Seyfert 2 - D_ L 15.6 Mpc - 1" 76 pc), classified as a Sb/ spiral galaxy, is one of nearest Seyfert 2 galaxies and has an inclination of 65^ with respect to the line of sight. lena2015 analysed the gas and stellar kinematics in the inner regions ( 530 680 pc), obtained from IFS GEMINI South telescope observations, revealing the presence of a bright nuclear component, two elongated structures to the north and south of the nucleus, and low-level emission extending over the whole field of view (FOV), probably photoionised by the AGN. Furthermore, lena2015 identified a bipolar outflow aligned with the radiation cone axis.

This feature is resolved in HST observations ferruit2000 into two bright knots that represent the approaching and receding sides of the outflow, respectively. Finally, lena2015 suggested the presence of an outflow and/or rotation in a plane roughly perpendicular to the AGN radiation cone axis, extending 2" 3" on both sides of the nucleus, characterised by an enhanced velocity dispersion and coincident with a faint, bar-like emission structure revealed by HST imaging ferruit2000 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1386 as a Seyfert 2 galaxy with dusty nuclear structure, a warped disk, and a compact southward radio jet.

quotes.quote: NGC 1386 is a Sb/ galaxy with a Seyfert 2 nucleus Malkan1998 . The HST imaging of this galaxy by Ferruit2000 reveals dusty nuclear region running parallel to the major axis. The ellipticity of the disc from the nucleus and outward increases from about 0.25 to 0.5 at 1.5 kpc with signs of warping. The radio observations of the source reveal a compact core with a jet that extends southward Nagar1999b . Although a similar extension was reported in \ and + \ maps Weaver1991, StorchiBergmann1996, Ferruit2000 , no clear correspondence between the optical and radio was inferred Mundell2009 .

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 1386's central ionized gas, describing its strong-emission region, velocity field, and signs of nuclear outflow, rotation, density structure, or inflow.

quotes.quote: This Sy 2 galaxy shows emission peaking at the nucleus and an ionisation axis making a small angle ( 20 ^ ) with the PMA, the latter of which is in approximate agreement with the KMA. Lena2015 ) pointed out the presence of a compact outflow along the ionisation axis that is not resolved by our observations. The kinematics show distorted rotation and the most conspicuous feature is an increase in perpendicularly to the ionisation axis, attributed to gas in equatorial rotation and outflow (as previously discussed in Lena2015 ). This increase in is associated with an increase in the gas density. Based on coronal lines seen in the near infrared spectrum of this galaxy, rodriguez-ardila2017b estimate a mass outflow rate of 11 M_ yr^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper uses the active galaxy NGC 1386 as a MUSE spectral-fitting example, showing that its blended emission-line spectrum needs three constrained kinematic components.

quotes.quote: The plots show the central spectrum of the active galaxy NGC 1386, extracted from the MUSE Bacon2010 integral-field spectroscopic observations presented in Venturi2021 . The emission line spectrum clearly requires at least three distinct kinematic components see also Lena2015 . The definition of the three kinematics components may appear ill-defined, due to the extensive blending of the lines. However, a meaningful decomposition can be obtained with some simple assumptions. Here I required the kinematics (V, ) of all five emission lines to be the same within each of the three kinematic components.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1386 as the prototypical Seyfert-2 case, deriving an AGN-photoionized NLR boundary, an NLR radius, and radial decreases in density and ionization parameter.

quotes.quote: Therefore, we studied the NLR of six Sy1 and six Sy2 galaxies by means of optical longslit spectroscopy with VLT/FORS1 and NTT/EMMI. In the following, we summarize these results, focussing on the nearby Seyfert-2 galaxy NGC 1386 which can be considered as prototypical for the whole sample.

Applying CLOUDY fer98 photoionization models, we show that the observed distinction between H ii like and AGN-like ratios in NGC 1386 represents a true difference in ionization source and cannot be explained by variations of physical parameters such as ionization parameter, electron density or metallicity ben06a . We interpret it as a real border between the NLR, i.e. the central AGN-photoionized region, and surrounding H ii regions. It allows us to determine the NLR size free of stellar contamination, yielding an NLR radius of 6 .

sources.summary: BeppoSAX data show NGC 1386 as a heavily obscured Seyfert with a contaminated PDS band, prominent iron line, low continuum, and cold-reflection Compton-thick evidence.

quotes.quote: NGC 1386 This source was detected also in the 20--100 keV spectral region by the PDS. However, further analysis indicated that most of the flux is due to a nearby Seyfert galaxy (NGC1365) that happens to be located at the edge of the PDS beam. As a consequence, we could not use the PDS data to constrain the X--ray properties of the source. The MECS spectrum is characterized by a prominent iron line and a very low continuum level. The low continuum makes estimates of the EW(Fe K ) very uncertain, since the latter becomes very sensitive to the continuum fit. However, we could set a 90 confidence lower limit of 2 keV to the EW(Fe K ), that strongly supports the Compton thick nature of this source. The Compton, cold reflection model provides the best fit to the high energy ( > 3 keV) data.

sources.summary: The review presents NGC 1386 as a Seyfert 2 with elongated ionized emission, disk rotation dominating most gas, and a compact nuclear outflow in the inner 50 pc.

quotes.quote: Yet another case of a compact nuclear outflow is observed in the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1386 lena14 , where our GMOS-IFU observations covered the inner 500 pc. We found a similar result to that found in NGC 2110: while the gas emission is elongated, extending up to the borders of the FOV, and can thus be attributed to ionization by a collimated nuclear source, the kinematics shows again that most of the gas is in rotation in the galaxy disk. Only within the inner 50 pc there is a region of high velocity dispersion and double components, interpreted as due to a nuclear outflow. We show this in Fig. n1386 , where the cartoon illustrates our preferred explanation for this case, but the general idea should also apply to the other galaxies with compact outflows discussed above.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1386 as a type-2 Seyfert where continuum subtraction reveals [O III] morphology matching the soft X-rays, and the hard X-ray emission is also diffuse and similar.

quotes.quote: NGC 1386 : We present in Fig.  fig:comp_ngc1386 the complete set of images for this object. We see that the continuum emission is completely different from the [ O iii ] and the morphology changes drastically, resembling that of the soft X-rays in the [ O iii ] case. The hard X-ray emission is also diffuse and matches the soft one. We can see reproduced three main emission regions in both X-ray images.

sources.summary: NGC 1386 is treated as a strong 22 GHz maser nondetection at 183 GHz, with the paper noting its complicated, non-rotating maser geometry.

quotes.quote: The other two nondetections -- NGC 1068 and NGC 1386 -- host strong ( 1 Jy peak flux density) 22 GHz maser systems Claussen_1984,Braatz_1996 , and we would have strongly detected 183 GHz emission from these systems even if it were an order of magnitude fainter than the corresponding 22 GHz emission. It is thus perhaps surprising that these systems are not detected by our 183 GHz observations, because the nondetection implies that any 183 GHz emission that might be present is substantially more than an order of magnitude fainter than the corresponding 22 GHz emission. It is also interesting to note that the 22 GHz maser systems in both NGC 1068 and NGC 1386 exhibit more complicated dynamics and geometries than many of the more ordered edge-on disk systems in our survey. The 22 GHz maser system in NGC 1068 is distributed in an approximately linear structure on the sky Greenhill_1996 , but it exhibits sub-Keplerian rotation velocities and may trace the upper layers of a geometrically thick torus or disk structure whose surface is irradiated by the jet e.g., Gallimore_2001 ; the 22 GHz maser system in NGC 1386 is not consistent with a rotating structure at all Braatz_1997 .

sources.summary: The paper interprets NGC 1386's central HST structure as a near-edge-on warped rotating spiral disk, deriving enclosed masses and finding ionized gas dynamically minor.

quotes.quote: The central 6 sized structure seen on HST H and [ O iii ] images of NGC 1386 appears to be the inner part of a near-edge-on warped rotating spiral disk that is traced in H within a diameter of 17 along p.a. 23 . This interpretation is based on observed kinematic continuity and a typical S-shaped dust lane crossing the kinematical center. The central velocity gradient yields a Keplerian mass estimate of 10 ^8 M _ inside R=100 pc and 5 10 ^9 M _ inside 0.8 kpc. The total mass of the ionized gas of 10^ 5-6 M _ is small compared to the dynamical mass of the spiral disk.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes X-ray spectra of NGC 1386, describing its best-fit components, Fe K emission or flux behavior, and comparison with previous modeling.

quotes.quote: NGC 1386 As noted in the main text, the spectral parameters between the XMM-Newton and Chandra observations were consistent, except for a lower multiplicative factor for the Chandra observation, indicating extended emission contaminated the XMM-Newton observation. Though the Chandra data were grouped by a minimum of 5 counts per bin, the XMM-Newton spectra were grouped by a minimum of 15 counts, so we used ^2 statistics in this analysis. To derive the 2 - 10 keV flux, we fitted spectra from both observatories simultaneously to better constrain the Chandra spectrum, using a double absorbed power law with a thermal component and a Gaussian feature at 6.4 keV to accommodate the Fe K emission, yet we report the Chandra flux only.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 1386's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 1386 is an edge-on spiral galaxy in the Fornax cluster at a distance of D= 16.5 0.8 Mpc (NED redshift independent median) with a Sy 2 nucleus with broad lines in the near-infrared reunanen_near-infrared_2002 that belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample. The nuclear radio source is slightly extended to the south (PA 170 ; nagar_radio_1999 ), and is surrounded by a biconical north-south extended NLR, which has been extensively studied (major axis 6 480 pc; PA 5 ; e.g., weaver_kinematics_1991,ferruit_hubble_2000,schmitt_hubble_2003,bennert_size_2006-1 ). In addition, a nuclear H _2 O mega-maser was detected braatz_survey_1996,braatz_survey_1997 .

sources.summary: NGC 1386 is used as a high-resolution nuclear-spectra comparison where PAH subtraction of IRS data is judged to approximate the nuclear emission well.

quotes.quote: For instance, in rus14 there is no PAH emission detected using high resolution nuclear spectra from T-ReCs when compared with IRS observations of NGC 7213 and NGC 1386.

To illustrate the effect of the starburst subtraction method, we show in Figure high the high resolution data from Michelle and T-ReCs of the galaxies Mrk 3, NGC 1386 and NGC 7213, compared to the IRS spectra.
However, in the cases of NGC 1386 and NGC 7213, we believe that the PAH subtraction approach represents a good approximation of the nuclear emission.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1386 as an SB0 Compton-thick AGN with a water megamaser, jet, extended narrow lines, and hard X-ray point-source complications.

quotes.quote: NGC 1386 NGC 1386 is an SB0 type galaxy at RA=03:36:46.18 (54.192 ), Dec=-35:59:57.87 (-35.999 ), and z=0.00290 (D _ lum 12 Mpc), with a water-megamaser ( Sch03mas ) and jet ( Nag99 ).

Previous observations of NGC 1386 have shown extended O 3 narrow lines (e.g., Sch03 ) aligned along the north-south direction and coincident with our defined ``cone'' region as shown in Figure fig:img:1386 .
NGC 1386 Compared to MKN 573, NGC 1386 presents a more challenging picture due to additional point sources primarily aligned with the cone region, even at the harder X-rays (Figure fig:grid:1386 ).

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 1386 is extended in soft and medium X-rays, shows O VIII emission, and has a strict upper limit on scattered soft continuum.

quotes.quote: , NGC 1068, NGC 1386, and NGC 3393 are significantly extended in the soft and medium bands, even on small ( 1 ) scales, while their hard bands are unresolved.
We do not characteristically find emission from fully-ionized species, although we measure O 8 in NGC 1386 and it may appear blended in the spectrum of .
We find strict upper limits of 10 in NGC 1068 and NGC 1386, 20 in NGC 3393 and NGC 7212, and 26 in the nucleus of .

gmw_00000169.name: NGC 1433
gmw_00000169.overview: NGC 1433 is a nearby barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1811.00984, arXiv:2301.03689] located in the Dorado group [arXiv:1309.7486, arXiv:2009.04090] that has been classified as a Seyfert 2 [arXiv:1309.7486, arXiv:1310.2770] or LINER [arXiv:1310.2770]. It features multiple resonant rings [arXiv:1309.7486, arXiv:1103.3796], a low star formation rate [arXiv:2301.03689], and a mild molecular outflow [arXiv:1811.00984, arXiv:1408.1591].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1433 as a strongly barred ringed spiral whose ALMA data show nested nuclear rings, a mild molecular outflow, filamentary CO emission, and no molecular torus.

quotes.quote: NGC 1433 is a strongly barred spiral galaxy, nicknamed the “Lord of Rings” because of the presence of clear nuclear, inner, and outer rings at the bar resonances Buta1996 . Our previous cycle 0 ALMA observations in Band 7, with 0 5 resolution, have revealed that inside the nuclear ring at 400 pc, there is also an inner nuclear ring at 200 pc, corresponding to the inner ILR Combes2013 . These observations revealed also a mild molecular outflow along the minor axis, of 7 M _ /yr, the smallest molecular outflow ever observed in the Local Universe. It is possible that the outflow in a previous more powerful phase has destroyed a potential torus. The tentative continuum point source near the center is not confirmed, and there is no evidence of any molecular torus.

The only small velocity gradient in the center corresponds to the outflow along the minor axis. This is supported by the coincidence of the optical and near-infrared emission in the center, showing no extinction Smajic2014 . The CO(3-2) emission has a very filamentary structure at small scale, as can be seen in Figure fig:1365-1433 .

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA CO observations of NGC 1433, finding a barred Seyfert with nuclear rings, a gaseous spiral, central continuum, and a molecular outflow.

quotes.quote: The galaxy \ is a strongly barred spiral with three resonant rings: one at the ultra-harmonic resonance near corotation, and the others at the outer and inner Lindblad resonances (OLR and ILR). A nuclear bar of 400 pc radius is embedded in the large-scale primary bar. The CO map, which covers the whole nuclear region (nuclear bar and ring), reveals a nuclear gaseous spiral structure, inside the nuclear ring encircling the nuclear stellar bar. This gaseous spiral is well correlated with the dusty spiral seen in Hubble Space Telescope images.

NGC 1433 The system \ is a nearby active barred galaxy; it is a member of the Dorado group which includes 26 galaxies (Kilborn 2005). It was classified as Seyfert 2 by Veron-Cetty Veron (1986), because of its strong nuclear emission lines and its high [ NII ] /H ratio.
Near the nucleus, there is an intense high-velocity CO emission feature redshifted to 200 km/s (if located in the plane), with a blue-shifted counterpart, at 2 \ (100 pc) from the centre.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1433 as a nearby galaxy with an elongated inner ring, plumes, resonant structures, and H I morphology closely following its optical form.

quotes.quote: The velocity field of the nearby galaxy NGC 1433 (Buta et al. 2001) beautifully demonstrates this characteristic alignment. The large inner ring in this galaxy has q_ o = 0.63, much more elongated than average.

A particularly interesting case with possible resonant features is the galaxy NGC 1433, which has an exceptionally well-defined and unusually highly elongated inner ring (dVA). In addition, the galaxy has two secondary spiral arcs off the leading sides of the bar called `plumes' by Buta (1984). Treuthardt et al. (2008) were able to simulate both the inner ring and the plumes, as well as the outer R _1^ pseudoring using a near-IR image to define the potential.

sources.summary: HST TRGB photometry places NGC 1433 among Dorado galaxies at about 14.2 Mpc; the paper argues a prior 9.1 Mpc TRGB value confused AGB stars with red giants.

quotes.quote: Galazutdinova 1 Special Astrophysical Observatory, Nizhnij Arkhyz, Karachai-Cherkessian Republic, Russia 369167; ntik@sao.ru Based on the archival images of the Hubble Space Telescope, stellar photometry of the brightest galaxies of the Dorado group: NGC 1433, NGC 1533, NGC 1566 and NGC 1672 was carried out. Red giants were found on the obtained CM diagrams and distances to the galaxies were measured using the TRGB method. The obtained values: 14.2 1.2 , 15.1 0.9 , 14.9 1.0 and 15.9 0.9 Mpc, show that all the named galaxies are located approximately at the same distances and form a scattered group with an average distance D = 15.0 Mpc.

For the galaxy NGC 1433, Sabbi_etal_2018 obtained the value D = 9.1 Mpc, i.e. it should be a relatively close galaxy. Indeed, if we take the stars of this galaxy outside the bright core and carry out their usual selection, then a jump in the luminosity function can be seen at I = 25 . ^ m 7 , which corresponds to the distance D = 9.1 Mpc. However, this jump refers to AGB stars, and the actual TRGB jump is weaker by one magnitude (Fig. fig8 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1433 as a prototypical ringed barred galaxy with circumnuclear and outer pseudo-rings, prior bar spectroscopy, and a young central disc relative to its old bulge population.

quotes.quote: NGC 1433: A prototypical ringed barred galaxy (Treuthardt et al. 2008 Treut08 ), with a morphological type (R')SB()ab. It has a circumnuclear ring with a diameter about 0.1 times the size of the bar (at 19 " , Crocker et al. 1996 Crock96 ; Hameed Devereuz 1999 HD99 ; Buta et al. 2001; Comeron et al. \ 2007), and an outer pseudo-ring about twice the diameter of the bar. Spectra along the bar were presented by P erez et al. (2009).

The luminosity-weighted age reflects the presence of dynamically cold structures, where there has been (or where there is currently) recent star formation, manifesting itself as central discs (e.g. in NGC 1433) or rings (NGC 1365 and NGC 1433 at 7 and 10 arcsec respectively, and NGC 1672 at 9 arcsec). The mass-weighted values are much flatter, indicating that the bulge regions are dominated in mass by an old stellar population (10-15 Gyr), although the central discs in NGC 1672 and NGC 1433 show mass-weighted ages slightly younger ( 6 Gyr).

sources.summary: The review cites NGC 1433 as a barred spiral where ALMA detected a compact molecular minor-axis outflow while molecular gas continues feeding the AGN through bar torques.

quotes.quote: Recently the ALMA observations at 25 pc resolution of the CO(3-2) line in the barred spiral NGC 1433 has revealed a molecular ouflow on the minor axis (Combes et al. 2013, see Fig 4.). About 7 of the molecular mass detected belongs to the outflow, with a velocity 100 km/s, uncertain because of ill-known inclination. This is the smallest flow detected up to now. The kinetic luminosity is 2.3 10 ^ 40 erg/s, and the flow momentum is > 10 L _ AGN /. The molecular gas is still fueling the AGN, as shown by the computed gravity torques of the bar (Smajic et al. 2014).

sources.summary: The paper reports NIR and ALMA observations of NGC 1433, using NIR data to locate its AGN and interpreting its central velocity gradient as possible molecular outflow or nuclear disk.

quotes.quote: In NGC 1433, we can determine the real position of the AGN only from the NIR data. A steep velocity gradient in the center (in ALMA/CO and SINFONI/warm H _2 ) is observed that could be interpreted as molecular outflow. However, from NIR emission line diagnostics, we conclude that the nuclear star formation rate is low compared to other low-luminosity AGN. This means that the outflow would be essentially triggered by the AGN alone (Combes \ ). An alternative explanation is a nuclear disk (Smaji \ ).

sources.summary: NGC 1433 is one of the PHANGS-HST/LEGUS comparison galaxies; its revised distance affects interpretation, and it shows the weakest survey agreement in cluster classifications.

quotes.quote: This only significantly impacts NGC 1433, which has a much larger revised distance (18.63 versus 8.3 Mpc). Data for NGC 1433, NGC 1566, and NGC 3351 originate completely from WFC3/UVIS , whereas NGC 628 observations also include imaging taken with ACS/WFC (as indicated with the ^ symbol). For NGC 1433, ResNet is more frequently in agreement with human classification.

Agreement is weakest for NGC 1433, with 38 for ML and 40 for human classification. This also makes sense in terms of NGC 1433, which Anand2021 have shown is actually at a distance of 18.6 Mpc rather than the 8.3 Mpc we assumed (in this paper only) for consistency with LEGUS.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 1433 among barred galaxies with elevated bar ages, flags its LumW metallicity gradient as a likely mosaic artifact, and measures age/metallicity profiles along bar pseudo-slits.

quotes.quote: The impact of bars in the orbital configuration of stars of different ages, together with the suppression of star formation along bars e.g., Neumann2019, Fraser2020 , caused by the bar dynamics i.e., velocity shocks and shear, see, e.g., Zurita2004, DiazGarcia2020 , could be the origin of the consistently higher LumW log Ages in bars, relative to disks, at a fixed galactocentric distance, across most of the radial range covered by both environments (except for the bar ends), in several of our sample galaxies (e.g., NGC 1365, NGC 1433, NGC 1512, NGC 3351).
The LumW metallicity gradient of NGC 1433 shows a strange discontinuous pattern that is likely an artifact due to subtle systematic differences between the MUSE pointings of the mosaic.

Figure fig:NGC1433_bar_mask shows the position of the pseudo-slits overplotted on the stellar mass surface density map of NGC 1433. We have measured radial gradients along each of these two pseudo-slits (excluding centers). The radial profiles (and the best-fit gradients, measured using an OLS fitting routine) of MassW age and metallicity measured along each one of these pseudo-slits for NGC 1433 are shown in Fig.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 1433 as an early ALMA barred-spiral case where a second molecular ring, current lack of AGN fueling, gas accumulation, and small molecular outflow were inferred.

quotes.quote: One of the first barred spiral observed was NGC 1433 (cf Fig. fig5 ). While only a star forming ring was observed at ILR with HST, a second ring was detected in the molecular gas with ALMA, corresponding to the second ILR. The computation of the torques have shown that the AGN is not presently fueled, but positive torques bring the gas from the center to the second ring. Negative torques outside this second ring contribute to accumulate gas there Smajic2014 . On the minor axis, an outflow has been detected, with small velocity.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1433 more complex than NGC 1350, with two major phase-shift crossings and distinct bar and oval/spiral pattern speeds.

quotes.quote: The case of NGC 1433 (Figure olrrings , lower frames) is more complicated.
