This galaxy shows two major P/N crossings, one surrounding the ends of the main bar and inner ring, and the other passing between the outer pseudoring and the extended oval connected with the inner ring. Interestingly, the radii of these two inferred CRs are very similar to the radii of the IUHR and CR derived by Treuthardt et al. (2008) from a single \ numerical simulation of NGC 1433. Our conclusion is that the bar and oval/spiral of NGC 1433 do not have the same .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1433 as a low-luminosity Seyfert with feedback, positive torques stalling gas at 100 pc, and a small molecular outflow at 10 pc.

quotes.quote: Feedback can occur also in low-luminosity AGN, like in the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1433 (see Figure fig6 ). The torques inside the nuclear ring are positive, and the gas is stalled there, at 100pc scale. Inside, at 10pc scale, a molecular outflow is detected on the minor axis, corresponding to 7 of the mass. This is the smallest outflow rate detected in a nearby galaxy.

sources.summary: The review cites NGC 1433 as a barred spiral where ALMA observations found a 200 pc resonant ring, positive inner torques that pile up gas, and a weak AGN-driven molecular outflow.

quotes.quote: In the barred spiral NGC 1433, a second resonant ring has been discovered at 200 pc, at the ILR of the nuclear bar (Combes et al. 2013). But the torques are positive inside, and the gas is piling up at this second ring (Smajic et al. 2014). There is also a molecular outflow on the minor axis, due to the AGN feedback. This is a weak outflow of 100 km/s in velocity, and dragging 7 of the molecular gas mass.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 1433 has a strong bar, inner ring, outer pseudo-ring arms, ordered HI rotation, and an HI ring following the outer arms.

quotes.quote: NGC 1433: The centre left panels in Fig. fig:mosaic4 show the DSS optical composite image and the 1 velocity map for this galaxy. From the optical map, we observe a bright and well-defined bar. At the ends of the bar is an inner ring and two outer spiral arms wind around to form an outer pseudo-ring. The 1 gas in this galaxy is observed to follow ordered rotation and a ring of 1 gas observed to trace the outer spiral arms.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1433 as an observed nested-bar comparison, noting similar bar shapes and showing its infrared, CO, velocity, and residual-velocity maps from PHANGS-ALMA data.

quotes.quote: Based on the recent PHANGS-ALMA data, we find 6 S2B candidates (NGC 1068, NGC 1087, NGC 1317, NGC 1433, NGC 4304, and NGC 4321) according to the classifications in erwin_04 and buta_etal_15 . NGC 1433 probably has the most similar bar shapes (i.e. stellar surface density contours) compared to our S2BSMU model. Although our model S2BSMU is not designed to match any particular galaxy, we still compare it with NGC 1433 in Figure fig:ngc1433 . The top row shows the S ^4 G 3.6 m image, CO(2-1) integrated intensity map, CO intensity-weighted mean line-of-slight (LOS) velocity map, and the residual velocity map obtained by subtracting the circular motion (i.e. the spider diagram) from the V_ los map of NGC 1433.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1433 as a barred Seyfert/LINER system with a smooth circumnuclear condensation, low central extinction, and no hot-spot evidence for massive stellar clusters.

quotes.quote: The cases of NGC 4388 and NGC 1433 can only be discussed on the basis of imaging results. The central condensation of NGC 1433 is large (we have determined a diameter of 31 1.7 kpc) and extremely smooth, much flatter than the point spread function: we therefore consider unlikely a major contribution from the LINER /Seyfert nucleus, which should manifest itself as a point source.

There is some evidence that the extinction in the central regions of NGC 1433 is much lower than in other galaxies: whereas available Balmer decrement measures indicate H absorptions of the order of 2--3 in other nuclei, the decrement given by Diaz for NGC 1433 indicates A(H ) 0.9 . We also notice that NGC 1433 is the only one among strongly barred spirals which has an amorphous circumnuclear region, with no hot spot that would indicate the presence of massive stellar clusters.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1433 as a pseudobulge galaxy, citing The bulge has a prominent nuclear spiral; n_b=0.8 , and the bulges is actively forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =0.3 mag.

quotes.quote: NGC 1433 PSBR2../SBb(s) Pseudobulge. The bulge has a prominent nuclear spiral. The decomposition yields n_b=0.8 , and the bulges is actively forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =0.3 mag.

sources.summary: The paper reports larger-radius gas knots in NGC 1433, treats its diagnostic classification as an exception, and includes it among LINERs with positive gradients.

quotes.quote: For seven galaxies (NGC 1433, NGC 1530, NGC 1832, NGC 2665, NGC 2935, NGC 3081 and NGC 4314) we have also found knots of gas emission at larger radii, corresponding to one or several crowded \ regions.
Studying the diagnostic diagrams for individual galaxies, we confirm the classification carried out by Veron-Cetty Veron (2006) with the exception of NGC 5101 and NGC 1433.
For LINERs, we find a positive gradient for three galaxies (NGC 1433, NGC 4314 and NGC 4394) and none of them has a negative gradient.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 1433's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 1433 is a low-inclination barred spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 8.3 2.3 Mpc tully_extragalactic_2009 with a possibly active nucleus, which has been classified as a Sy 2.0 veron-cetty_study_1986 or a LINER storchi-bergmann_ultraviolet_1995 . This AGN has barely been studied and in particular no X-ray works are available. At radio wavelengths only a low-angular resolution measurement was performed, which detected a weak non-thermal nuclear compact source (diameter 4 ; PA 90 ; harnett_radio_1987 ).

sources.summary: The PHANGS-HST pilot sample describes NGC 1433 as a relatively dust-free, low-star-formation barred spiral at 18 Mpc and the least dusty, lowest-SFR galaxy in the four-object sequence.

quotes.quote: The galaxies are NGC 1433 (a relatively dust-free, low SFR barred spiral), NGC 3351 (a barred spiral with low dust content and SFR in the outer region, but a high SFR ring with extensive dust in the inner region), NGC 628 (a grand-design spiral with moderate SFR and dust), and NGC 1365 (a dusty barred galaxy with very high SFR). Galaxy distances range from 10 Mpc (NGC 628 and NGC 3351), to 18 Mpc for NGC 1433, and 20 Mpc for NGC 1365 gagandeep21 . The four galaxies are shown in Figure fig:4x2_image in the order they will be discussed in this paper, from the lowest SFR (0.56 M _ /yr), least dusty galaxy (NGC 1433) to the highest SFR (16.90 M _ /yr) dustiest galaxy (NGC 1365).

sources.summary: NGC 1433 is described as weakly radio emitting in its central star-forming ring and ring-like spiral arms, with no bar star formation and no detected polarization.

quotes.quote: NGC 1433 (Fig. 17) has weak radio emission from the central region which hosts an irregular star-forming ring of 5 \ or 0.3 kpc radius (Maoz et al. \ ). Star formation has not been detected in the bar but is noticeable in the ring-like spiral arms, in particular at the ends of the bar (see H image by Crocker et al. \ ) where weak radio emission is seen. No polarization has been detected.

gmw_00000170.name: NGC 1553
gmw_00000170.overview: NGC 1553 is a lenticular galaxy [arXiv:2009.14731] interacting with NGC 1549 [arXiv:1310.2770] and featuring a wide system of outer shells [arXiv:2206.01578]. It hosts a young, LINER-type active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2009.14731] and exhibits resonance rings [arXiv:2206.01578] with clumpy inner H-alpha emission [arXiv:2206.01578].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1553 as a ringed S0 with centrally concentrated emission, LINER and AGN-like nuclear evidence, a young nucleus, and NUV/FUV ring structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 1553 The galaxy is classified S0 with ring (Type=-2.3 0.6). Comeron2014 classified it SA(rl,nr'l)0 ^+ , i.e. two rings are present in this lenticular galaxy. 75 of the \ flux is measured within an ellipse of 60 \ of semi-major axis, containing the core and the ring visible in Figure NGC1553 , i.e. in a relatively small central region with respect to the size of the ellipse reported in Table HA-measures . No \ regions are revealed on the galaxy (see, however, Dorado-compact ).

The nuclei of the two shell galaxies, NGC 1549 and NGC 1553 are LINERs see e.g. Annibali2010, Rampazzo2013 . Optical line strength indices found that NGC 1553 has a young nucleus with a luminosity weighted age of 4. 0.7 Gyr Annibali2007 .

In the X-ray domain the emission of the nucleus of NGC 1553 is AGN-like Flohic2006 . GALEX observations detected both NGC 1553 Marino2011a and NGC 1549 in NUV while a ring is barely visible in FUV in NGC 1553.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC1553 as the clearest LMXB-sample outlier, with significant X-ray growth-curve deviations and outer-region luminosity-function differences from the average early-type pattern.

quotes.quote: Individual galaxies The L_X growth curves of two galaxies, NGC1553 and NGC1291, show significant deviations from the distribution of the NIR light (Fig. fig:gcurves_lx ). The luminosity distributions for these two galaxies also exhibit largest deviations from the average XLF, although consistent with the latter within statistical uncertainties, according to the Kolmogorov-Smirnov test (Table tab:ks_test ). These two galaxies might indeed present an example of the population age dependent variations in the shape of the luminosity distribution. In both cases the deviations from the average LMXB XLF occur at the high luminosity end as might be expected for such variations.

NGC1553 Comparison of N_X and L_X growth curves for this S0 galaxy indicates possible variations of the luminosity distribution with the distance from the nucleus. This is confirmed by Fig. fig:xlf_ngc1553 , presenting luminosity functions for the inner and outer regions. In the inner ring, the luminosity distribution of the sources is consistent with that of other early type galaxies (solid smooth line marked ``LMXB''). In the outer ring, on the contrary, it deviates significantly from the average (long-dashed line), both in the slope of the distribution and in the luminosity of the brightest sources.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1553 as an S0 galaxy with known shells and rings, FUV evidence for a ring-lens bump, clumpy inner H-alpha, and disk-structured FUV emission.

quotes.quote: The HyperLEDA classification for this galaxy is S0 with a ring. Malin1983 discovered a wide system of shells around this galaxy (see their Figure 2). The colour-composite image shown in Figure NGC1553 does not show such features, neither in FUV.CaF2, as expected, nor in the red continuum image due to the short exposure times. The recent classification by Comeron2014 suggests the presence of resonance rings in this lenticular SA(rl,nr'l)0 ^+ .

Although these features are derived by IR data, a faint bump appears in the FUV.CaF2 luminosity profile of Figure NGC1553 between 30-40 , corresponding to the ring-lens (rl) above. \ emission has been detected by Fabry-Perot (FP) high resolution observations of the NGC 1553 inner regions Rampazzo2007 . The FP \ monochromatic map in the inner 30 \ results quite clumpy (see Figure 8 in Ram2020).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1553's Chandra point-source results to estimate discrete-source X-ray flux, noting a low-temperature component and cautioning that low luminosity makes population fluctuations important.

quotes.quote: Observations of NGC 4697 ( SarazinIrBreg00 ) and NGC 1553 ( Blantonetal01 ) reveal considerable numbers of point sources with hard spectra. Blanton show that, at least in the case of NGC 1553, the emission from resolved point sources is best fit using a model which includes a low temperature component. From the Blanton results we estimate that the total flux from discrete sources, excluding the AGN, is 8.58 10 ^ 13 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 in the 0.3-10 keV band. Using our distance and L _B for NGC 1553 gives = 29.44.

Assuming a 20 conversion factor produces = 29.52, very similar to our other estimates. However, both NGC 1553 and NGC 4697 have low X--ray luminosities, and relying purely on the lowest luminosity galaxies for measurements of may be unwise. irwinsarazin98 note that small fluctuations in the discrete source populations in these objects could cause a large degree of scatter in L _X , as their total X-ray luminosities are small.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1553 as a shell-system case with central H emission evidence and a potentially recent starburst comparable to pair galaxies with shells.

quotes.quote: Among the pair galaxies of our sample, only a few possess shell structures, namely RR 225a, RR 225b and RR 278a to which the system NGC 1549+NGC 1553 (the difference among the two systemic velocities is V 31 km s ^ 1 : Longhetti \ 1998b) in the Dorado group can be added.

Another interesting case is NGC 1553 in the center of which Trinchieri \ (1997), using narrow band imaging, have found H emission. Longhetti \ (1998a,b) did not detect H emission probably because it was too faint or it was filling the absorption feature. If so, also the value of the H+K(CaII) line may be contaminated by H in emission. It follows from all this that burst in NGC 1553 could be as recent as in pair galaxies with shells.

sources.summary: The paper estimates NGC 1553's Bondi rate and finds its jet-dominated model roughly matches X-ray luminosity but fails to fit the X-ray spectral slope.

quotes.quote: NGC 1553 The Bondi accretion rate was estimated by pelle05 . Even though the JD model roughly accounts for the estimated L_X , it fails to fit the slope of the X-ray spectrum even with a small value of p . We take this as evidence that this source is unlikely to be JD. The estimated m _ out is consistent with the lower limit on m _ Bondi obtained by pelle05 .

sources.summary: The paper finds three central-kiloparsec point sources in NGC 1553, with the central bright source spectrally consistent with an AGN and dominating the hard central X-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: NGC 1553 (L2/T2, S0) has three point sources in its central kiloparsec. The brightest one is exactly at the center of the galaxy and its spectrum is well fitted either by a two-temperature plasma model ( kT_ 1 =0.14^ +0.04 _ 0.09 keV, kT_ 2 =18.0^ +70 _ 10.4 keV) or a power-law ( =1.2^ +0.2 _ 0.1 ). The preference of a the two-temperature over a one-temperature plasma model and a power-law model is justified by the F-test. Both of the above models give a luminosity of L_ 0.5-10 keV = 1.5 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 1553's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 1553 is an inclined early-type spiral galaxy interacting with NGC 1549 (11.5 away) at a distance of D= 16.4 5.1 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with an active nucleus, classified as a LINER or transition object flohic_central_2006 . The presence of an AGN in NGC 1553 is supported by the detection of a compact source in radio harnett_radio_1987 and in X-rays blanton_diffuse_2001,flohic_central_2006 . After its detection in , NGC 1553 was observed with temi_cold_2004 and with /IRS and MIPS.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC1553 as strongly underpredicted in the basic model, notes a spiral feature through its center, and says it remains underpredicted by about two orders.

quotes.quote: Sources that were under-predicted 4-7 orders of magnitude are: NGC1291, NGC1316, NGC1553, NGC4261, NGC4438, NGC4594, NGC4697 and IC4296.

NGC1291 is an early type spiral, NGC1316 is disturbed early type elliptical, NGC 1553 has a spiral feature passing threw a center, NGC 4261 core is surrounded by a disk of gas and dust, NGC4438 has an outflow bubbles which also may indicate angular momentum to dominate the accretion flow, NGC4594 is a spiral galaxy, the last two sourced NGC 4697 and IC4296 are elliptical radio galaxies.
Two sources: NGC1553 and NGC 4261 are still under-predicted of about 2 orders of magnitude.
